description: Thonar Silver-Blood is a Nord warrior who lives in The Treasury House in Markarth. Along with his politically involved older brother, Thongvor, Thonar runs the Silver-Blood Family, the most influential family in the Reach.
canonical_lines: The Imperial agent? That's right. I knew. How many dogs is the Empire going to send after me? This is my business. My city. You Empire lovers should learn to stay out of it. Now get out. | They're my puppets. I have their 'king' rotting in Cidhna Mine. He was supposed to keep them under control. | My prison. The source of half the silver in Skyrim. The most secure prison in Tamriel. No one escapes. I thought keeping Madanach down there would keep him under control. | My eyes inside Cidhna Mine tell me that Madanach is dead. You've done a great service to the Silver-Blood family. I've had the Jarl officially pardon you, and taken care of a few other loose ends. | Yes, my Jarl. | My king. Madanach. When the uprising fell at the hands of the Nords, they threw him in the mines. I don't know how, but he lives. I get his messages, and I hand out his orders without question.

Name: Thongvor Silver-Blood
description: Thongvor Silver-Blood is a Nord warrior and a member of The Treasury House owned by the Silver-Blood clan; he is often seen roaming around Understone Keep in Markarth. He is the older brother of Thonar Silver-Blood.
canonical_lines: Typical Imperial lies. First you take away Talos, now you're keeping us from seeing our honored dead? You and the Jarl will answer for any desecration of my ancestors' bodies. | You there. You another Imperial puppet? Answer me. | There's a war on and you don't know what I'm talking about? The Empire is destroying everything Skyrim stands for. Jarl Igmund can refuse to see me all he wants. The Silver-Blood family will be heard. | Jarl can refuse to see me all he likes. He can't ignore us forever. | I am the Jarl of Markarth, now. The Silver-Blood family is finally where it should have always been. | It's fine. Much better than the rickety tools I had in the Legion.

Name: Yngvar the Singer
description: Yngvar the Singer is a Nord barbarian who lives in Markarth. He claims to be a brute, but is in fact a bard, educated at the Bards College in Solitude.
canonical_lines: Reburrus, I need you to draft a letter to Ulfric requesting more men. | Thongvor, do you really think that's wise? I mean, King Ulfric is such a busy man, and there are so many areas of Skyrim to protect. | Whose side are you on, Reburrus? I'm not worried about Skyrim, I'm worried about the Reach. Specifically, the part of the Reach we're standing on. | Thongvor's made me his housecarl. Remember, me killing you is legal. | Used to have run-ins with the guards, now they have run-ins with me. | I want word on our campaign against the Imperials.

Name: Aicantar
description: Aicantar is a High Elf conjurer, and is the nephew of Calcelmo.
canonical_lines: You heard me, captain- our work must not be disturbed. Kill any intruders on sight. Call for backup if you need it. But let no one through. | Accidents like this just don't happen. Someone is trying to sabotage my uncle's research. | I...all right. If there is a thief, he won't leave this tower alive. But shouldn't we inform Master Calcelmo? | I'll deal with my uncle. Just...go! Scour this place from top to bottom! | I'm Calcelmo's nephew. I help him around the laboratory. | Don't let my uncle go on about his theories. You'll be here all night.

Name: Anton Virane
description: Anton Virane is a Breton citizen living in Markarth. He was born in Daggerfall in High Rock, which is where he learned to cook.
canonical_lines: The Jarl gives me two incompetent assistants. Worthless. | Yes, yes, for the hundredth time, I am a Breton. I was born in High Rock. And then I came here. I am not a Reachman! | The... the Gourmet? Never! I don't know what led you here, but nothing will betray my trust. I'll take the secret of the Gourmet's identity to my grave. | Okay. All right. Wonderful. You're welcome! I'll just be on my way, then... | Before you say anything, no, I am not from the Reach. | I have meals to cook for the entire castle and almost no help.

Name: Calcelmo
description: Calcelmo, Scholar of Markarth, is a High Elf conjurer of great renown who dedicates himself to researching the Dwemer. He and his nephew Aicantar study the Dwemer ruins beneath Markarth.
canonical_lines: Correct. It's a private place where I can catalog my research. Occasionally, the Jarl will come in and have questions, but that's thankfully rare. | Ha! I'll see to it the Jarl hears of your abuse of a court scholar. Shame on you. | Preposterous! That research represents years of personal toil in some of the most dangerous Dwemer ruins in Skyrim! You must be mad to think I'd allow anyone to see it before it's completed. | What are you doing here? The excavation site is closed. I don't need any more workers or guards. | Nchuand-Zel? The ruins underneath Markarth? The wealth of artifacts that I've based two human lifetimes of research on? | I told you I'm not hiring any more guards. Why do you people always bother me when I'm trying to finish my research?

Name: Faleen
description: Faleen is a Redguard warrior and the housecarl of Jarl Igmund of Markarth. She can be found in Understone Keep.
canonical_lines: Igmund is alive, and I'll keep it that way, Jarl or not. | You lay one finger on Igmund, and I'll end you Stormcloak. | This is why I say we take the fight to them. Gather the Legion and drive those blasted beasts out of the Reach for good. | Very well. I'll send the request for additional Legionaries to protect trade to the other Imperial holds. Hopefully, Solitude can spare more men. | Yes, my Jarl. | Forgive me for worrying about the future of Markarth. The lifeblood of the city is gold, Igmund, and it doesn't stop beating because there's a war on.

Name: Ghorza gra-Bagol
description: Ghorza gra-Bagol is an Orc blacksmith in Markarth. Her brother is Moth gro-Bagol.
canonical_lines: That's right. An Orc stronghold. Kept to ourselves. Lived as our ancestors would have wanted. Wasn't enough for me, though. I left. Didn't want to end up as the third wife of some lowly chieftain. | It's an honorable craft. I'll show you what I can. | Let me know if you need something forged. | Is that what you call it? In the Legion we would have called it 'useless.' Look how brittle the metal is! | Your job is to do what I say. And I say you have many nails and fittings to make before you even touch a blade. Now get to work. | I'm sorry, Ghorza. I just... flinch, and uh....

Name: Igmund
description: Igmund is a Nord barbarian who rules the city of Markarth as jarl with assistance from his steward and uncle, Raerek. He is supportive of the Imperial Legion in the current civil war.
canonical_lines: I came here hoping to learn that more Imperial reinforcements were on their way to Skyrim, but so far I've been disappointed. | I admit, I've never felt entirely at ease in the Thalmor embassy. | Hmm. I don't want to get involved in anything beneath my dignity as Jarl. | Ulfric Stormcloak? The rightful High King? That's bordering on treason, man! Stand up. Stand up, I say! | They live here too, my Jarl. That's what I'm telling you. | This is why I say we take the fight to them. Gather the Legion and drive those blasted beasts out of the Reach for good.

Name: Legate Emmanuel Admand
description: Legate Emmanuel Admand is a Breton soldier who leads the Legion forces in the Reach. He can be found in Understone Keep if the Imperials control the Reach, or at Reach Imperial Camp if the Stormcloaks control the Reach.

Name: Moth gro-Bagol
description: Moth gro-Bagol is an Orc and the personal blacksmith for Jarl Igmund of Markarth. He is the brother of the other Orc blacksmith in Markarth, Ghorza gra-Bagol.
canonical_lines: The Empire has always been good to the Orcs, but this war of theirs is none of my concern. | I served in the Legion with Thongvor Silver-Blood. He's a good man and a tough soldier. | It's fine. Much better than the rickety tools I had in the Legion. | Sometimes I miss the Legion, back when the Empire stood up for Skyrim. | Good. I'd prefer using water to cool my metal, but it's what the Jarl wants. Here. Some of my best armor. Wear it well. | I'm the Jarl's personal blacksmith, but that doesn't mean I can't sell you a few pieces for a fair price.

Name: Ondolemar
description: Ondolemar is a Thalmor Justiciar living in Understone Keep in Markarth. Along with two Thalmor Soldiers, Ondolemar is stationed in the City of Stone to observe and prevent any kind of Talos worship, in accordance to the White-Gold Concordat.
canonical_lines: If you see any signs of Talos worship, it's your obligation to report them to me. | We're the ruling body of the Aldmeri Dominion. Saviors of Mer. Victors of the Great War. The Empire exists because we allow it to exist, and I'm here to make sure the Jarl of Markarth remembers that. | Ah, there we have it. This will be all I need to have Ogmund taken care of. I would like to personally thank you on behalf of myself and the Thalmor for your assistance. Here, for your work. | It's a necessary part of my job to mix with the upper classes of Skyrim, such as they are. | You have the honor of addressing a member of the Thalmor. Bask in it. | The Thalmor know how to deal with Talos worshippers.

Name: Raerek
description: Raerek is an elderly citizen who lives in Markarth. He is the steward of Jarl Igmund, who rules from Understone Keep.
canonical_lines: I don't think we have anything to discuss, Stormcloak. | They live here too, my Jarl. That's what I'm telling you. | This is why I say we take the fight to them. Gather the Legion and drive those blasted beasts out of the Reach for good. | Very well. I'll send the request for additional Legionaries to protect trade to the other Imperial holds. Hopefully, Solitude can spare more men. | Yes, my Jarl. | Umm... No, my Jarl. I'm afraid there isn't.

Name: Rondach
description: Rondach is a Breton citizen of Markarth and works in Understone Keep's kitchens. He seems to be terrified of his superior, Anton Virane.
canonical_lines: I have to work, or Anton will rip my head off. | Only reason Jarl keeps Anton on is because he cooks up a storm. | Voada, you spilled laundry water in the soup pot again! Do I need to cut off your fingers to teach you a lesson? | You touch her, and I swear it'll be your fingers that go missing, you stupid Breton! | Anton sure looks like he's from the Reach, but don't tell him that. | Now now, brother. is just being his usual gloomy self, he didn't mean it, did you ? You're just being silly again.

Name: Tacitus Sallustius
description: Tacitus Sallustius, an Imperial blacksmith, is the hapless apprentice of Ghorza gra-Bagol at the public smithy in Markarth.
canonical_lines: I'm not very good for anything more than smithing nails right now. | One time, I almost cut a finger off sharpening a blade. I hate blood. | Is that what you call it? In the Legion we would have called it 'useless.' Look how brittle the metal is! | Your job is to do what I say. And I say you have many nails and fittings to make before you even touch a blade. Now get to work. | I'm sorry, Ghorza. I just... flinch, and uh.... | I'm Ghorza's assistant. I help her with the forge.

Name: Thalmor
canonical_lines: I am a Thalmor Justiciar on important business that you are interfering with. | You got me. I believe in Talos. | Who are the Thalmor? | We are special envoys of the Aldmeri Dominion, the rightful rulers of Tamriel. If you'd like I can show you why. | What are you doing in Skyrim? | We're making sure your Emperor wasn't lying to his elven masters when he agreed the Empire would give up false gods and foolish beliefs. Perhaps there's something you wish to confess?

Name: Voada
description: Voada is a Breton citizen who works in the Understone Keep kitchen along with her brother Rondach and bossy master chef Anton Virane. She also handles the dogs that live near the kitchen area.
canonical_lines: It's been a hard day cleaning. Good, honest work I say. | Voada, you spilled laundry water in the soup pot again! Do I need to cut off your fingers to teach you a lesson? | You touch her, and I swear it'll be your fingers that go missing, you stupid Breton! | Voada, you spilled laundry water in the soup pot again! | Don't mind the dogs, they get anxious when they haven't had dinner. | You should talk to Anton if you need anything. He's the master chef.

Name: Argis the Bulwark
description: Argis the Bulwark is a Nord warrior living and working at Vlindrel Hall in Markarth. Once you have purchased the home and attained the rank of thane of the Reach by completing quests issued by the jarl, Argis is appointed to be your personal housecarl.

Name: Cairine
description: Cairine is a Breton citizen living in The Warrens, the hub for all the poor and sick of Markarth. She used to work at the Smelter in Markarth, but is now too sick to do so.
canonical_lines: Something's happened, I can't quite remember... I don't feel... right... I'm going to lay down... for awhile... | Great gift of blood you've given me. Blood that boils in my veins. Gives me strength. You can use my coffin whenever you like. | I keep an eye on their comings and goings. No one suspects poor Cairine. Poor, poor Cairine. Ha! | You don't want to be here. No one wants to. | Don't know anyone here doesn't have Rattles or Ataxia. | A pathetic creature in her present condition, but the gift will embolden her. And her lowly state suits her to being our eyes and ears in Markarth.

Name: Cosnach
description: Cosnach is a drunken Breton warrior who is usually found at the bar in the Silver-Blood Inn. He is a porter for the Arnleif and Sons Trading Company, which can be found directly across the path from the Silver-Blood Inn.
canonical_lines: I'm drunk, and it's been a while since I had a good fight. A hundred septims says I can take ya, bare-handed. | Hey, I may have had a few, but I can still count. You don't have a hundred septims on you. | Wait, I can still fight. No, no I can't. | Oh yeah. Pretty little thing. Had a room here I think. Ask the innkeeper. | "Check the shipments" Lisbet says. "There are no shipments" I says. | There's no shipments coming in, so I do nothing. Just drink.

Name: Degaine
description: Degaine is a Breton beggar living a cumbersome existence in The Warrens, the place where Markarth hides the poor and sick. He recruits you to steal a valuable statue from Markarth's Temple of Dibella, having previously been caught in his attempts to steal the statue himself.
canonical_lines: Fine. Not like it will matter much. Jarl's not going to arrest Nepos. Just tell me when you want me to go. | Fine. I'll confess. Just let me know when you want to head over to the Jarl. | Alms. Give me some meat, you fat Nord! | Picking on me again, you backbiting Nord? Tired of those prissy nobles turning down your rotten slabs of venison? | What is there to know? It's in the temple, you bring it out. You bring it to me, I pay you. Now get moving. | Give it to me quickly, before they see us together. You've riled up the priestesses, and the guards don't take kindly to that. Here's your share. Nicely done.

Name: Dryston
description: Dryston is a Breton barbarian working for the Forsworn. During the related quest he tries to beat you up after you nose around too much.
canonical_lines: I was sent by Nepos the Nose. The old man hands out the orders. He told me to make sure you didn't get in the way. That's all I know, I swear! | Nepos is in charge. That's all I know. | You've been digging around where you don't belong. It's time you learned a lesson. | You again. Guess you need another lesson, huh? | Someone who doesn't like you asking questions. | You'll listen to this.

Name: Eltrys
description: Eltrys is a Breton citizen who lives in Markarth. He was recently married to Rhiada and the couple awaits their first baby.
canonical_lines: Huh. Not sure. I don't worship Talos, myself. I think I heard someone mention it was underneath the Temple of Dibella, in the big crag in the center of the city. | I'm so sorry. I hope the Eight give you more peace in the future. | The Forsworn? Strange. Well, I hope the Eight give you more peace in the future, for what it's worth. | You don't have to say sorry to me. I just hope the Eight bring us more peace in the future. | Oh, I think you dropped this. Some kind of note. Looks important. | No. I was just getting some fresh air. Had one too many pints of mead at the Silver-Blood Inn.

Name: Garvey
description: Garvey, a Breton scout, is one of the poor and sick of Markarth. He resides in The Warrens, and is an expert level trainer in Sneak.
canonical_lines: Warrens ain't for you. Trust me. | Oh yes. I know everyone who sleeps in the Warrens. Kind of the one who passes the keys around. I guess someone else will be taking his room, now. | No. It's not. Trust me. | No, I don't think so. | And I wasn't stuttering. No key. | The Warrens isn't a place for your type. What do you want?

Name: Hathrasil
description: Hathrasil is a Breton citizen who lives in Markarth. He spends most of the day working at the silver smelter alongside Omluag.
canonical_lines: First war, and now dragons. Everywhere they go, the Nords bring nothing but ruin. | Divines, I'm tired. This is too much work. | I know what I'm doing. Stop telling me. | Does it look like I can afford losing a day's wage? That damn Orc isn't getting the best of me. | I could smelt metal in half the time as you weak-willed pink-skins. Do I have to do everything? | No Mulush. I'll work harder.

Name: Omluag
description: Omluag is a Breton citizen who lives in The Warrens and works at the Smelter in Markarth. He shares his room in the Warrens with Hathrasil, a co-worker at the Smelter.
canonical_lines: Can't talk. Mulush will beat me if I stop working. | I spill just a bit of metal, and Mulush takes it out of my hide. | Can't you see I'm working? Beat it. | I saw you. With the Forsworn. By the old gods, that was something. | I'll be damned. Someone who actually escaped Cidhna Mine. You're the talk of the Warrens. | We've lost a lot of good people to that damn crusade. Drive out the Nords, they say. Well, the Nord ain't driven out.

Name: Alva
description: Alva is a Nord vampire sent by Movarth Piquine to Morthal to transform some of the townsfolk into vampires while using the rest as cattle.
canonical_lines: Well, aren't you the handsome one. You and I should spend some time together. | Well, aren't you the pretty one. I bet all the men chase after you. | I know you for what you are. This town's blood is ours. Leave it! Now we must return to our roles lest the sheep suspect the wolves. | I feel wonderful, just like every night. | Oh Hroggar, you know I'm always hungry. Don't worry, I'll eat when I need to. | I'm bored, Benor. Nobody wants to have any fun with me.

Name: Hroggar
description: Hroggar is a Nord lumberjack currently enthralled by Alva, a vampire. Hroggar lives in Alva's house and will turn hostile should you enter while he's there.
canonical_lines: I... I'm not one for talk. Try to keep to myself. | I just try and do my job, and not to think about what's happened. Try to find what happiness I can. | That's true, but I'm in a new house now. A better house. | Yeah, that was a shame. But now Alva takes care of me. And I take care of her. | Alva's been good to me ever since... well, since the fire. | I have Alva to take care of now. I don't need Helgi anymore.

Name: Agni
description: Agni is a Nord child living in Morthal. She was taken in by the resident wizard Falion after the loss of both her parents, and is currently training to be his apprentice.
canonical_lines: Do you like magic? Falion says I'm good at learning. I like learning about magic. | My mother and father died years ago. Falion takes care of me now. | I asked Falion if someday I could learn from the mages in Winterhold, but he doesn't like that idea. | When I grow up, will I go to the College in Winterhold? | No, I don't think that will be necessary. | But I'm learning about magic, and that's what they do there. Shouldn't I go?

Name: Falion
description: Falion is a Redguard conjurer and resident wizard of Morthal. He is a master level trainer in Conjuration and also sells magical equipment and spells at his house.
canonical_lines: If you stand before me to accuse me of sacrificing children, or eating the hearts of the dead, you may save your breath. Beyond that I seek only to be left alone to pursue my research. | I keep to myself. I offer spells and scrolls to those who need them, and wisdom in Conjuration magic for those who wish to learn. Beyond that I seek only to be left alone to pursue my research. | If you are here to purchase, I have many spells available. Do come in. | Morthal is a troubled place, and it is my duty to see it rest in peace. | The changes in Morthal are of little consequence to me. So long as the laws are upheld, I do not care who does the upholding. | As anyone will tell you, Morthal can be a dangerous place. My... talents... are useful here. I help maintain order, even if it goes unnoticed.

Name: Benor
description: Benor is a Nord warrior living in Morthal. He claims to be an amazing fighter and is ready to prove it for a bet of 100 gold.
canonical_lines: You're a real fighter. I like that. You ever need my steel by your side, you just ask. | I figure the guards could always use some help, so I keep my eyes open. | Been hoping they'd make me a guard, but not yet. Still, I'll help them out if I can. | The Jarl does little to protect us these days. | You ask me, we'd be better off without that wizard in town. | I'm the best warrior in Morthal, and that's no boast.

Name: Morthal Guard
description: Morthal Guards are guards who act as the military and police force of Morthal. These guards serve under Jarl Idgrod.

Name: Aslfur
description: Aslfur is a Nord ranger who serves as steward to Idgrod Ravencrone, the Jarl of Hjaalmarch. He is also her husband, and the father of Idgrod the Younger and Joric.
canonical_lines: If you've business with the Jarl, I'd ask that you speak to me first. | I do what I can to see that Idgrod's time isn't wasted, and that she only has to deal with important matters. | Too many bother my wife with their petty problems, and then don't even understand the advice she gives them. | The only thing that matters to me is keeping my wife safe. I won't let anyone harm her. | Ah, you saw that, did you? Life in Morthal has been troubled lately. The people are uncertain, restless. They merely look to the Jarl for leadership. | There's news of rebellion against the Empire. Strange noises have been heard in the marshes at night. And then, the tragedy with Hroggar's home. The men simply seek wisdom. Everything will be fine.

Name: Gorm
description: Gorm is a Nord warrior and the housecarl for Idgrod Ravencrone, the Jarl of Hjaalmarch.
canonical_lines: I'm just here to protect the Jarl, not to chat. | You will not disrespect Jarl Idgrod while in this Hall. Do we have an understanding? | Morthal has enough problems without you bothering me. | I'm under a lot of pressure. If you don't leave me alone, I won't be held responsible. | This wasn't what I wanted. Not at all. | All that ever mattered was what was best for Morthal. Leaving it in Ulfric's hands... It's not right.

Name: Idgrod Ravencrone
description: Idgrod Ravencrone is an elderly Nord mystic. She is the Jarl of Hjaalmarch and runs the administration with her husband, Aslfur, who serves as her steward.
canonical_lines: In Morthal, there is little to rule. But there is much to teach. And so, I am Jarl. | I stand where I have always stood. Here in Morthal. Now is not the time to fight amongst ourselves. I fear dark days approach and all of Skyrim's strength will be needed. | I will be Jarl someday, mother? | There are words spoken, and words unspoken. Beware these Thalmor, for they are adept in both languages. For this reason, I avoid them when I can. | So, life has brought you to Morthal, and to me. What purpose this serves, we will no doubt see. Welcome. | I speak, and others hear. It is my hope that instead they listen.

Name: Idgrod the Younger
description: Idgrod the Younger is a Nord sorceress living in Highmoon Hall in Morthal. She takes care of her brother, Joric, with the help of potions given to her by Danica Pure-Spring.
canonical_lines: Someday I'll be Jarl. Mother reminds me often. I hope I can help the people of Morthal the way she has. | I will be Jarl someday, mother? | I don't... I haven't mother's gift, not quite, but I've seen things. | For now, looking after Joric is my job. | It's difficult sometimes... To know whether what I see is real or not. | I keep hoping this is another vision, that it's not real. But it is, isn't it? We really have lost our home.

Name: Joric
description: Joric is a Nord child living in Morthal with his sister Idgrod the Younger. His parents are Idgrod Ravencrone and Aslfur.
canonical_lines: You're different, aren't you? Not like anyone else. Not in Morthal, or Skyrim. | Gorm thinks I'm mad. He doesn't say it, but I know he thinks it. Is he right? | I get lost sometimes. I'm not sure where I go, but I'm not... I'm not here. | It's Falion. He...He did it. I don't know what it was, but he did it. | My mother sees things. She doesn't know why, but she does. Only sometimes, she doesn't see me at all. | I don't understand. Why can't we go home?

Name: Legate Taurinus Duilis
description: Legate Taurinus Duilis is an Imperial soldier, and Legate of the Imperial Legion, stationed in Morthal. During the day he is found in the war room of Highmoon Hall and at night he sleeps inside the Morthal Guardhouse.

Name: Valdimar
description: Valdimar is a Nord sorcerer found in Highmoon Hall in Morthal. He becomes your personal housecarl when you have earned the title of Thane in Hjaalmarch.
canonical_lines: I'm yours to command, Thane. I'll wait here until you need me. | Do you need something from me, Thane? Or can I carry something for you? | I'll return to Highmoon Hall in Morthal until you need me again, Thane. | I'll return to Windstad Manor and remain on guard, my Thane. | What do you need, Thane? | Is it time to move out?

Name: Jorgen
description: Jorgen is a Nord lumberjack who runs the mill in Morthal. He doesn't care about either side in the war, he just wants to get his work done.
canonical_lines: Unless it's about the mill, I don't have time to talk. Too much to do. | The mill is my responsibility, and that's what I care about. That's all. Understand? | I just want to be left alone to do my job, and to enjoy what little free time I have when I have it. | We've gone from a Jarl lost in her own head to a Jarl that cares for nothing but herself. Lot of good that does us. | The world's going mad, and our Jarl does what? She hides inside with her 'visions'. We need a leader, not some mystic! | The world's going mad, and our Jarl doesn't care. She's only in it for herself. If she's the best Ulfric has to offer, we might've been better off with the Imperials.

Name: Lami
description: Lami is a Nord apothecary and the proprietor of the Thaumaturgist's Hut, an alchemy shop in Morthal. She is also the common trainer in alchemy.
canonical_lines: If you need any alchemy supplies, I'll do my best to provide them. Assuming I haven't used them all up. | Jorgen and I both work hard, all the time. Morthal is important to both of us. | I don't get many customers, but that leaves me time to try things out. Experiment, I guess. | Oh, do come in! I hope I have what you need. | I... I'm not sure. I think perhaps, but I don't know what it is. | All right, can you describe it to me?

Name: Jonna
description: Jonna is the Redguard owner of the Moorside Inn in Morthal. She moved to Morthal with her brother Falion, and now runs the unsuccessful inn.
canonical_lines: Now there's a mystery. She ran away when the war started. Her husband, Thonnir, was devastated. No note or nothin'. I'm pretty sure she up and joined the Stormcloaks. | Welcome to the Moorside. If you need anythin', I'll be 'round. Good to have a customer. | Finally, someone comes in. Kick off your boots, stay awhile. Let me know if there's anything I can help you with. I got nothing but time these days. | There ain't much to offer, but if you wanna place dry to spend the night, I'll rent you a room. | Runnin' an inn weren't my plan. But Falion decided to move here, so I joined him. | Falion had his work to keep him busy, and I needed somethin' to do. So, here I am.

Name: Lurbuk
description: Lurbuk is an Orc bard found at the Moorside Inn in Morthal. The local innkeeper, Jonna, claims that the only reason she hasn't booted him out is because there aren't any other customers for him to bother.
canonical_lines: The fair Jonna does me a great courtesy by allowing me to entertain the guests of her noble establishment. | I, Lurbuk the Great, will win the hearts of the people of Morthal one day, mark my words! | I'm a bard, trained at the Bards College in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying. | Jonna, do you think the townspeople are warming to my serenades? | No. They ain't. And they aren't gonna. If you weren't payin' for your room, I'd have thrown you out a long time ago. | Oh, I shouldn't be surprised if they come 'round. And if you're never seen or heard from .

Name: Thonnir
description: Thonnir is a Nord lumberjack living in Morthal with his son, Virkmund. From 8am he can be found working at Jorgen's mill, and from 11pm he can be found sleeping in his house located not far from the Thaumaturgist's Hut.
canonical_lines: This damned war has cost us... me... everything. | No! You're wrong. You must be wrong. Laelette may have met her fate out in the marsh. I refuse to believe Alva had anything to do with this. There is no way you can prove it to the Jarl. | I thought Laelette left to join the war. I hadn't heard from her since she left. | I'm just a poor man trying to make a living. I'd leave Morthal were it in my power. | My boy's to grow up without a mother? What life is that? It's not right, I tell you. | What's the Jarl going to do about it?

Name: Virkmund
description: Virkmund is a Nord child who lives in Morthal with his father, Thonnir. He's waiting for his mother to return home someday, even though his father tells him not to.
canonical_lines: Have you seen my mother? I'm still waiting for her to come home. | Papa says that I shouldn't keep waiting. Why not? Mama has to come home some time. | Who are the Stormcloaks, and why would mama want to spend time with them instead of me?

Name: Bralsa Drel
description: Bralsa Drel is a Dark Elf miner who lives in Raven Rock. She has fallen on hard times since Raven Rock Mine closed and has spent all of her fortune made in the mine on drinking, to the point where she was kicked out of the Retching Netch.
canonical_lines: I lost my house, my family, my life... and then I lost my mind. How could it have gotten any worse? | Don't pity me. And don't look at me like that. I know what I am. | I could swing a pickaxe better than any man in the mines. Made thousands of coins. Now it's all gone. | The voices in my head are gone now thanks to you. Now I can finally get some rest. | Look at me, I'm a different person. And I have you to thank for it. | Don't waste your life like I did.

Name: Rirns Llervu
description: Rirns Llervu is a Dark Elf living in Raven Rock who works at the local mine. Before the Earth Stone is cleansed, he will be seen working there mindlessly.
canonical_lines: Bralsa's been nice enough to show me how to swing a pickaxe. | Anything you can offer, please. I'm starving. | I'm asking myself why Miraak was making us build these shrines... and I'm afraid the answer will be far worse than any of us can imagine. | I feel, different somehow. More myself. | That fetcher... making us build his shrines. If I could only get my hands on him... | Because of you, my life has meaning again. Thank you!

Name: Dreyla Alor
description: Dreyla Alor is a Dark Elf farmer living in Raven Rock, although she hates Solstheim, as her time is spent trying to eke out a living at her shop. She wants to return to Morrowind, but doesn't want to leave her widowed father, Fethis.
canonical_lines: He's twice your age and only wants to marry into our family to use my contacts at the East Empire Company. No, I won't allow it. | Miserable... just miserable. I hate this place. | If you're looking to purchase something, speak to my father, Fethis. | I can't believe that I was building that shrine at night... I never knew. | I know you risked a lot freeing me from Miraak's control. Thank you! | What if Miraak decides to make us do something else next... like kill someone we love. I can't bear the thought!

Name: Fethis Alor
description: Fethis Alor is a Dark Elf farmer living in his house at Raven Rock. He is a general goods merchant who will sell various types of items, and requests that you locate East Empire pendants for him.
canonical_lines: Bah, you're just like the rest of the folks around here. Mark my words... the East Empire Company will return to Raven Rock, and when they do, I'll be at the top of their list. | He's twice your age and only wants to marry into our family to use my contacts at the East Empire Company. No, I won't allow it. | Milore, I've finally gotten that letter back from the East Empire Company. | Mining gear? No. However, I used to sell East Empire Pendants until I ran out of them. Popular little item. | Yeah, so. What can I get you? Got everything from potions to pitchforks. | It's busier now that things are starting to look up. I guess I might stay a bit longer after all.

Name: Captain Veleth
description: Captain Modyn Veleth is a Dark Elf warrior living in Raven Rock. He is Captain of the Redoran Guard in Raven Rock.
canonical_lines: Not sure. I was told it's an old Imperial farm that was wiped out by the eruption of the Red Mountain. Not certain who lived there... it was already a pile of rubble when I arrived in Raven Rock. | See that they stay that way, or else I'll send you back to Skyrim in a crate. You understand me? | The security of both Raven Rock and Councilor Morvayn is my primary concern. | Some consider my methods harsh, but playing the coddling nursemaid has never won a battle. | Don't let my posting here fool you. I was once in command of over a hundred troops... my luck simply changed when our homeland was destroyed. | With the ash spawn threat taken care of, I can give my men the rest they deserve. Well done, friend.

Name: Redoran Guard
description: Redoran Guards are Dunmer soldiers in the service of House Redoran, serving in Raven Rock under Captain Veleth.
canonical_lines: Why you'd leave your homeland behind to venture here is beyond me, but suit yourself, Nord. | I can't believe Raven Rock Mine is open again. Finally, something worth defending. | I hate to break it to you, but there isn't a scrap of ebony left in the mines. You might want to just move on. | You can go where you please, but I'd stay inside the Bulwark if I were you. | Last time I rode Gjalund's ship, I was heaving my guts for a week. Dunmer weren't meant to ride on water. | Glover Mallory is a wonder with the hammer and anvil. He mended my armor like an old pro.

Name: Aphia Velothi
description: Aphia Velothi is a Dark Elf priestess living in Raven Rock. She offers common-level training in Restoration.
canonical_lines: I'm sure he's down there somewhere, buried under a mountain of rubble like the East Empire Company claims. My greatest fear is that my stubborn mule of a husband will end up suffering the same fate. | Far from it. In fact, I'm no longer welcome there. | Don't be put off by my husband's mood... he enjoys having visitors. | Allow me to apologize for Crescius's behavior, I'm afraid he hasn't been himself lately. | Those East Empire people should be ashamed of themselves for abandoning Raven Rock. | I don't know what you did in those mines, but it put a smile on Crescius's face. I've never seen him so happy.

Name: Crescius Caerellius
description: Crescius Caerellius is an elderly Imperial miner living in Raven Rock. He is almost eighty years old, and has spent many years on Solstheim looking for a new source of ebony.
canonical_lines: For all I know, I might be the last Imperial left on Solstheim. | A secret the East Empire Company swept under the rug two centuries ago. It killed my great-grandfather, and left Raven Rock with a worthless and tainted mine. | East Empire Company called it 'a terrible accident,' claiming that he was lost in a rockfall, but I know better. | My wife and I were cleaning out our home, and we came across some of my great-grandfather's things locked in an old chest. I found an unsent letter he'd written to the East Empire Company, and a key. | I'm telling you... there's something big down there, something that the East Empire Company wanted to hide from everyone. | I do. I want to know what happened to Gratian, and what the East Empire Company is keeping from all of us.

Name: Glover Mallory
description: Glover Mallory, a Breton thief, is a blacksmith in Raven Rock. He is the brother of the prominent Thieves Guild member, Delvin Mallory, and a member of the guild himself.
canonical_lines: Steel, dwarven, ebony... if it's broken, I can mend it. | A lot of folks around here look to strangers for help. I hope you won't disappoint them. | I've heard of stealing things from people, but never thought you could steal someone's mind. | Stealing a man's mind... it just isn't right. Thanks for saving me from that madman. | I feel, different somehow. More myself. | Bring your weapons and armor to me. I'll fix it up right.

Name: Garyn Ienth
description: Garyn Ienth is a Dark Elf farmer who lives in Raven Rock on his farm with his wife Milore. According to his wife, Councilor Morvayn says he supplies most of the town's food.
canonical_lines: My wife and I are doing the best we can to keep Raven Rock fed. | If you ever need apothecary supplies, talk to my wife Milore. | My wife's quite the alchemist. Thanks to her, my ash yams are growing quicker than ever before. | Welcome to Raven Rock, traveler. Could I interest you in any fine ash-grown foods? | If I could just get my hands on Miraak for what he did to us... | I'd been waking every morning with my hands scrapped and raw and now I know why. I appreciate what you've done for me.

Name: Milore Ienth
description: Milore Ienth is a Dark Elf apothecary located in Raven Rock, living on her farm with her husband Garyn. She provides expert level training in Alchemy.
canonical_lines: Milore, I've finally gotten that letter back from the East Empire Company. | If you're looking for any exotic ingredients, just ask. I might have what you need. | I don't know who sent you here, but you've given us something to build on here. I hope someday we can repay you for your kindness. | Just thinking that some madman was inside my mind. Gives me shivers just thinking about it. | Remember, I have plenty of potions and ingredients if you're ever in the market. | I don't think so, though it sounds like a name I've heard before...

Name: Adril Arano
description: Adril Arano is the Dark Elf who greets you when you arrive at Raven Rock for the first time. As Second Councilor of Raven Rock, he is the dedicated servant of Councilor Morvayn, House Redoran's representative and ruler of Solstheim.
canonical_lines: Look, the East Empire Company didn't give me a choice. They've raised their prices again, and there's nothing I can do about it. | I don't recognize you, so I'll assume this is your first visit to Raven Rock, outlander. State your intentions. | Miraak... I... I'm not sure that I do. | That's the kind of attitude that's going to land you in prison, but I'll let it go for now. | Just remember, Raven Rock is sovereign territory of House Redoran. This is Morrowind, not Skyrim. While you're here you will be expected to abide by our laws. | I've sworn to keep Councilor Morvayn safe, and I intend to take that oath to the grave if need be.

Name: Cindiri Arano
description: Cindiri Arano is a Dark Elf who lives in Raven Rock. She is the wife of Adril, the Second Councilor of Raven Rock.
canonical_lines: A few months ago, I had sent an important folio to the First Edition Bookshop in the Imperial City to be rebound. They sent it back to me recently, but it never arrived. | Why does that sound so familiar? There's no one here by that name, and yet I swear I've heard it. | I... I don't know. It almost feels like a dream. Something about a temple on the island... | You look able enough to venture outside of town. Perhaps you could help me recover something precious that was lost? | Mephala bless you! You found it! Well, you've certainly proven your worth around here to me. Please, accept this gift as a token of my appreciation! | Offer to help and then change your mind because I mentioned the reavers? Hmph, some sellsword you are.

Name: Lleril Morvayn
description: Lleril Morvayn is the House Redoran Councilor of Raven Rock and ruler of Solstheim. His actual influence doesn't spread far beyond Raven Rock without the resources of the ebony mine.
canonical_lines: Whether you're simply visiting or staying here, I think you'll find our citizens will welcome you with open arms. | On behalf of House Redoran and as Councilor of Raven Rock, I bid you welcome. | Your contributions have earned you citizenship here, friend. I'll always consider you one of us. | I feel I owe you my life for thwarting the House Hlaalu plot to have me assassinated. My deepest thanks. | Yes, I imagine it would to someone not of our people. If you're interested in pursuing the subject, help yourself to any of the historical volumes in my library. | No, Councilor. I'm afraid we're going to fall short once again.

Name: Evul Seloth
description: Evul Seloth is a Dark Elf miner who lives in Raven Rock Mine after it is reopened. He wears miner's clothes and boots, and he carries a steel dagger, a pickaxe, and a selection of common items and gold.
canonical_lines: I've been out of the business so long, I almost forgot how to swing a pickaxe. | Good to know that the mine's safe again. I heard a lot of strange things happened here. | I haven't set foot in these mines for ages. It's strange being back after so long. | Thanks to House Redoran getting these mines reopened, I can provide for my family again. | It's good to get back to mining after all these years. | This mine's amazing! Have you ever seen so much ebony in your life?

Name: Gratian Caerellius
description: Gratian Caerellius is the skeleton of an Imperial that can be found dead in Raven Rock Mine, carrying a small amount of gold and his journal.

Name: Meden Maren
description: Meden Maren is a Dark Elf miner who lives in Raven Rock Mine after you have reopened it.
canonical_lines: I've been out of the business so long, I almost forgot how to swing a pickaxe. | Good to know that the mine's safe again. I heard a lot of strange things happened here. | I haven't set foot in these mines for ages. It's strange being back after so long. | Thanks to House Redoran getting these mines reopened, I can provide for my family again. | It's good to get back to mining after all these years. | This mine's amazing! Have you ever seen so much ebony in your life?

Name: Naris Mavani
description: Naris Mavani is a Dark Elf miner who lives in Raven Rock Mine. He appears after you reopen the mine.
canonical_lines: I've been out of the business so long, I almost forgot how to swing a pickaxe. | Good to know that the mine's safe again. I heard a lot of strange things happened here. | I haven't set foot in these mines for ages. It's strange being back after so long. | Thanks to House Redoran getting these mines reopened, I can provide for my family again. | It's good to get back to mining after all these years. | This mine's amazing! Have you ever seen so much ebony in your life?

Name: Tolenos Omoren
description: Tolenos Omoren is a Dark Elf miner who lives in Raven Rock Mine. He appears after you reopen the mine.
canonical_lines: I've been out of the business so long, I almost forgot how to swing a pickaxe. | Good to know that the mine's safe again. I heard a lot of strange things happened here. | I haven't set foot in these mines for ages. It's strange being back after so long. | Thanks to House Redoran getting these mines reopened, I can provide for my family again. | It's good to get back to mining after all these years. | This mine's amazing! Have you ever seen so much ebony in your life?

Name: Drovas Relvi
description: Drovas Relvi is a Dark Elf farmer who works as a cook at the Retching Netch in Raven Rock. He works for Geldis Sadri.
canonical_lines: Geldis may be a tough boss, but I can put up with it. Keeps a roof over my head. | I don't mind working at the Retching Netch, but I was hoping for a bit more when I moved to Solstheim. Ah well | Drovas, I thought I told you I needed the jazbay crushed for my Emberbrand Wine. Where is it? | Damn it. I wish you would have told me sooner. | Drovas! I need you to clean the rooms again. I went through there, and there's ash everywhere. | Sorry, boss. The ash comes in faster than I can clean it up.

Name: Geldis Sadri
description: Geldis Sadri is a Dark Elf pawnbroker who owns and operates The Retching Netch in Raven Rock. Among other things, he sells Sadri's Sujamma, his own special sujamma brew which he promises to be the "finest sujamma to ever cross your lips".
canonical_lines: Coast has been dangerous lately... lot of Reavers about. I've heard they're searching for the treasure of Haknir Death-Brand, the pirate king. | Welcome to the Retching Netch, outlander. What can I get you? | I know everyone in Raven Rock, and they know me. You want stories? I've got plenty. | I've seen many visitors come and go, but you... there's something about you that I like. | The mine's open, people are buying drinks and I'm making money again... just like old times. | I hear you're the reason Councilor Morvayn's still breathing today. Serves those damn Hlaalu traitors right!

Name: Mogrul
description: Mogrul is an Orc member of the Dawnguard found patrolling the exterior of Fort Dawnguard.
canonical_lines: I don't think the vampires would be stupid enough to attack us here, but you never know. | I'm glad we're finally taking the fight to those monsters. | I hope the vampires try to attack us here. A lot easier than hunting them down in their holes. | Got to keep alert. We don't want to get caught napping like the Vigilants did. | Isran knows how to fight those monsters. He's been preparing for this day for a long time. | Now that the Dawnguard are back, the vampires' reign of terror is about to end.

Name: Slitter
description: Slitter, a Dark Elf bandit, is Mogrul's right-hand man. They both go into the Raven Rock inn, the Retching Netch, for a short time during the day.
canonical_lines: I work for Mogrul. You got a problem, you talk to him. | You looking for trouble? Stay out of my way. | Get away from me, outlander. | Haven't you got something better to do? | You should listen to Mogrul. | Mogrul doesn't lend coin to outlanders. Look somewhere else.

Name: Teldryn Sero
description: Teldryn Sero, a Dark Elf spellsword, is a mercenary found in the Retching Netch. He can be hired as a follower for 500 gold.
canonical_lines: Whiterun... the pride of Skyrim. Doesn't look like much to me. Now Blacklight on the other hand, that's a proper city. | Riverwood looks peaceful. If I had to settle in Skyrim, this might be the place I'd choose. | Anytime you want to head for Skyrim, I'm right with you. I've spent enough time on Solstheim to last a lifetime. | I've got swords, spells and a few other tricks up my sleeve. You'll find I'm full of surprises. | I was enjoying our travels together before we parted company. I'd be happy to rejoin you for no charge... if you're interested. | Did you still want me to help you or not?

Name: Mirri Severin
description: Mirri Severin is a Dark Elf living in Raven Rock. She is known as the daughter of Vendil and Tilisu Severin.
canonical_lines: If you ask me, my father knows how to run things better than the Councilor. | Ugh, get away from me. | I'm worried. I think she knows. | She's jealous. Can't say that I blame her. | This town bores me. How much longer must we stay in this wretched place? | What am I supposed to do until then?

Name: Tilisu Severin
description: Tilisu Severin is a Dark Elf living in Raven Rock. She is known in town as the wife of Vendil and mother of Mirri.
canonical_lines: My husband Vendil feared visitors would shun Raven Rock. I'm glad to see he was mistaken. | I hope you enjoy your stay in Raven Rock. | Ask my husband. I'm busy. | I don't know what you're talking about. I'm here to do a job, and that's it. | I've seen the way you look at her. Do you take me for a fool? | Look, "dearest", what I do on my own time is my business. You stick to your business and we'll both be fine.

Name: Vendil Severin
description: Vendil Severin is a Dark Elf who lives in Raven Rock in Severin Manor. His wife is Tilisu Severin and his daughter is Mirri Severin.
canonical_lines: Always glad to help, serjo. What can I do for you? | I don't know. There are many who would've already expected us to leave this place. | A fair question, my friend... a fair question. | If there's anything else I can do, just let me know. | I hesitate to ask for anything else. Your family's done so much for us already. | I'm worried. I think she knows.

Name: Elder Othreloth
description: Elder Othreloth, a Dark Elf conjurer, is a priest living in Raven Rock and keeper of his town's Temple.
canonical_lines: I remember a temple on Solstheim, and working to build something. It... it was not a pleasant dream. | Spirits be praised! I'm hoping you didn't suffer much at the hands of those foul beings. Please, accept this as a token of my thanks. | Though our spirits may be low, and danger lurks beyond the light, I am confident that They will never abandon us again. | Of course I'm certain. I wished to bless the tombs with the monthly invocation, but I couldn't get the door to open. | I'm certain it's only stuck, Elder. I'll speak to Glover Mallory and see if he can take a look at it. | I was. I wandered the wastes of Vvardenfell, carrying the message of the Reclamations from tribe to tribe.

Name: Galdrus Hlervu
description: Galdrus Hlervu, or "Attendant Hlervu", is a Dark Elf mage working as a priest at the Raven Rock Temple. He is the assistant of Elder Othreloth.
canonical_lines: Excuse me, but the Temple is for followers of our faith alone. Outsiders aren't welcome here. | Elder Othreloth is grooming me to be his eventual replacement. Soon, I'll be running the Temple my way. | Miraak... It sounds very familiar. Not someone here, but... I'm not sure I can recall. | I can't be sure. I know I've heard that name, but it... it doesn't feel real. Does that make any sense? | There's a shrine at the Earth Stone, I think. I can't really remember... I think I knew at one time, but now I'm not sure. | Get that vile liquid away from me!

Name: Aerin
description: Aerin is an Imperial warrior residing in Riften. He rescued Mjoll the Lioness from outside the Dwemer ruins of Mzinchaleft and nursed her back to health.
canonical_lines: Aerin, I owe you my life. It would take a threat to all of Skyrim for me to depart. | I suspected as much. The real question now is do we do something about it ourselves or do we speak to the Jarl? | I visit the temple sometimes, but perhaps not often enough. Thanks for bringing this to me. | I've never seen Mjoll this upset about anything. The city really gets to her. | Mjoll told me that it isn't this bad everywhere. Maybe one day I'll venture out and see if she's right. | Mjoll's brought me nothing but safety and of mind... and some lessons with the blade.

Name: Mjoll the Lioness
description: Mjoll the Lioness is a Nord warrior and ex-adventurer residing in Riften. She is close friends with Aerin, an Imperial warrior who saved her from death outside a Dwemer ruin in which she lost her favorite sword.
canonical_lines: I'm watching you, Maven Black-Briar. One day, you'll slip up and your Imperial friends won't be there to catch your fall. | Aerin, I owe you my life. It would take a threat to all of Skyrim for me to depart. | I suspected as much. The real question now is do we do something about it ourselves or do we speak to the Jarl? | I've really missed traveling like this. Walking the breadth of Skyrim has always filled my heart with excitement. | I love the cold air... it's exhilarating. Perhaps it's because of my Nord blood, but there's something about it that makes me feel alive. | People say Skyrim is one of the most dangerous places in Tamriel. I think they're wrong... it's magnificent and I'm proud to call it home.

Name: Keerava
description: Keerava is an Argonian innkeeper in Riften. She tends The Bee and Barb alongside Talen-Jei.
canonical_lines: Mjoll the Lioness used to adventure across Skyrim before she settled down in Riften. I hear it all ended when she lost her enchanted blade. | Look around you, I'm barely keeping this rathole together. The war's seen to that. | I can't stand this city. It's impossible to make a decent living here. | What can I get you? Mead? Wine? | Head north to Winterhold. There's a College there, they teach magic. Most Nords don't care for them. Else you could go see the Jarl's wizard. Might be able to help you. | The city guard has been tearing Riften apart looking for the source of our skooma problem. I'd hate to be in their shoes if they get discovered.

Name: Louis Letrush
description: Louis Letrush is a Breton bard who is initially found in Riften, frequenting the Bee and Barb. He has an arrangement with Sibbi Black-Briar that has gone awry, with which he would like your assistance.
canonical_lines: Looking for work? I need someone to deliver a message to Sibbi Black-Briar. | I'm still looking for someone to deliver my message to Sibbi Black-Briar... | Head to the Riften jail and talk to Sibbi. Get him to tell you where Frost and his Lineage Papers are and bring them to me outside Riften. You'll be well paid for your time. | I'm not exactly... comfortable around guards or jails. Definitely not when associates of mine have been locked up. No, I'll meet you outside of town. | I'll meet you outside of town. | Go on, then. Deliver my message to Sibbi.

Name: Marcurio
description: Marcurio, an Imperial destruction mage, is a sellsword and potential follower found within the Bee and Barb in Riften.
canonical_lines: For a modest fee, I'll bring my formidable arcane powers to bear against your foes. What do you say? | Well, look what the horker dragged in. Still wandering all over Skyrim and causing trouble? | I knew you couldn't stay away. Admit it, you were lost without me. | If you change your mind, seek me out here. | Since you paid my fee recently, I suppose it's only fair that I join you once more. | Don't make me wait too long. I get bored very easily.

Name: Talen-Jei
description: Talen-Jei is an Argonian living in Riften who works at the Bee and Barb. He reveals that he is smitten over Keerava and is planning to marry her.
canonical_lines: With the rumors going around about how poorly your Guild's been doing, she's become much too bold. I'm not that foolish. The last thing I want is a war with your people. | Welcome to the Bee and Barb, milord/milady. If I can interest you in one of our special drinks, you let me know. | Is there something I do for you? | If you'll excuse me, I have other things to attend to. | Three in fact. They're my own recipe. Brought them over here from my days as a bartender in Gideon. | First is the 'Velvet Lechance' which is a mixture of blackberry, honey, spiced wine and a touch of nightshade... perfectly safe, I assure you.

Name: Edda
description: Edda is an Imperial beggar from Riften who, during the day, can be found near the market stalls, asking for septims. She sleeps in Beggar's Row between midnight and 7am.
canonical_lines: I can't take this place anymore. The trees... all the colors, but the city, so dark. Why? It makes no sense. | Are you scared of death? I am. I hear everything just stops... forever. It just all goes away, but you don't realize it. I don't want to die. | Please, please. I need but a septim. A septim is all I ask. Is that so bad? | Yes, I hope this helps. | Yes, of course. This war is causing your woes, it's hardly your fault. | I would if I could, but I can barely make ends meet.

Name: Madesi
description: Madesi is an Argonian pawnbroker and a stall merchant selling jewelry in Riften. He has a rivalry with fellow merchant Brynjolf, a member of the Thieves Guild whose "miracle cures" have been drawing business away from Madesi.
canonical_lines: Come see me in the market if you're looking for the finest custom jewelry in all of Skyrim. | They aren't invading, milord... it's simply a response to Ulfric and his Stormcloaks. I assure you everything will be fine. | If anything pleases the eye, don't hesitate to make me an offer. | Greetings, honored friend. Could I interest you in some fine jewelry? | Good, these will make wonderful settings for my rings. | The ivory from this tusk will be perfect for the inlay on my amulets.

Name: Snilf
description: Snilf is a Nord living the hard life of a beggar in Riften.
canonical_lines: Sure, sure. I'm a dirty beggar... why would you want to even speak to me. | You think you know pain? Do you!? Try watching your wife and daughter burn alive after they've been ravaged by brigands! That's pain, my friend. | Snilf, it's me. I don't have a roof over my head either, remember? | Yes, I hope this helps. | Another uncounted causality of war. Here you are, you poor man. | Certainly, my good man. Here.

Name: Hemming Black-Briar
description: Hemming Black-Briar, a Nord warrior, is the son of Maven Black-Briar and brother of Ingun and Sibbi. Mjoll the Lioness, Riften's self-appointed protector, describes Hemming as a "spoiled brat" who follows his mother's word "like a loyal dog follows its master".
canonical_lines: Once I'm in charge I have bigger aspirations than just Skyrim. I plan to bring Black-Briar Mead to all of Tamriel. | Defense? No one would dare attack Riften. I'm not sure you realize just how well connected Jarl Maven is with the Empire. With a single snap of her fingers, we could have a legion here in a fortnight. | I don't care what you think. The Empire put Maven on the throne for a reason. That's all you need to know. | I'm almost certain he'll reach the Imperial City tomorrow, so we should have an answer by the end of the week. | I don't understand how paper will help the dregs of this city, but suit yourself. | Fancy yourself the adventuring type, eh? It's just as well, I doubt your combat prowess would prove valuable in a real battle anyway.

Name: Ingun Black-Briar
description: Ingun Black-Briar is an aspiring Nord alchemist living in Riften. She is the daughter of Maven Black-Briar, and the sister of Hemming and Sibbi Black-Briar.
canonical_lines: Hm? Sorry, just thinking about my experiments. | My mind is on my experiments today. What did you need? | My experiments continue to go well thanks to you. How can I return the favor? | My experiments can't be completed until you've brought me those ingredients. Do hurry. | To restore Master Elgrim's supply I would need 20 deathbell, 20 nightshade and 20 nirnroot. Am I sensing that you may be interested in gathering these rare ingredients for me? | That means more time for my experiments and less time in the field. Much appreciated.

Name: Maul
description: Maul is a Nord thief and the right hand man of Maven Black-Briar, the leader of the Black-Briar family. Should Maven be named Jarl of the Rift when the Imperial Legion claim the hold, Maul becomes Maven's housecarl.
canonical_lines: Hey, if the Empire and the Stormcloaks want to kill each other over some foolish cause, I say let 'em. All I care about is two things: watchin' Maven's back and countin' the gold she pays me to do it. | I don't know you. You in Riften lookin' for trouble? | Hey, you. Get over here, I want to talk to you. | You can pretend not to hear me all you want... but you better stay out of the Black-Briars' business. | Yeah? Well, I got news for you; there's nothing to see here. Last thing the Black-Briars need is some stranger stickin' their nose where it doesn't belong. | I'm through with you. Get out of my way.

Name: Maven Black-Briar
description: Maven Black-Briar is the matriarch of the Black-Briar family, and unofficially controls most of Riften. She is the mother of Hemming Black-Briar, Ingun and Sibbi.
canonical_lines: A real piece of work, that one. Mercer's holding something over his head, keeping him loyal. Talk to Vex. She used to know him very well... if you catch my meaning. | I do respect power, and the Thalmor have that. | I don't recall seeing you at one of these before. And I know everyone who's anyone in Skyrim. | Once I'm in charge I have bigger aspirations than just Skyrim. I plan to bring Black-Briar Mead to all of Tamriel. | Defense? No one would dare attack Riften. I'm not sure you realize just how well connected Jarl Maven is with the Empire. With a single snap of her fingers, we could have a legion here in a fortnight. | I don't care what you think. The Empire put Maven on the throne for a reason. That's all you need to know.

Name: Indaryn
description: Indaryn is a Dark Elf warrior that Maven Black-Briar has appointed to run the Black-Briar Meadery in Riften. He is merely a puppet for Maven and not really allowed to make decisions on her behalf and, as a result, Indaryn struggles to keep his questionable staff in line.
canonical_lines: I'm the brewmaster around here. What did you want? | I'm quite busy. Need something or can I get back to work? | Good, now maybe I can get back to work. | Why don't they listen to me, Keerava? Why won't they do as I say? | Maybe they're afraid of you. Maybe it's your breath. How should I know? | They always try to take advantage of me. Give them an inch and they'll take a mile.

Name: Bolli
description: Bolli is a Nord citizen who lives in his house in Riften, and works at the Riften Fishery. He owns the fishery, and has a charitable nature, giving away much of his wealth.
canonical_lines: New in town, eh? Here for the fishing I'd guess. | I have no idea what you're talking about. I'd never sleep with anyone besides my good wife, . | Failed: "Hand what over? I have no idea what you're talking about. | I should say so. | Thanks again for the delivery to Markarth. What can I do for you? | Nivenor, my darling. What a pleasant surprise.

Name: Nivenor
description: Nivenor is a Wood Elf citizen who lives in Riften. Wife to the charitable Bolli, she finds his passion for giving silly and pointless.
canonical_lines: If you're looking for a handout, you'll want to speak with my husband. | If you're looking for a handout, you'll want to speak with my husband. If you're looking for company, I suppose I could do worse. | At this rate, he'll give the entire Riften Fishery away to charity. Then how will I live? | I spend every at the fishery, so I know the meaning of hard work. All those forms to fill out and file; it's exhausting! | Mark my words. If Bolli thinks he's giving all of our wealth away to some poor slob, I'll leave him high and dry. | If you're looking for a handout, you're not going to get one from me.

Name: Elgrim
description: Elgrim, an elderly apothecary, is the proprietor of his namesake store Elgrim's Elixirs. He looks after both his wife Hafjorg and his promising apprentice Ingun Black-Briar.
canonical_lines: I'm kind of busy, but if you need any potions or ingredients, let me know. | I'll be over here if you need any potions or ingredients. | Do you have any idea how busy I am? My mixture could be burning as we speak! | Take a look at my supplies if you must, but do hurry... I'm not getting any younger you know! | I have no time for Marla or Mara or whatever her name is... there's work to be done! | I'm sure my mother can compensate you for it.

Name: Hafjorg
description: Hafjorg is an elderly apothecary who helps her husband Elgrim run his alchemy shop, Elgrim's Elixirs. She is generally warm towards her husband and you, but does provide slight nagging in reference to the location of the shop along the Riften canal waterways.
canonical_lines: This place has seen better days, but it's not my fault. | Where's my head? I never even greeted you properly. | I apologize in advance for my husband's crankiness; I promise you he means well. | Thank you. I'll be sure to get Elgrim to examine this as soon as his... busy schedule allows. Here, I had him make you a few things for all that travel. | Hmmm. Never read anything from the temple before. I suppose there wouldn't be any harm in reading it. | Elgrim, honey. I'm concerned about your new pupil. She seems very... odd.

Name: Brand-Shei
description: Brand-Shei is a Dark Elf trader within Riften. He was orphaned at a young age and was raised by Argonians in Black Marsh.
canonical_lines: Yes, I heard. I think all of Skyrim knows by now. | Gems, potions, exotic ingredients... I have them all! | The fairest prices and the highest quality in Skyrim! | Can I interest you in some fine goods from Morrowind? | If there's anything you wish to purchase, just let me know. | If you're ever in the market, I deal in specialty goods from Morrowind at one of the stands.

Name: From-Deepest-Fathoms
description: From-Deepest-Fathoms is a disturbed Argonian citizen who can be found wandering around the docks outside Riften Fishery. She's in possession of a particular item she would like to see returned to its place of origin, due to it carrying too many bad memories for her.
canonical_lines: It is done. I won't go back to Avanchnzel. I won't. | I could have left it behind. I could have put it back where it came from. | You. You must take the Lexicon. Free me of my burden. | The memories. I cannot stand them. You must take them away, return them to Avanchnzel. You must take the Lexicon from me. Please... take it now. | I won't be the one to go back there. I won't return to Avanchnzel. You can't make me do it. | Please don't leave. You must help me.

Name: Grelka
description: Grelka is a Nord merchant who resides in Riften. She is the owner of one of the stalls in the center of town and will sell you weapons, apparel and various other miscellaneous items.
canonical_lines: Can't you bother me at my armor stand in the market? Or are you that desperate for training? | I'll teach you how to use it effectively, and keep you quick on your feet. | There are ruined sewer tunnels beneath the city. The only way I know how to get in there is through a door off of the canal. If I were you, I'd keep my feet dry and stay up here. | Grelka, I wanted to talk about that suit of leather armor you sold me. The fasteners are already coming loose. | Why should I pay for a repair on something I just bought? | All right, all right. Just bring it to me tomorrow and I'll fix it for you.

Name: Haelga
description: Haelga is a Nord citizen and the promiscuous owner of Haelga's Bunkhouse in Riften. She saved her niece Svana Far-Shield from the orphanage when her parents died, and now has her working as a maid in the Bunkhouse.
canonical_lines: New around Riften? Take my advice. Stay at the Bee and Barb... the Bunkhouse isn't for you. | Oh, it's you. Come to extort more from me? | There's a College in Winterhold that teaches magic. It's very secretive, though, and you know how people hate secrets. The Jarl's court wizard was taught there. That one's full of secrets. | I don't know why I bother. | What does he want now? I already explained to him that you can't get blood from a stone. | Please! Don't take the statue! It's the only thing of value I have left!

Name: Niluva Hlaalu
description: Niluva Hlaalu is a Dark Elf citizen who works at Black-Briar Meadery but is struggling to pay her rent and is worried Indaryn will fire her for being a skooma addict.
canonical_lines: Hey, don't look at me that way! Who are you to judge me? | So what if I have a problem? I know how to deal with it! | If Indaryn finds out, he'll have me thrown out of the Meadery for certain. So don't breathe a word of it to him. Not a word! | Oh what am I going do? I can barely afford to stay at the Bunkhouse. | I'm going to stop. That's what I'm going to do! Then everyone will see that I'm not as useless as they think. | Maramal thinks he's so smart; tells me that Mara will cleanse the filth from my blood. Ha! What does he know?

Name: Svana Far-Shield
description: Svana Far-Shield is a Nord living and working in Haelga's Bunkhouse in Riften.
canonical_lines: Try and get those marks without violence please. I don't want to be responsible for their deaths... or yours. | I'd appreciate it if you'd keep your hands to yourself. | The Bunkhouse isn't an inn. I'd recommend the Bee and Barb. | I'm saving myself for someone special. | No! I'm saving myself for someone special. Someone who loves me dearly. | You're wrong! Sibbi and I are in love and you're just jealous!

Name: Tythis Ulen
description: Tythis Ulen is a Dark Elf citizen living in Riften and working at the Riften Fishery. He lives in the Bunkhouse.
canonical_lines: If Riften is the best Skyrim has to offer, I may as well turn back right now and just head for home. | They aren't invading, milord... it's simply a response to Ulfric and his Stormcloaks. I assure you everything will be fine. | I'm beginning to think leaving Mournhold was a mistake. | Oh, interesting. I'll read it this evening. | But I've heard from the Snow-Shods that they intend to burn this place to the ground! | Brand-Shei... have you managed to find those boots for me?

Name: Ungrien
description: Ungrien is a Wood Elf food vendor and an employee of the Black-Briar Meadery in Riften. He staffs the tasting counter at the front of the meadery, and gives glowing endorsements of the mead and Maven Black-Briar's business practices under penalty of dismissal (and possibly execution).
canonical_lines: Welcome! Allow me to recommend the Black-Briar Mead... the purest brew in all of Skyrim! | Hello there, can I pour you a Black-Briar Mead? | We serve only the best here... Black-Briar Mead. May I get one for you? | I'm off work at the Black-Briar Meadery. I'll be back there in the morning. | Damn it! Sorry. I'm not very good at this. Look, just don't say anything to Maven, will you? I've spent weeks trying to get that right and if she sees how stupid I am, she'll get rid of me. | Yeah... fired. Sure. That's what I meant.

Name: Wujeeta
description: Wujeeta is an Argonian citizen who works at the Riften Fishery. When first spoken to, she requests your help in overcoming a skooma addiction, for which she simply requires a healing potion.
canonical_lines: I overheard Bolli say that only the Jarl carries the key to the warehouse. When I meet Sarthis there, he's usually waiting for me outside with his bodyguard. | What am I going to do? | Oh, my aching head. Why do I do this to myself? | Please help me, I'm going to lose my job at the Riften Fishery. | I really need your help! I don't want to lose my job at the fishery! What am I going to do? | I wouldn't even clean my tail with this trash.

Name: Alessandra
description: Alessandra is an Imperial priestess of Arkay, who is solely and bitterly responsible for tending to Riften's Hall of the Dead. She is quite uncomfortable about her line of work, due to some unfinished business in the past.
canonical_lines: This is all I know how to do... prepare and inter the deceased. | I'd like to make the journey to the Hall of the Dead in and give my ceremonial dagger to Andurs, the caretaker. I believe once it's interred with my father's remains I'll be at peace with his memory. | Yes. Perhaps one day I'll be able to make the journey, but for now I need to tend the dead. | I hadn't asked you because I was afraid you'd say no. I appreciate your help. Here, I'm entrusting you with the dagger. Bring it to Andurs and he'll take care of the rest. | She finally decided to return it to her father. Bless her heart. I will make sure this finds its way to his tomb as soon as possible. Thank you for bringing it, my child. | The pilgrimage is complete, I can feel it.

Name: Iona
description: Iona is a Nord warrior who is assigned to you as a housecarl when you become a thane of the Rift. She does not exist until this happens and she is available to follow you as soon as she does.

Name: Constance Michel
description: Constance Michel is an Imperial citizen who works at Honorhall Orphanage in Riften. While the orphanage leader Grelod the Kind mistreats the children with beatings and verbal abuse, Constance is entirely different and takes great pride in making the children feel safe and loved.
canonical_lines: I will do nothing of the sort. You have no business here. I'm sorry, but you really must leave. | Grelod... murdered. Here. I... I need... air... You... you... Get out of here! Get out! | I'm sorry, but the children aren't up for adoption right now. You should go. | If there's nothing else, I really must ask you to leave. | I can't believe Grelod is dead. I had no love for the woman, but to be murdered, in cold blood... | This war has created so many orphans, and so few are willing to open their hearts to them...

Name: Francois Beaufort
description: Francois Beaufort is an Imperial child who lives in the Honorhall Orphanage in Riften. His parents left him at the orphanage around a year prior to the events of the game, and he is waiting for them to return and collect him, as they promised him they would.
canonical_lines: Those who shirk their duties will get an extra beating. Do I make myself clear? | That's better. Now scurry off, my little guttersnipes. | I do hope mummy and father come to get me soon. Ever since that Aventus boy left, Grelod has been so angry. | My parents dropped me off here a year ago. But they're coming back to get me. They said so. | I heard he went back to his house in Windhelm, and is trying to get the Dark Brotherhood to come and murder Grelod. That would be so wonderful. | I heard he went back to his house in Windhelm, to try and get the Dark Brotherhood to come and murder Grelod. And... he actually pulled it off!

Name: Grelod the Kind
description: Grelod the Kind is the elderly sadistic headmistress of the Honorhall Orphanage in Riften. She beats the children and refuses to allow their adoption.
canonical_lines: I hate it here. Grelod the Kind is the meanest person in all of Skyrim. | Those who shirk their duties will get an extra beating. Do I make myself clear? | That's better. Now scurry off, my little guttersnipes. | Samuel is getting to be a problem. Fancies himself something of a 'leader' to the other children. Put an end to the nonsense, or I will. | Hroar's crying is keeping me up at night. I'll give you one chance to talk the tears out of him, or he's getting the belt. | I can't abide constant talk of his parents. 'They're coming for me.' 'This is only temporary.' The boy must accept his fate.

Name: Hroar
description: Hroar is a Nord child living at the Honorhall Orphanage in Riften. He was apparently named after the sound of a lion's roar.
canonical_lines: Those who shirk their duties will get an extra beating. Do I make myself clear? | That's better. Now scurry off, my little guttersnipes. | I was named after the sound a lion makes. You know: rrroooaaaarr! Really. | It's too bad the orphanage ain't letting us kids get adopted. I'd be the best son ever. | Please, mister/lady. You got to get me out of here. Ever since Aventus left, Grelod's been even meaner than usual. I swear, she's going to kill one of us. | I used to hate it here, but it's not so bad anymore. Constance is real good to us.

Name: Runa Fair-Shield
description: Runa Fair-Shield is a Nord child, and the only female child in the Honorhall Orphanage in Riften. She claims that she is "strong and quick" and willing to do any chore.
canonical_lines: I hate it here. Grelod the Kind is the meanest person in all of Skyrim. | Those who shirk their duties will get an extra beating. Do I make myself clear? | That's better. Now scurry off, my little guttersnipes. | I'm strong, and quick, and can do any chore you want. Come on, adopt me. Please? | Things are much better now that Grelod is dead, but still. I'm sick of this place. | Kill one person, and you can solve so many problems. I wonder at the possibilities.

Name: Samuel
description: Samuel, an Imperial child, is one of the children living in Honorhall Orphanage in Riften. His mother used to work at the orphanage before she died when he was born.
canonical_lines: Those who shirk their duties will get an extra beating. Do I make myself clear? | That's better. Now scurry off, my little guttersnipes. | Grelod ain't going to like some stranger just hangin' around. Not that I care. | Sometimes, when she goes to her room to work or sleep, I slip out for a bit. Noontime, every day - that's when I hit the streets. A boy's gotta make a livin', right? | Samuel is getting to be a problem. Fancies himself something of a 'leader' to the other children. Put an end to the nonsense, or I will. | I can't abide constant talk of his parents. 'They're coming for me.' 'This is only temporary.' The boy must accept his fate.

Name: Marise Aravel
description: Marise Aravel is a Dark Elf food vendor selling her produce near The Bee and Barb, close to Riften's market. She used to live in High Rock.
canonical_lines: My food is the best in all of Skyrim; always as fresh as the day it was collected. | I run the meat and produce stand at the market. Come by and see me if you want some of the freshest food you've ever eaten. | Thanks, I was actually worried I'd run out. | Oh! Well if you change your mind, come back and see me. | Perfect, just perfect! These should help keep my stock fresh for quite a while. And, as promised, here's my trade. Hope you like it. | Can't tell you. It's a bit of a trade secret I picked up when I was living in High Rock.

Name: Dravin Llanith
description: Dravin Llanith is a Dark Elf farmer who works and lives at Merryfair Farm. He is married to Synda Llanith.
canonical_lines: Dragons, thieves and war. I should have stayed in Morrowind. | I can't stand them, they represent everything I hate about Skyrim. They're tearing the place apart! | You even think of taking something from my house... and I'll kill you. | Fine. I'll wait until someone else with a little more courage comes along. | That's it! That's my bow! | Thanks. It would be nice to have it back... means a lot to me.

Name: Synda Llanith
description: Synda Llanith is a Dark Elf farmer who works and lives at Merryfair Farm with her husband Dravin. She believes in honest pay for honest work and will pay you for harvested wheat, cabbage, and gourd.
canonical_lines: I can't stand them, they represent everything I hate about Skyrim. They're tearing the place apart! | I'm afraid we have little to offer you in the way of hospitality. | You'll have to forgive my husband's manners. He's not used to pleasantries. | The war's been hard on the farm and hard on Dravin. We don't know what to expect from one day to the next. | Someday, I hope we can get away from here... perhaps move back to Morrowind.
