This isn't at all what we expected. | Now that you've brought Dravin's bow back, he's back to his crabby old self. For better or for worse, I still thank you.

Name: Anuriel
description: Anuriel is a Wood Elf and the steward to Jarl Laila Law-Giver of Riften. She is usually found at the Jarl's side in Mistveil Keep and will sleep from midnight to 8am in her bedroom just beyond the doorway to the inner part of the Keep.
canonical_lines: Welcome to Mistveil Keep. I'm Jarl Laila's steward, Anuriel. | Anuriel, would you please stop declaring that the city is safe for the Jarl to roam? You and I both know that isn't true. | I believe it to be a falsehood perpetrated by the Empire in order to weaken the confidence in your ability to rule. | As Riften's Steward I'm afraid I can provide only limited assistance, but speak your mind. | I'm afraid my days as the steward of Riften has come to an end. | The Imperials now control Riften and have placed Maven Black-Briar on the Jarl's throne.

Name: Harrald
description: Harrald, a Nord warrior, is the son of Jarl Laila Law-Giver and brother to Saerlund. He lives in Mistveil Keep in Riften.
canonical_lines: Out of my way. The son of the Jarl has no time for idle conversation with travelers. | I see you still have the run of the place, traitor. | Come now brother, don't act all high and mighty. I know you could care less about the war one way or another. The things you say are just an act to please mother. | If this isn't about official city business, you're wasting my time. | I presume you have something important to say? | Hmph. I should have known better than to ask a knave.

Name: Laila Law-Giver
description: Laila Law-Giver is the Jarl of Riften, though the amount of influence she actually has over the citizens of the Rift is debatable.
canonical_lines: I believe it to be a falsehood perpetrated by the Empire in order to weaken the citizen's confidence in your ability to rule. | So enters Maven Black-Briar, the glint of Imperial coin in her eyes. So tell me, what's the price for a woman's integrity these days? | Welcome to Riften, traveler. I hope the road fared well for you. | What can I do for you? | Speak, I wish to hear everything my subjects have to say. | I feel so helpless out here. The people of Riften depended on me, and I failed them.

Name: Saerlund
description: Saerlund, a Nord citizen, is the son of Jarl Laila Law-Giver and brother of Harrald. He lives in Mistveil Keep in Riften.
canonical_lines: I see you still have the run of the place, traitor. | Come now brother, don't act all high and mighty. I know you could care less about the war one way or another. | Come to gloat, have we? To poke fun at the jarl's youngest son? | I can feel you laughing behind my back. | Did Harrald send you to taunt me? | I'm disappointed in you brother. You had it all. You had it all and your mouth cost you everything.

Name: Unmid Snow-Shod
description: Unmid Snow-Shod is a Nord warrior who lives in Mistveil Keep as the housecarl of Jarl Laila Law-Giver. He is the son of Vulwulf and Nura Snow-Shod, and brother to Asgeir.
canonical_lines: As Jarl Laila's housecarl, I would ask that you maintain a respectable distance from her at all times. | I'd request that when speaking to Jarl Laila, you show the proper respect. | Everyone knows the Thieves Guild uses the old sewer system beneath Riften as their hideout. I'd go down there myself, but that would leave Jarl Laila unprotected. | I killed scores of the Empire's men before I was forced to surrender. | Anuriel, would you please stop declaring that the city is safe for the Jarl to roam? You and I both know that isn't true. | Being in exile is the most humiliating thing I've experienced in my life.

Name: Wylandriah
description: Wylandriah is the court wizard of Riften. She lives in Mistveil Keep and provides shop services for a variety of magical implements.
canonical_lines: In fact, if you could retrieve a few things for me, I could start on my next set of experiments. | Excellent. I can't wait. Well... why are you still standing here? Oh, right. You need to know where they are! That would help. | Excuse my disorganization, but I'm in the middle of some delicate experiments. | Did we have an appointment? No... a delivery? I can't remember. | I know you. Where was it? Hm. Oh! You were getting something for me? | Okay, now where did I put those spiders?

Name: Shavari
description: Shavari, a Khajiit elemental mage, is a Thalmor agent who has been tasked with killing you in Riften.
canonical_lines: And now you pay for meddling in the Thalmor's affairs. | The Thalmor's memory is long. | Pff. Go away, if you know what's good for you. | Esbern cannot elude us, fool.

Name: Bersi Honey-Hand
description: Bersi Honey-Hand is the Nord owner and vendor of the general goods store Pawned Prawn in Riften. His wife Drifa lives in the store with him.
canonical_lines: Petty threats and fist waving are not going to sway me. You people are all talk, and everyone knows it! Pay to protect me? You can't even protect yourselves! | Likewise. Now, I have a lot to do, so I'm afraid you'll just have to leave! | I'm going to make you sorry for this! | Please, reconsider what you're doing. Maybe I can pay later... | Please, no more. I'll pay, I'll pay. Here! | Oh! There's no need for that. I know why you're here. Tell Brynjolf not to worry about it anymore. Oh! And look, I even have the payment I owed. Here, take it.

Name: Drifa
description: Drifa is a Nord pawnbroker living in Riften. She owns the Pawned Prawn with her husband Bersi Honey-Hand, and will take over the business if anything happens to him.
canonical_lines: If there's anything you need, I'd be glad to help you. | My husband Bersi will be the death of me. He spends too much time worrying about Riften and not enough about himself. | Bersi's afraid that someone's making a move to take the Pawned Prawn from us. I think he's being paranoid. | I just care about my husband so much and I hate to see him in this state. | Drifa? I was looking through our books and there's an entry for 'spices.' Says we spent 300 septims. What is that? | Spices? I'm certain I have no idea what you're talking about.

Name: Brynjolf
description: Brynjolf is a Nord thief, and a member of the Thieves Guild in Riften. He is initially found at a market stall in the center of Riften selling mysterious red elixirs, although he will usually approach you and inform you that he knows you didn't make all of your money legally (or if you don't have much gold on you, will offer you a way to make some).
canonical_lines: Be careful, lad/lass. This is the last place in Skyrim I'd ever want to send you. Just find a way in, get the information and leave. And you have permission to kill anyone that stands in your way. | That'd be Vald. A real piece of work, that one. Mercer's holding something over his head, keeping him loyal. Talk to Vex. She used to know him very well... if you catch my meaning. | Come out and face me, you coward! | I can smell your fear. | Come on. Show me you've got what it takes! | I think they got the message.

Name: Delvin Mallory
description: Delvin Mallory is a Breton thief and an important member of the Thieves Guild in Riften. He is always found in the Ragged Flagon, located inside the Ratway.
canonical_lines: I've got a mark from a person who just got an item from a relative outside of Skyrim. They probably should have been quieter about it. You in? | Come back later and I may have some work for you. | Talk to me again if you prove your worth to Brynjolf. | We're goin' to get along nicely. So, if you've got the nerve, I've got plenty of extra jobs to help get the Guild back on its feet. | Brynjolf, huh? Lemme guess. He plucked you off the street and dropped you into the thick of things without tellin' you which way is up. Am I right? | Lyin' to my face isn't gonna win you any favors with me, I can promise you that. On the other hand, takin' some of these extra jobs off my hands, now that'll get you to the top of my list.

Name: Dirge
description: Dirge is an Imperial food vendor and a member of the Thieves Guild who lives and works at the Ragged Flagon as their bouncer. He is brother to Maul, a former member of the Guild.
canonical_lines: They call me Dirge, 'cause I'm the last thing you hear before they put you in the ground. Why? You think it's funny or something? | {{Hover|Yeah, my problem is I'm too tired to punch your face in.|No, no. Sorry if I sounded so mean there. My brother always said I have a big mouth. Here, have one on me. | I think I have some more of these padding my mattress. | Maul? Yeah. That jerk works for Maven Black-Briar now. Left me down here watching this garbage heap. I guess he's better at all that talking stuff anyway. I'd just mess everything up. | Stay outta trouble, or there's gonna be trouble. | Last person who made trouble for Vekel ended up with a smashed head.

Name: Galathil
description: Galathil is a Wood Elf found in the Ragged Flagon in Riften. She can change your appearance for the price of 1000 gold, but not your race or gender.
canonical_lines: Hmm, I suppose I can work with your face. After all, the sculptor cannot always choose the finest clay. | What have you done to yourself? I can't work with you in this state, night-walker. | I assumed you were here to see me about your face. To have it sculpted into something more... artful than Nature has bestowed upon you. | So are you here as a client? Shall I remake your face? | Hmm. Very well. Just tell me what you desire - beauty or hideous deformity, nothing is beyond my skill. | But, my techniques only work on the living, not those that have crossed over into undeath.

Name: Sapphire
description: Sapphire, a Nord thief, is a member of the Thieves Guild in Riften. She has a rather traumatic personal story and is thus a tough character, especially when it comes to repaying debts.
canonical_lines: They call me Sapphire because I love to steal them. | Don't get me wrong, I'll steal any gem that isn't nailed down. But there's something about those blue stones that gets my blood boiling. | When people ask my real name, know what I tell them? Don't ask. | Sure I might have some dirt in my past, but that's just ancient history. | I'm a deadeye marksman, lethal with a blade and dangerous in bed. And it only gets better from there. | Last time someone cheated me, I cut off their hand. Still have it actually.

Name: Tonilia
description: Tonilia is a Redguard pawnbroker, fence, and lookout for Delvin Mallory. As you progress in the guild, you will be able to gain access to three more fences by handling a moon sugar delivery for her.
canonical_lines: So, you're the new recruit, eh? Well, looks like you and I are going to have to get very well acquainted. | Are you sure? Once I exchange it for you, I'm not taking it back... unless you want to sell it at a discount of course. | Good. Then there isn't much more to say. Here's your armor, just make sure you put it to good use. | Okay, there you go. | Make up your mind already. | Subsequently, she'll thank you:

Name: Vekel the Man
description: Vekel the Man is the Nord food vendor and bartender at the Ragged Flagon, which he owns. He has 100 barter gold and sells cheap food and ingredients.
canonical_lines: The last person that made trouble for Vekel ended up floating in the canal... catch my drift? | I wouldn't stay here long if I were you. | Don't make trouble or else I'll ask Dirge to 'walk' you out. | So, you're Brynjolf's new protege, eh? Don't look like much to me. | Something has to change soon or I may as well pack this place up and leave it for the skeevers. | I hear even the simple jobs are becoming difficult. It never used to be like this.

Name: Vex
description: Vex is an Imperial thief and an important member of the Thieves Guild in Riften. She is always at The Ragged Flagon, located within The Ratway in Riften.
canonical_lines: You need advice? Talk to Brynjolf... I got better things to do. | I'm not here to hold your hand. Figure this one out for yourself. | One, I'm the best infiltrator this rathole of a Guild's got, so if you think you're here to replace me, you're dead wrong. | Then we understand each other... good. Now, it's time to get your feet wet and I don't want to waste a lot of time talking about anything but business. | I'm not going to sugar-coat it for you. We're in a bad way down here. | Who knows. Old Delvin thinks it's some kind of a curse. I think he's crazy. If you want my opinion I say it's just plain old bad luck.

Name: Drahff
description: Drahff is a Nord bandit archer who can be found scheming alongside Hewnon Black-Skeever in the entrance to the Ratway. Both men will attack you on sight.
canonical_lines: I dunno, Drahff. They'd skin us alive if they knew we were doin' this. | Why are you always acting like such a big baby? I've gotten us this far. | You worry about bashing people's heads in, I'll worry about the Guild. Okay? | I'm going to check the entrance to the Ratway. Be right back. | No need to get hasty. I... I was just testing you. You can go on ahead. Let 'em go, Hewnon. All clear. | I doubt that very much.

Name: Esbern
description: Esbern, an elderly Nord conjurer, is a chronicler and agent of the Blades who has survived the investigations of the Thalmor by hiding in Riften's Ratway. Long obsessed with the foretold return of the dragon Alduin, the World-Eater, Esbern serves as your mentor, guide, and advisor.
canonical_lines: Oh, how reassuring! Most likely you're with the Thalmor and this is just a trick to get me to open the door! | One moment, I know time is of the essence, but mustn't leave secrets for the Thalmor... there's one more thing I must bring... | Hasn't she told you? We were both Blades, back when that still meant something. Now we're just fugitives from the Thalmor. I didn't know she was alive, but I'm not surprised. She was a survivor. | Very true! Hmm. I wonder... do you know the Jarl's palace in Whiterun? | What?! No, that's not me. I'm not Esbern. I don't know what you're talking about. | Delphine? How do you... so you've finally found her, and she led you to me. And here I am, caught like a rat in a trap.

Name: Gian the Fist
description: Gian the Fist is an Imperial thief located in the Ratway beneath the city of Riften. As his name suggests, he uses unarmed attacks that do significant damage.

Name: Hefid the Deaf
description: Hefid the Deaf, a Nord beggar, is part of the insane community found in the Ratway Warrens underneath Riften.
canonical_lines: Ha ha ha ha! No! Never find me! | Inkpot. Stone. Bucket. Book. Knife. | Bucket. Knife. Book. Inkpot. Stone. No, no, no. | Knife. Yes. Book. Yes. Bucket. Yes. Inkpot? No. | Go away. Go away. Go away!

Name: Hewnon Black-Skeever
description: Hewnon Black-Skeever is a Nord warrior who can be found scheming alongside Drahff in the entrance to the Ratway. Both men will attack you on sight.
canonical_lines: I dunno, Drahff. They'd skin us alive if they knew we were doin' this. | Why are you always acting like such a big baby? I've gotten us this far. | You worry about bashing people's heads in, I'll worry about the Guild. Okay? | I'm going to check the entrance to the Ratway. Be right back. | This far? We're livin' in a sewer. You said we'd have a house as big as the Black-Briars' by now. | What are you lookin' at?

Name: Knjakr
description: Knjakr is an elderly cook living in The Ratway Warrens of Riften. He is implied to be a cannibal, and when approached will try to entice you into his cell.
canonical_lines: Yes, yes. Closer. I won't bite. Promise. | I have a present for you. A nice, shiny present. | I'm going to eat well tonight, my darling! | Give me just one... little... bite! | I've been waiting for you, my sweet one. | No, no, no. I've got more blood than you know!

Name: Salvianus
description: Salvianus is a retired soldier who fought in the Great War between the Thalmor and the Empire. He subsequently went mad and moved into The Ratway Warrens (below Riften).
canonical_lines: They were golden, even when they were dead. But their blood was red. I knew it would be. | I had a medal. It had someone's face on it. I threw it away, after. It didn't help. | It's hopeless. He told me. He's right, you know. | He explained it all to me. Before, when I still understood. I don't remember, but I still know. | You don't think there's any hope, do you? I don't. He's right. | I finally understand what it all means. What its all for. The killing. And the dying.

Name: Snippy
description: Snippy is a unique mudcrab being raised by Viriya in her attempts to farm mudcrabs. It grows from the juvenile mudcrab you bring her during the quest In A Pinch and will be found following her around Riften docks the next time you visit her.

Name: Swims-In-Deep-Water
description: Swims-In-Deep-Water is the master angler of the Riften Fishery, and he takes you under his tutelage so that you can master the craft, as well as to keep him from financial ruin. He is the main person you interact with in the Fishing creation along with Viriya.
canonical_lines: You expect me to believe that? | You are like my brood to me. Here. A token. | Maybe you can help me. | You've already learned all I can teach you. | That doesn't change anything. | It speaks the truth.

Name: Viriya
description: Viriya is a retired Redguard adventuress who works at the Riften Fishery, where she handles special fishing requests and attempts to breed mudcrabs in the hope of selling their meat on the town's market. She is the main person you interact with in the Fishing creation along with Swims-In-Deep-Water.
canonical_lines: Maybe you can help me. You're headed to Dawnstar. It's all in this note. This won't be easy, but that's why I'm sending you. Think you can handle it? | I understand. I've come to rely on you. There's something I want you to have. It was my father's. It would be my honor if you carried it. May the gods keep you. | By the Gods. I don't even know what to say. | It would seem so. I wanted to give you this, a token of my appreciation. Thanks, good luck out there. | We sure show them, eh? Thanks for your part in all that. | Stay sharp out there.

Name: Molgrom Twice-Killed
description: Molgrom Twice-Killed is a Nord thief and a former member of the Thieves Guild. He is currently doing time in the Riften Jail as a punishment from Brynjolf who disapproved of his methods.
canonical_lines: Get away from me. | Piss off. I'm busy. | Waste of talent throwing me in here, right? | Yeah, I was lucky though. After Vex tore the guy to pieces, she gave me a potion. If she hadn't been there... Anyway, after I got back to the Guild, I decided to start calling myself Twice-Killed. | Give Vex a kiss for me. | What? What are you looking at?

Name: Sibbi Black-Briar
description: Sibbi Black-Briar is a Nord thief and a member of the Black-Briar family; his brother is Hemming Black-Briar, and his sister is Ingun. Maven Black-Briar is his mother.
canonical_lines: Have you come to gawk at me or is this a social call? | Well, well. Aren't you a sight for sore eyes. I've always got time for lovely ladies. | Visit me anytime. I'm not going anywhere soon. | When I find that bitch, I'm going to wring her little neck. | That's the spirit. Find her and I promise you'll be well compensated. | Well, if it isn't my little problem-solver.

Name: Threki the Innocent
description: Threki the Innocent is a Nord thief imprisoned in Riften Jail. She wears only a roughspun tunic and a pair of footwraps.
canonical_lines: They threw me in here because I called Ulfric a liar; a deceiver. | Let me out of here, damn it! I'm not a criminal! | Ulfric doesn't care about Talos. He yearns for the throne of Skyrim and he'll say anything to rally people to his side. | But now, because of Ulfric's greed, countless lives have been lost. | On second thought, maybe I'm better off in here. At least I'll die with a clear conscience. | This is what happens to you when you speak your mind in this city.

Name: Hofgrir Horse-Crusher
description: Hofgrir Horse-Crusher is a Nord warrior and the owner of Riften Stables, which he runs with his assistant Shadr. He gained his name following an incident in his youth, during which he got drunk and managed to break a young horse's back after trying to mount it.
canonical_lines: Bah, I'm not afraid of anything out here. Walls are just a weakling's hiding spot. Now me, I'm no weakling. In fact, I bet I've got the strongest arms in all of Skyrim. | Well come back when you earn some more so I can take it from you! | I don't blame you. If I were in your shoes, I'd chicken out too. Well, offer still stands if you ever want to have a go. | I don't believe it. I guess you were right! | Not at all. It's good to be put in your place once in a while. I guess I shouldn't boast so much... it gets expensive! | Suit yourself. I'm always here.

Name: Shadr
description: Shadr is a Redguard farmer living in Riften. He works as Hofgrir Horse-Crusher's assistant in the stables.
canonical_lines: Yes, I heard. I think all of Skyrim knows by know. | Hofgrir is a great teacher. I've learned so much about horses from him. | Horses are all I know. I've been riding since I could walk. | If the forest wasn't so dangerous, I'd spend every spare moment riding. | If it's horses you want, you'll want to speak to Hofgrir. I'm afraid I'm in too much trouble to help you. | If it's horses you want, I'm not sure I can help you. I have too much on my mind as it is.

Name: Sigaar
description: Sigaar is a Nord warrior, and the carriage driver in Riften. For a fee, he can transport you to any of the other major cities in Skyrim.

Name: Orini Dral
description: Orini Dral is a Dark Elf bandit and bodyguard to Sarthis Idren. He protects Sarthis by patrolling inside the entrance to Riften Warehouse, and watching for intruders.

Name: Sarthis Idren
description: Sarthis Idren is a Dark Elf bandit and skooma dealer based at the Riften Warehouse. He is a source of displeasure for the jarls of Riften, who have tried to get rid of him.

Name: Romlyn Dreth
description: Romlyn Dreth is a Dark Elf thief working for the Black-Briar Meadery. He is secretly stealing Black-Briar mead from the meadery, and sells bottles of it for 10 gold each.
canonical_lines: I'm selling the best mead in town for cheap. Interested? | Here you go. Oh, and keep it quiet. This stuff isn't exactly easy to get a hold of at these prices, if you catch my drift. | Suit yourself. If you change your mind, I'll be around. | I believe you have a delivery to make. | A Dreth never forgets a traitor. You'll pay for this! | Do you know what Indaryn told me? He said if I'm late one more time he'll kick me right out the door!

Name: Asbjorn Fire-Tamer
description: Asbjorn Fire-Tamer is a Nord blacksmith working for Balimund, the owner of the Scorched Hammer in Riften. In the event of Balimund's demise, Asbjorn will assume the role of blacksmith at the Scorched Hammer.
canonical_lines: Oh, well thank you. I'll try and read this if I can. | Have you met Balimund? I'm his apprentice. He's teaching me all about being a blacksmith. | I used to live at the orphanage here in Riften. Grelod said my ma and da left me there when I was a baby... that they didn't want me. | Balimund took me in and has been so kind to me. I don't know why, but he thinks I can be a great blacksmith like him. | At first I don't know why he picked me. I never even held a hammer in my life! But the first time I picked it up, it just felt right! | Folks in town say that Balimund is the greatest blacksmith in all of Tamriel. I hope I can live up to that some day and make him proud of me.

Name: Balimund
description: Balimund is a Nord and the local blacksmith of Riften as well as an expert-level trainer in Smithing. He wears a blacksmith's apron and a pair of shoes.
canonical_lines: Shutting this place down would dishonor my family's heritage, but what other choice will I have? | Lazy good-for-nothing, isn't he? Sent you down here when I'm a stone's throw from the keep? Here's the sword. Tell Harrald to stop slaying walls and perhaps his blade will stay in better condition! | Thank you for the blessing, I'll put this to good use. | You wish to do what I do? Very well. | If you need any more smithing work, come see me again. | Well, it was. Sadly, this forge is dying and I've used the last of my fire salts. If I can't feed it soon, it may grow cold.

Name: Addvild
description: Addvild is a Nord farmer who works for Vulwulf Snow-Shod at Snow-Shod Farm with his recently married wife, Leonara Arius. He appreciates honest work and will pay a septim each for leeks and potatoes and five septims each for wheat.
canonical_lines: My darling, you've made me the happiest woman in all Skyrim. | Vulwulf has been quite generous to let me take care of their farm considering I am not part of their family. | The are very trusting to let me run this place without their supervision. | I'm glad there are guards outside the farm. I don't think I could defend the place alone. | Pretty quiet out here on the farm. My wife and I kind of like the solitude. | Did you see the fires from Goldenglow Estate? I was afraid the whole place was going to burn to the ground!

Name: Leonara Arius
description: Leonara Arius is an Imperial farmer who works with her recently married husband Addvild at Snow-Shod Farm.
canonical_lines: This was the first door in Skyrim I knocked upon, and little did I know my future husband would be answering the door. | My darling, you've made me the happiest woman in all Skyrim. | Addvild's made me a happy woman. I wouldn't want to be with anyone else in the world. | My husband and I were just recently married. | I like the farm life. It's a huge change from living in a city. | I'm originally from Cyrodiil, but I left there after I had a falling out with my family.

Name: Asgeir Snow-Shod
description: Asgeir Snow-Shod is a Nord warrior living in Riften. He works at the Black-Briar Meadery as Maven Black-Briar's investor and partner.
canonical_lines: The best. Vittoria's a good woman. Any man should be so lucky. I know my father's not happy about me marrying an Imperial, but that's his problem. | I'll rest easy when we've driven every last Imperial pig back across the Jerall Mountains! Now, do me the honor I deserve as your father and drink up! | Now now, fella. Let's not get too close to my new bride, eh? | Well aren't you a pretty thing? Should my new bride worried? He he he he. | Not really my usual read, but I'll give it a try. | Sorry, I'm afraid I can't help you much right now... I'm currently in mourning.

Name: Nura Snow-Shod
description: Nura Snow-Shod is a Nord priestess of Talos, and wife to Vulwulf Snow-Shod. She has two sons, Asgeir and Unmid Snow-Shod.
canonical_lines: Talos bless you, my child. | Ugh. Get this awful paper away from me, by Talos! | To think, my little boy is married. And to an Imperial. Oh, they grow up so fast. | Oh. Why... why certainly. I keep telling myself I'm not losing a son, I'm gaining an Imperial. Daughter. An Imperial daughter... | Then by Talos, you do what you feel you're meant to do. I'll take care of your father for you. | How can a humble servant of Talos help you today?

Name: Vulwulf Snow-Shod
description: Vulwulf Snow-Shod, a Nord warrior, is a staunch supporter of the Stormcloaks, and the patriarch of Clan Snow-Shod. He is husband to Nura Snow-Shod, a Talos priest, and their sons are Asgeir and Unmid.
canonical_lines: Be brief Imperial, there's only so long I can stand being downwind of your stench. | Ah! A fellow Nord! I trust you're bringing the fight to the Empire. | Oh, I know enough. I know those trouble-making Stormcloaks refuse to submit to Imperial authority. Such seditious behavior. Why it's treason! | I still think my boy should have had a traditional Nord wedding. Outside, in the cool mountain air. | No, I'm not having a good time. My boy just married a gods-forsaken Imperial. Skyrim is full of eager Nord women, and he beds down with the enemy. | It was the Empire killed her! I know it! They killed her so they can blame it on the Stormcloaks!

Name: Briehl
description: Briehl, a Nord warrior, is an acolyte at the Temple of Mara in Riften, where he works under Dinya Balu and Maramal.
canonical_lines: They should be back shortly. You're welcome to wait here if you like.

Name: Dinya Balu
description: Dinya Balu is a Dark Elf priestess of the Temple of Mara in Riften, and the wife of Maramal. She will request that you help some people with their love, and hand out religious missives to the citizens of Riften.
canonical_lines: Lady Mara's messenger returns. What can I help you with, my child? | Oh my, how wonderful! Lady Mara will be so pleased! Here you are. Now make sure you give out every single one, we can't afford to let even one lost soul stray from Her warmth. | Oh, splendid! Just splendid! Mara smiles upon us this day. Allow me to give you her gift... a true blessing to aid you in times of need. Despair not, her warmth will protect you always! | to meet you, I'm a priestess at the Temple of Mara. | What is it you seek, my child? | Good journey, my child!

Name: Maramal
description: Maramal is a Redguard priest of the Temple of Mara, located in Riften, and the husband of Dinya Balu. Maramal is usually found either in the Temple or walking around the city, but is located initially at the Bee and Barb inn.
canonical_lines: How may I help you, my son/daughter? | Yes. If you were ready, of course. Are you familiar with how marriage works in Skyrim? | Here you are. Now remember, people in Skyrim respond to deeds. You will find more people interested in your love if you help them. Good luck. Mara bless you. | Thank you! I can promise you that this will be put to good use. | Lost the last one I gave you? Very well, here you are. Mara keep you. | I do. Now and forever.

Name: Arnskar Ember-Master
description: Arnskar Ember-Master is a Nord blacksmith in the Ragged Flagon in Riften. He moves into the area after you complete three special high-profile jobs for Delvin Mallory.
canonical_lines: They don't call me the Ember-Master for nothing. What do you need? | Need your weapon repaired? Or could I interest you in a new one? | Looking for the best smith in Skyrim? You found . | You're in it deep... better start shoveling your way out.

Name: Cynric Endell
description: Cynric Endell is a Breton thief and a member of the Thieves Guild in Riften. Before joining the Guild, he worked as a jailbreaker but quit after a botched job landed him in a High Rock prison for three long years.
canonical_lines: So it's true. You've turned traitor. I can't believe it. | I never saw this coming, I thought we were just having a run of bad luck. | I think you're all right. In fact, I'm kind of impressed how well you're doing around here. Just keep it between us, okay? | I've seen a lot of thieves come and go, but I've never seen anyone as good as me. | I could crack the lock on any house blindfolded before you even got your picks out of your pocket. | I don't care if Vex was chosen as a Guild Third over me, she deserved it.

Name: Etienne Rarnis
description: Etienne Rarnis, a Breton rogue, is found in the dungeon of the Thalmor Embassy during the quest Diplomatic Immunity, where you are required to infiltrate the embassy to discover what knowledge the Thalmor have regarding the return of the dragons. He is being interrogated when you arrive.
canonical_lines: Ah! You made it! Good to see you, my friend. I'll never forget how you saved my life from those Thalmor maniacs. | Stop. Please. I don't know anything else. Don't you think I'd have told you already? | No... for pity's sake... I've already told you everything... | Start at the beginning, as usual. If you persist in this stubbornness I'll have... | I don't know his name. Like I've already told you a hundred- Ahhh! | Like I said, I don't know! I've seen him down in the Ratway. Maybe he lives down there, but I don't know for sure.

Name: Garthar
description: Garthar is a cocky Nord thief who joins the Thieves Guild after at least three special reputation quests have been completed. He was picked up by Vex after a roadside encounter with some wolves and it appears he has feelings for her.
canonical_lines: If I find Mercer first, I'm going to crush his skull with my bare hands! | Brynjolf tells me you'll make an excellent Guild Master. Oh, he's looking for you, by the way. | Name's Garthar. Remember it, because you're going to hear a lot about me. | Still learning the ropes, but I'll get the hang of it. | Off for some wine, eh? Drink one for me. | It's kind of an amusing tale. I was making my way down the road from Ivarstead towards Riften, and I saw Vex fighting off a couple of wolves.

Name: Herluin Lothaire
description: Herluin Lothaire is a Breton apothecary who operates a shop at the Ragged Flagon. He will move in after you have completed two Thieves Guild special reputation jobs for Delvin Mallory.
canonical_lines: So, name your poison! Ha ha ha. Oh, I never get tired of that one! | Need some sort of toxin for a special occasion? That's where I come in. | You want my advice? Pay your debt to Vex quickly and she'll forget the whole thing. | I sell all sorts of poisons for almost every occasion.

Name: Mercer Frey
description: Mercer Frey is a Breton rogue, and the initial guildmaster of the Thieves Guild. He can typically be found behind his desk in the Ragged Flagon Cistern.
canonical_lines: Mercer? This is the one I was talking about... our new recruit. | Yes, I understand. | Good. Then I think it's time we put your expertise to the test. | I'm not sure. | If you're not sure maybe you don't belong here. I'll ask again, are we clear on all of this? | I'll let that comment go because you're new here. Ask things out of turn again, and we have a problem. Now, are we clear on all of this?

Name: Niruin
description: Niruin, a Wood Elf thief, is a member of the Thieves Guild and the master trainer for Archery. Before joining the guild, he was a rich wine merchant in Valenwood, but found his previous occupation too boring.
canonical_lines: The honey they collect on that hunk of rock goes into making Black-Briar Mead; just about the best tasting mead in all of Skyrim. | Ugh. The wine in Skyrim tastes like urine compared to the fine vintages we had in Valenwood. | I don't miss home one bit. I'm glad to leave that life behind me. | I don't bother with silly pickpocketing jobs when I can make far more at the gambling table. | Come find me if you need more marksman training. | If you ever need more practice with the bow, just grab me.

Name: Ravyn Imyan
description: Ravyn Imyan is a Dark Elf thief who used to be a Morag Tong assassin, and will join the Thieves Guild once you have completed at least three of the special reputation quests.
canonical_lines: We were an assassins guild that was sanctioned by the Empire to provide public as well as private executions. We did this in the name of Mephala, whom I used to quite fervently worship. | Vex is ready to tear you apart. I hope you have a good excuse for bringing Karliah here. | I don't know, you don't look so impressive to me. I mean, you're no Gray Fox, but you must be doing something right. | I'm busy, is this important? | Are you bothering me for a reason? | Finally. Now I can get back to work.

Name: Rune
description: Rune, an Imperial thief, is a member of the Thieves Guild in Riften. As a child, he was found abandoned on a shipwreck on the north coast by a fisherman and has been trying to find out about his past through a runed stone that was found on him.
canonical_lines: Have no idea what my birth name really is, and I frankly don't care. | Being brought up by a poor family, I had to learn how to steal if I wanted to make ends meet. | Brynjolf actually caught me trying to pick his pocket in the market. If he was anyone else I would have gotten away with it. | I've never seen anyone with skills like yours. I just wanted to let you know that if you need anything, you can talk to me. | Perhaps. They could be nonsense... inane scribbles done by someone in idle boredom. But if not... if they actually mean something, they might tell me where I'm from... what ship was I on. Everything. | Actually, the fisherman who found me, the man I call my father gave it to me. Thought it was fitting I suppose. I never changed it, because it never felt right to do so.

Name: Syndus
description: Syndus is a Wood Elf fletcher who operates a shop at The Ragged Flagon. He will move into The Ratway after you complete one of the Thieves Guild's "special" jobs for Delvin Mallory.
canonical_lines: I'm surprised you're even showing your face around here after what you did. | I've got all sorts of weapons for the discerning marksman. What can I get you? | Skilled, lethal and good-looking. Yep, it's a curse. | If you're in the market for the finest bows money can buy, you're talking to the right person.

Name: Thrynn
description: Thrynn is a Nord thief and a member of the Thieves Guild in Riften. Before joining the Guild he was part of a bandit clan, but left after a violent and fatal disagreement with the chief over killing women and children.
canonical_lines: I'm the heavy hitter around here. When someone needs pushing around, I'm the one they send in. | Brynjolf pulled me in a few years ago. Said the Guild was lacking some muscle, so I joined up. | I've busted heads for Maven Black-Briar when she wants to keep her name out of it. Pays good, so why not? | I leave all that sneaking around nonsense to everyone else; believe me, when I'm coming for you... you'll know it. | Used to run with a bandit clan in The Pale. Turns out I didn't like them and they didn't like me, so we parted ways. | I joined up with them maybe ten years ago. Small clan... maybe twenty men. We'd raid villages, rob caravans... but it was always about the killing for them.

Name: Vanryth Gatharian
description: Vanryth Gatharian is a Dark Elf blacksmith who establishes a weapon store in front of the Ragged Flagon in Riften's Ratway once you have improved the Thieves Guild. He sells enchanted weapons as well as armor and the materials to make them.
canonical_lines: Can I interest you in anything? Some fine armor perhaps? | Looking to fix your armor? | Be glad to repair any damage to your Guild armor. | Come back when you're in the Guild's good graces.

Name: Vipir the Fleet
description: Vipir The Fleet, a Nord thief, is a member of the Thieves Guild and the master trainer for Pickpocket. Apparently, he earned his nickname for running all the way from Windhelm to Riften instead of mounting his horse after a botched job.
canonical_lines: I wouldn't bother smacking at those hives with a weapon. Set 'em on fire and they'll go up like kindling. | I can't believe he emptied the vault. Right from under our noses. | I heard Brynjolf wants to see you about being Guild Master. I suppose my turn will come one day, but for now you'll do nicely. | Don't talk to me, you're supposed to be at the ceremony! | You're making waves around here. I like that. If you ever want to talk about anything, you let me know. | If you need pickpocket training, just let me know.

Name: Valindor
description: Valindor is a Wood Elf warrior who was born in Valenwood. He spent most of his childhood playing in the towering trees that grow there with his brethren.
canonical_lines: Well met. I don't think they're hiring anyone at the moment, so if you're looking for work, you're out of luck. | The trees... they're beautiful here. Reminds me of autumn at home. | The fall forest makes me miss my childhood; playing among the towering trees in Valenwood and living with my brethren. | Seeing the sorry state of this city almost makes me regret my decision to leave my home when my apprenticeship completed. | I was fortunate enough to be guided by one of Silvenar's master armorers until he passed. | On his deathbed, my master urged me to take over his business but I decided against it. I chose to wander Tamriel instead.

Name: Addvar
description: Addvar is a Nord fishmonger selling his wares from the market in Solitude. He is the husband of Greta and father of Svari.
canonical_lines: I don't think he would have wanted your absolution, Rorlund. He worshiped Talos. I appreciate the thought though. | You shouldn't leave Solitude without trying our fish. Best in Skyrim. | Down at the docks. There are a few good spots, if you can avoid the East Empire Company workers and those Red Wave thugs. | You should tell her that her uncle is scum that betrayed his High King. Best she know now, Addvar. | Just a little one! I was hungry! | I'll talk with Jala, dear. I'm sure she'll accept a potato as repayment.

Name: Greta
description: Greta is a Nord citizen of Solitude. She is the sister of the recently executed Roggvir, as well as the wife of Addvar, and mother of Svari.
canonical_lines: Then like Roggvir, you're a Nord at heart. It's hard for many of the people here to understand. I think Aldis understands. I don't think I could have stayed here if anyone else had... | That's my brother up there. | I'm not looking for conversation. | If you want to talk about my brother, I'm not interested. | I'm in mourning. Can't you see that? | Are you here to talk about my brother? Do you think his execution was justified?

Name: Svari
description: Svari is a Nord child living in Solitude, and the daughter of Greta and Addvar. She is upset by the death of her uncle Roggvir, but upon further conversation she also explains she is worried about her mother, who has stopped going to the Temple of the Divines.
canonical_lines: I... I don't want to talk. | I don't have anything to say. | Mother says I shouldn't talk to people... | I shouldn't be. Mama says if I do, people are going to ask about uncle Roggvir. | I shouldn't be. Everyone wants to ask me about mama and uncle Roggvir. | Maybe if I don't talk, they won't either.

Name: Angeline Morrard
description: Angeline Morrard, a Breton apothecary, is the proprietor of Angeline's Aromatics in Solitude. Her niece, Vivienne Onis assists her in the store, and takes over ownership of the store if her aunt dies.
canonical_lines: Wonderful. I hadn't really hoped to run in to someone who had been there. | Ah, well I guess it was unlikely. I'm sorry to waste your time. | You got Aldis to tell you something? I knew you would. What did he say? | Ah, welcome to Aromatics. Can I help with some herbal needs? | No, no. Mostly, I'm just a simple herbalist. Lami over in Morthal should be able to help you. | No, no. I'm just a simple herbalist. You'll want to see Arcadia, over in Whiterun.

Name: Vivienne Onis
description: Vivienne Onis is a Breton apothecary who works at Angeline's Aromatics in Solitude with her aunt, Angeline Morrard. She becomes the owner of the store if anything happens to Angeline.
canonical_lines: It's too bad the Burning of King Olaf was called off this year. I think some of the younger bards would really have gotten a kick out of it. | I think our flutist here has gotten better since the Burning of King Olaf. | I'm so glad we got to have it. I thought for sure Elisif would never consent. | I love weddings, don't you? | I just can't wait to be a bride someday. Someday... | Oh my, yes. I absolutely love weddings. I haven't found my special someone yet, but there's still hope. Vittoria is, well, getting up in years...

Name: Aia Arria
description: Aia Arria is an Imperial bard at the Bards College in Solitude. According to her teacher, Pantea Ateia, Aia is the only student at the College with actual talent, a fact that Aia takes great pride in.
canonical_lines: I can't understand why Jorn would want to join the Imperial Legion. Soldiers only appreciate one thing, and it isn't music. | Pardon me, but I trust you've been working on your poem, on the classical heroes of Skyrim, yes? | What're you going to do after you graduate? I'm thinking of joining the Legion. I hear they're looking for skalds. Just think. Me - a war bard. | Lady Ateia has already arranged for me to perform in the court of the Jarl of Falkreath. | Lady Ateia says my voice is second only to hers. But she's selling me short. | Few can resist my charms. Even fewer are worthy of them.

Name: Ataf
description: Ataf, a Redguard bard, is a student at the Bards College in Solitude. Being the youngest member of the college, he spends his days in classes to improve his talents at his chosen profession.
canonical_lines: Viarmo is trying to get Elisif to allow the Burning of King Olaf. I don't want to look like I don't know how to drink in front of the other bards. | Anything I can do for you, I will. | I'm a bard, from the Bards College here in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying. | I think Illdi's better than Dean Pantea actually knows. She just gets intimidated when she's in class and can't sing. | I think someone has a crush on Illdi. | I went out ice fishing a few days ago.

Name: Bendt
description: Bendt is an elderly cook working the kitchen in the basement of the Bards College. He is not particularly fond of people entering his premises, which might explain why the majority of students enjoy their meals at the local tavern instead.
canonical_lines: Candy! Get your King Olaf's Festival candy! | You might as well have some sweets. I made them for the festival after all. | You've found the Bards College kitchen. Congratulations. | Meals are served to Bards at designated times. Find an inn if you want to make requests. | You look a little sturdier than most of the Popinjays around here. | Tasty, sugary treats!

Name: Giraud Gemane
description: Giraud Gemane, a Breton, is the Dean of History at the Bards College in Solitude. He teaches history and can often be found on the ground floor library, keeping a watchful eye on the students, or found drinking in the Winking Skeever.
canonical_lines: Pardon me, but I trust you've been working on your poem, on the classical heroes of Skyrim, yes? | Hello, yes? I'm the Dean of History. And scribe work, when I can find some quiet time. | My position, "Dean of History," actually covers a wide range of subjects. I teach poetry, writing, history, and the finer points of court life. | What a fascinating time to be alive! Mark me, these days will be remembered long after we're gone. | If you love history, you're in the right place. Skyrim is steeped in it. | I suppose I should return to my papers...

Name: Illdi
description: Illdi is a Nord bard studying at the Bards College. Although she respects her teacher, Inge Six Fingers, and refers to the College as a musical museum, she is very insecure and claims that she is not very well liked by anyone.
canonical_lines: Do you know I look forward to Burning of King Olaf each year just because of the fire? | Pardon me, but I trust you've been working on your poem, on the classical heroes of Skyrim, yes? | You know, I don't get the impression that I'm very well liked at the College... | I'm so glad you got back from Olaf's Tomb okay. They gave you a tougher task then they gave me. | Thank you. I'm still at the beginning of my lessons but Inge... Dean Six Fingers... says I'm quite good with the flute. | I'm a bard, from the Bards College here in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying.

Name: Inge Six Fingers
description: Inge Six Fingers is an elderly bard and the Dean of Lute at the Bards College in Solitude. When you are officially declared a member of the college at the end of Tending the Flames, she will ask you to retrieve the first hand-crafted eight-string lute ever created, starting the quest Finn's Lute.
canonical_lines: I'm Dean of Lute at the Bards College, and no, I don't give private lessons. | Only reason I'm so good with a lute is because I'm old. It takes a lifetime to master. | I'm a bard, from the Bards College here in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying. | It makes me sad whenever I think about it. | What? You did? Oh sweet Divines! You did! There is no way I can pay you what this Lute is worth. But I can teach you a few tricks I've learned over the years. | Some of them have a long way to go, but they're mostly passable. Don't let them hear I said that, though!

Name: Jorn
description: Jorn is a Nord bard who resides at the Bards College in Solitude. He is also the person responsible for preparing the effigy for the suspended festival of The Burning of King Olaf.
canonical_lines: I wish only to graduate and enroll with the Legion. My drums will lead our troops to victory. | Lisette's a fine lass, but when I join the Legion, she's not the type to wait for my return. | Pardon me, but I trust you've been working on your poem, on the classical heroes of Skyrim, yes? | It's an honor to have Viarmo for instructions on drum, but he's far too reserved for my tastes. | And why wouldn't I be? We sing tales of kings, queens and their politics, 'tis true. But do you know who really makes history? The person who writes it. | I'm a bard, from the Bards College here in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying.

Name: Pantea Ateia
description: Pantea Ateia is an Imperial bard and teacher at the Bards College in Solitude. She is an experienced flute-player from a long line of them, as her instrument has been passed down for seventeen generations.
canonical_lines: If you ask me, this damned rebellion can't end soon enough. I had to end my tour of Skyrim's courts. Too dangerous to travel. | Don't forget my name. Someday, everyone will be saying it. | It's been my honor to perform at the Blue Palace on many occasions. His late lordship was quite the admirer. | Now you can say you've met the most talented bard in all of Skyrim. | To be honest, I can't stand some of my students. Only Aia shows potential for greatness. The rest are just wasting my time. | Yes, madam. I know I have a lot to work on.

Name: Viarmo
description: Viarmo, a High Elf bard, is Headmaster of the Bards College in Solitude and as such, oversees the efforts of the three teachers, Inge Six Fingers, Pantea Ateia, and Giraud Gemane. His pride and joy is the Burning of King Olaf, an annual festival held by the College in order to celebrate Solitude.
canonical_lines: Yes. According to Giraud, our histories keeper, the portion of the Edda dealing with King Olaf might still exist in Dead Man's Respite. I need you to retrieve the poem. | I think Giraud here would give you the best explanation of both it and the history of King Olaf's Verse. You should speak to him about it. | Hmmm, Olaf did become king and Numinex escaped later. It's entirely possible... and I'm sure the court will like that, I'm writing it in. | Oh that is exciting! I'm sure the court and the Jarl will love it. I'm writing it in now. | I wish to present King Olaf's verse from the Poetic Edda. | Ah, Viarmo. I assume you are here to petition for the reinstatement of the Burning of King Olaf Festival.

Name: Kayd
description: Kayd is a Redguard child and the son of Sayma and Beirand. He can be found running around in the city of Solitude with his friend, Minette Vinius.
canonical_lines: I thought adventurers were supposed to look tough? | I bet my papa could beat you up. | I bet my papa could have beat you up. He was stronger than anyone. | Burn the King! | I suppose in wartime, people aren't looking to part with their old junk. | Not that I mind the break of course, but still.

Name: Sayma
description: Sayma is a Redguard general goods merchant who runs Bits and Pieces in Solitude. She is married to Beirand and they have one son, Kayd.
canonical_lines: Welcome. Look around. We've got everything you could want in life. At least... that's what Beirand always tells me. | Welcome to Bits and Pieces. I'm sure you'll find what you need here. | Hmph. You know, you've got a certain... fire in your eyes. Just like my Beirand. Please, feel free to look around. We've got a bit of everything. | I suppose in wartime, people aren't looking to part with their old junk. | Not that I mind the break of course, but still. | Beirand's my husband. He works the forge up at Castle Dour. I barely see him nowadays, what with him filling orders for the army.

Name: Bolgeir Bearclaw
description: Bolgeir Bearclaw is a Nord warrior and housecarl to Jarl Elisif the Fair of Solitude, never leaving her side when she holds court in the Blue Palace.
canonical_lines: The Jarl has advised me not to talk about which court attendants I'd like to punch in the face. More's the pity. | What a... fine idea, my Jarl. However, I would suggest we wait until the war has ended. After all, the General needs his soldiers out in the field, fighting battles against the Stormcloaks. | I must protest, my Jarl. A parade would make you an easy target for a Stormcloak assassin. You're safer here in the palace. | Oh, very well. I can see there's no changing your stubborn Nord minds. But when this war is won, I will have my parade! | Yes, my Jarl. | We lost another five guardsmen, my Jarl. If General Tullius continues to conscript our men and send them off to battle, we'll have none left to protect the city.

Name: Elisif the Fair
description: Elisif the Fair is the Nord Jarl of Solitude. She is the widow of High King Torygg, and rumors abound that she may take the reins of power in Skyrim herself.
canonical_lines: With the passing of my dear husband Torygg, the weighty position of Jarl has fallen to me. Please direct questions to my Steward. | Then we will immediately send out a legion to scour the cave and secure the town. Haafingar's people will always be safe under my rule. | Th...thank you, my Jarl thank you. | Your eminence, my scrying has suggested nothing in the area. Dragon Bridge is under Imperial control. This is likely superstitious nonsense. | No but my husband Torygg would want a proper burial and this is the way Nords are buried in Skyrim. | Yes, when we buried my husband I wasn't able to make an offering to Talos. I was hoping you would take something of his, a warhorn handed down from his father, and place it at a Shrine of Talos.

Name: Erdi
description: Erdi is a Nord maid living in the Blue Palace of Solitude. She is involved in the quest The Mind of Madness, where she provides you with the key to the Pelagius Wing of the palace.
canonical_lines: So exciting when new folk come into town. I bet you've had plenty of adventures. | Someday, I'll be a gallant heroine. You make it look so.. appealing. | Are you my gallant hero? Have you come to whisk me away, to a life of adventure and romance? | I made a wish in the town well that a golden knight came to take me to his castle. Oh, does it spoil the wish if I tell? | Someday, a gallant hero will ride up and take me away. Someday... | Oh, I'd get in trouble for that. It's not allowed. Besides, it's scary in there!

Name: Falk Firebeard
description: Falk Firebeard is a Nord warrior and the personal steward to Elisif the Fair, the Jarl of Haafingar. With Elisif in mourning after the death of High King Torygg, Falk Firebeard oversees all court business and will provide you with quite a few tasks on her behalf, in particular the unfortunate return of The Wolf Queen.
canonical_lines: I am the Jarl's Steward. If you have court business to tend to, you talk to me. | The Jarl is not to be disturbed with common affairs of court. You direct those concerns to me. | She still mourns greatly for her husband, High King Torygg. So she leaves many of the courtly details to me. | Then we will immediately send out a legion to scour the cave and secure the town. Haafingar's people will always be safe under my rule. | Th...thank you, my Jarl thank you. | Your eminence, my scrying has suggested nothing in the area. Dragon Bridge is under imperial control. This is likely superstitious nonsense.

Name: Odar
description: Odar is a Nord citizen who works as the cook at the Blue Palace in Solitude. While he is content to remain a cook, his humble profession allows him to overhear a great deal of rumors, including gossip about the public's lack of faith in Jarl Elisif.
canonical_lines: Jarls come and jarls go, but a good cook stays for a lifetime. And I'm a very, very good cook. | Most of the time, people just forget I'm even here. That's why I hear so much good gossip. | Everyone knows General Tullius wields the real power in Solitude. Elisif? Ah, she's a figurehead. A puppet. | Somethin' just ain't right about that Sybille. You know, I've never seen her eat a thing? That can't be natural. | Back to my errands. | Sometimes I like to think my cooking is like playing the flute or singing a song.

Name: Sybille Stentor
description: Sybille Stentor, a Breton mage, is the court wizard for Jarl Elisif the Fair, located in the Blue Palace of Solitude. She sells novice and apprentice spell tomes from all schools, along with adept Illusion spell tomes if your skill is high enough, as well as robes, soul gems, and a few scrolls.
canonical_lines: Very. I helped raise him. Oh, I could not have been more proud to see Torygg on the throne. He made a fine king. A fine king... | Then we will immediately send out a legion to scour the cave and secure the town. Haafingar's people will always be safe under my rule. | Th...thank you, my Jarl thank you. | Your eminence, my scrying has suggested nothing in the area. Dragon Bridge is under imperial control. This is likely superstitious nonsense. | And? I've seen jarls come and go. This one only became Jarl because her husband died. She'll be replaced in a matter of months. | Be quick. I have little patience for mundane concerns.

Name: Una
description: Una is a Nord citizen working as a servant and housekeeper at the Blue Palace in Solitude.
canonical_lines: I keep coming out here thinking I'll see what others see in this... frivolity. | I'm pleased when the palace is clean, I'm pleased when the beds are made on time, I'm pleased when Erdi isn't around. Most of those don't happen very often. | Not on your life. It's dangerous in there, and Falk doesn't even like me and Erdi going in every year to clean out the spiders. | If you really want to... Just be careful of the ghost! He snuck up on me once and scared me sick. It took a week for me to feel better... | I guess I should be getting back to work. It was more fun talking, though. | Watch your feet. We just cleaned there!

Name: Bryling
description: Bryling is a Nord warrior, a thane of Solitude, and the owner of Rockwallow Mine. She has a house in the city but spends most of her day in the Blue Palace.
canonical_lines: I'm serious. If Erikur knew about us, he'd force you to resign from your position. And without you, he'd have the Jarl bent around his little finger. | You're right, of course. But when this war is over, I'll gladly resign and we can stop sneaking around like this. | I'm not just talking about my subjects. I'm talking about my people: the proud Nords of Skyrim. | I'm serious. If Erikur knew about us, he'd force you to resign from your position. And without you helping the Jarl, Solitude would be weaker. | I can always find time to share a word with a warrior. | Many condemn the Stormcloaks, but I refuse. There is honor in fighting for what you believe.

Name: Irnskar Ironhand
description: Irnskar Ironhand is a Nord warrior who lives in Solitude where he acts as Thane Bryling's personal protector. He is owed a substantial gambling debt by Octieve San, an old Breton who also lives in Solitude.
canonical_lines: I'm not just talking about my subjects. I'm talking about my people: the proud Nords of Skyrim. | What news have you heard from the war, Irnskar? | Unless we have specific business, I'm not interested. | When I was a young rascal, I broke a man's arm with a single punch. Course, I had a horseshoe hidden in my glove... | I think Bryling's the one you want to talk to. I'm mostly just here to scare away the riff raff | You don't want to talk to me. I'm just a stand in for a dead Thane.

Name: Captain Aldis
description: Captain Aldis is a Nord captain in the Imperial Legion in charge of the Solitude guards. When you first arrive in Solitude, he is personally overseeing the execution of Roggvir.
canonical_lines: Good. I'll see if I can get some copies made for the men. Here, something for your trouble. The Legion thanks you. | I'm afraid that kind of thing doesn't work on Captains in the Imperial Army. | I think needs to know. | I... I've been trying to find the time and place to let her know. | I'm sorry, I can't help you. | Well, I'm making it "need to know.

Name: Emperor Titus Mede II
description: Emperor Titus Mede II is the current Emperor of Tamriel at the time Skyrim takes place. He is an aged, wise and powerful man, who is forced into visiting Skyrim by the Dark Brotherhood, upon the command of Amaund Motierre.
canonical_lines: And, once more, I prove Commander Maro the fool. I told him you can't stop the Dark Brotherhood. Never could. | Tsk. How unfortunate. I had hoped you would honor an old man's dying wish. Well, let's be done with it then. | Thank you. Now, on to the business at hand I suppose, hmm? | Hmph. Pity. I had hoped to obtain even a modicum of justice. Well, on to the business at hand then, hmm? | Well? I won't fight you, so we may as well get this over with. | Come now, don't be shy. You haven't come this far just to stand there gawking.

Name: General Tullius
description: General Tullius is an Imperial General of the Legion. Originally from Cyrodiil, he was appointed Military Governor of Skyrim and tasked with crushing the Stormcloak Rebellion.
canonical_lines: Look around the room and you'll see what we're up against. Just between you and me, a lot of what Ulfric says about the Empire is true. | I swear Elenwen holds these parties just to make the Empire look bad. Almost makes me want to join the Stormcloaks. | That's putting it mildly. The Thalmor invited me here to remind everyone that they can tell the Empire what to do. I would refuse, but I don't want to jeopardize the peace between us. | Elenwen is a formidable woman. She lacks for neither cunning nor ambition. Now that I think of it, she would have made a fine Imperial general. | I'm telling you, Ulfric's planning an attack on Whiterun. | Don't you Nords put any stock in your own traditions? I thought the Moot chose the king. We're backing Elisif. When the Moot meets, they'll do the sensible thing.

Name: Gianna
description: Gianna is an Imperial cook in the Castle Dour Emperor's Tower. She is the castle's cook, and is assigned to assist the Gourmet when the Emperor visits Skyrim.
canonical_lines: Well, I don't mean to seem disappointed, but... I just never figured the Gourmet was an Imperial. I had dreamed of something more exotic, maybe... | You're a Nord! I never would have guessed it! But where in the world did you learn to cook like that? Here in Skyrim? Or maybe somewhere less... | It's just... Well... you're an Orc! Ha ha ha! If the people of the Empire only knew! I mean, now I understand your need for secrecy. Most would... | You're a High Elf! I... I never would have guessed it. So many of your recipes are clearly aimed at the commonfolk. It just seems... | I guessed right! I... I always imagined the great Gourmet was a Wood Elf. You see, only someone with a knowledge of woodland herbs could combine... | Well, I just never expected the Gourmet to be a Breton. It almost seems too obvious. Some of the greatest cooks have been Bretons, and...

Name: Legate Adventus Caesennius
description: Legate Adventus Caesennius is an Imperial soldier who can be found in Castle Dour in Solitude. He is a member of the Imperial Legion.
canonical_lines: Don't take too long figuring it out. I've got a war to run here. | This won't be easy, but that's why I'm sending you. Think you can handle it? | I knew I could count on you, soldier. Here this is for you. | As long as the rebels are willing to die fighting the Empire, we must be willing to oblige them. Here this is for you. | Peace may yet return to Skyrim. But until then, we fight. Here this is for you. | This is none of your concern, citizen.

Name: Legate Rikke
description: Legate Rikke, General Tullius' chief lieutenant, is a loyal Imperial officer as well as a Nord who firmly believes that Skyrim must remain part of the Empire. She personally knows the key players of the rebellion, Ulfric Stormcloak and Galmar Stone-Fist, as they were once friends and fought side by side in the Imperial Legion during The Great War.
canonical_lines: I am a loyal citizen of the Empire. My parents were Legionnaires, and I've followed in their footsteps. But more importantly, I am a daughter of Skyrim and wish to see her made whole again. | I'm telling you, Ulfric's planning an attack on Whiterun. | Don't you Nords put any stock in your own traditions? I thought the Moot chose the king. We're backing Elisif. When the Moot meets, they'll do the sensible thing. | You two, stay and guard the entrance. We don't want any Stormcloak reinforcements taking us by surprise. Everyone else, with me. | I'll never surrender Skyrim into the hands of a corrupt and dying Empire. | Well Ulfric, you can't escape from me this time. Any last requests before I send you to... to wherever you people go when you die.

Name: Solitude Guard
description: Solitude Guards are guards who act as the military and police force of Solitude. These guards serve under Jarl Elisif.
canonical_lines: If you've come to Solitude to join the Imperial Legion, speak to Rikke. If not, just keep your nose clean while you're here... or you'll end up like Roggvir. | He's the sorry bastard who's going to be executed. A gate guard who let Ulfric Stormcloak escape the city, after he killed the High King. | The warehouse is off-limits. I suggest you go back the way you came. | How should I know? I don't work for them. If you want to help, I suppose you could head to Windhelm and talk to Orthus Endario. He's in charge over there. | There's nothing for you in the warehouse, so why don't you take a walk?

Name: Bjartur
description: Bjartur is a Nord prisoner found in Castle Dour Dungeon in Solitude, apparently jailed for being a staunch supporter of the Stormcloaks.
canonical_lines: I stand with the Stormcloaks. You Imperial dogs will never break my will. Never! | Leave me be, Imperial. I haven't got anything to say to you. | I mean, the Redguards fought off the Aldmeri and the Empire, and now Hammerfell is an independent state. That's all we Nords want for Skyrim! | They aren't my "people." I'm with the Empire because they pay me. And that means I'm out here, and you're in there. So shut up, scum! | You won't get anything out of me, you filthy sons o' horkers. | You here for my next interrogation? I got nothing but time...

Name: East Empire Dockmaster
canonical_lines: Do you have business with the East Empire Company? | I will thank you not to interrupt me in my tasks. | Good day to you.

Name: Snorreid
description: Snorreid is a Nord warrior who lives in the East Empire Company Warehouse. He spends his days idly sleeping on one of bedrolls in the top section of the warehouse's southern part.

Name: Erikur
description: Erikur, a Nord warrior, is a thane of Solitude. He oversees the local businesses and imports low quality weaponry from Black Marsh to sell to the Imperial army for a high price.
canonical_lines: I understand what's best for Solitude... and Jarl Elisif knows it. | I've heard the Empire plans to levy a tax on our trading ships. Falk, you've got to do something about this. | The Jarl would be wise to remember that it is her thanes - and my profits - that are paying for this war. | A wise course of action, my Jarl. However, we have little food or water to spare. | My apologies, Jarl Elisif. I was thinking only of the importance of the war effort. | I will make the arrangements, my Jarl.

Name: Gisli
description: Gisli is a Nord ranger living in Solitude. She is the discouraged sister of the arrogant Thane of Solitude, Erikur, and spends most of her time badmouthing him.
canonical_lines: Hello. I'm Gisli. But you can just call me Erikur's sister. Everyone does. | Welcome to the center of the world. Or at least my brother Erikur's world... | Have you met my dear brother Erikur? The man's as trustworthy as a Khajiit goldmonger. | Quickly, if you please. I'm quite busy running errands for my bastard of a brother... | But you won't get any better than this from me. | I'll be sure to check 'Talk to random stranger' off my list of today's tasks...

Name: Melaran
description: Melaran is a High Elf mage who lives in Erikur's House in Solitude. He can normally be found at the Blue Palace.
canonical_lines: No doubt most folk in Skyrim consider the dragons terrifying. I find them... wondrous. | Oh, I think you'll find that magic is rare in Skyrim. Winterhold, though... The College there has it in abundance. Look there if you wish to learn. | Speak if you must, but mind your tongue. I do not suffer fools gladly. | Sybille Stentor has a grasp of magical theory that I would never have expected from a human. Even a Breton. | I make sure Erikur keeps breathing. | I provide my wisdom and insight to the court.

Name: Evette San
description: Evette San is a Nord food vendor who has a house and a market stall in Solitude. She deals in a variety of wines and other beverages.
canonical_lines: Oh, yes. A secret family recipe. The only good thing my father Octieve ever did. In fact, it's so good, no one else in Skyrim even tries to make any. | They're... adequate. Honestly, I rely on the business brought in by the Burning of King Olaf, and that's not likely to happen this year. | I've got a shipment of Spices the East Empire Company is holding up. I need someone to convince them to release it. | Do you know I look forward to Burning of King Olaf each year just because of the fire? | Viarmo is trying to get Elisif to allow the Burning of King Olaf. I don't want to look like I don't know how to drink in front of the other bards. | I think I may wait until the Burning of King Olaf to have Spiced Wine.

Name: Octieve San
description: Octieve San is an elderly Breton citizen living in Solitude, frequently found in the Winking Skeever. He lives with his daughter, Evette San, in her house.
canonical_lines: Oh, yes. A secret family recipe. The only good thing my father Octieve ever did. In fact, it's so good, no one else in Skyrim even tries to make any. | Take my advice. Never gamble. Damn Irnskar has me in debt up to the eyes. | Being old's not so bad. Daughter keeps me fed, and my working days are done. | I'm too old to work, but that don't make me too old to drink. | You want to know the one truth I've learned in all my years? Life's too hard to face sober. | You should speak to my daughter, Evette. She's the brains of the family.

Name: Fihada
description: Fihada is a Redguard fletcher and the owner of the Archery shop similarly named Fletcher in Solitude.
canonical_lines: The same place I learned my charm and quick wits: I used to be an Imperial ranger, my boy! | You'd make a better Imperial ranger than me. I may have charmed the locals, but my fellow soldiers hated my waggling tongue. | Hello and welcome, my friend. How may I help you on this fine day? | True, I may look the part of dashing swordsman, but I prefer the art of wit to that of warfare. | Perhaps I'll be seeing more of you. | Wish I could learn some of that charm. I'm just good with bows, not people.

Name: Jawanan
description: Jawanan is a Redguard fletcher. He will only appear as the vendor in the Fletcher shop in Solitude if Fihada, the original owner, dies.
canonical_lines: The same place I learned my charm and quick wits: I used to be an Imperial ranger, my boy! | You'd make a better Imperial ranger than me. I may have charmed the locals, but my fellow soldiers hated my waggling tongue. | Ah, yet another new face. I'm from Hammerfell, you see. I can hardly keep track of all the people I've been meeting. | Always good to see a fellow traveler. I hope you've found the roads relatively safe. Free from dragons, at the very least... | Fletching suits me. It's difficult, and sometimes tedious. But a well made arrow strikes forth like the fury of a god. | If you're looking for any archery or fletching supplies, you should see Fihada. I'm just the apprentice.

Name: Styrr
description: Styrr is an elderly priest of Arkay at the Solitude Hall of the Dead.
canonical_lines: You mean Roggvir. Yes. I made an appeal to Captain Aldis. He allowed Roggvir to have a proper Nord burial, as is the custom in Solitude. | Hello, my child. Arkay watch over you. | You've returned! I'm hoping successfully. | Excellent! These things do have a way of working out when people take action. I'll sanctify the remains. | May Arkay bless you with a long life, a proud death, and a restful grave. | May Arkay bless you with a long life.

Name: Ahtar
description: Ahtar is a Redguard warrior in Solitude where he works as public executioner and warden of the Castle Dour Dungeon. He recently let one of his prisoners escape and will task you with killing the escapee.
canonical_lines: Ain't got one. But I'd fancy a chance at that Jaree-Ra's scrawny neck. Filthy Argonian never would have set foot in this city, if High King Torygg was still alive. I can tell you that. | I mean, the Redguards fought off the Aldmeri and the Empire, and now Hammerfell is an independent state. That's all we Nords want for Skyrim! | They aren't my "people." I'm with the Empire because they pay me. And that means I'm out here, and you're in there. So shut up, scum! | You wouldn't be a sellsword, would you? I have a little problem you could solve. | Remember. I need that bandit dead. Not alive. | That's a load off my mind. Here's your pay.

Name: Jala
description: Jala is a Nord merchant in Solitude. She runs a produce stand at the marketplace where she can be found most of the time.
canonical_lines: Fruit, fresh off the boat! The freshest in Skyrim! | You get around, right? If you come across any Stormcloaks... gut one for me. They did the same to my brother in Whiterun. | I have my ways, but it's not easy these days. The Blackblood Marauders have been plundering ships off the coast. A lot of people been hurt by their foolishness. | It's been easier lately. The Blackblood Marauders haven't been seen since the Icerunner attack, so my shipments have been more regular. | That I do. I had a messenger from Dragon Bridge stop here on his way to the Blue Palace, not too long ago. | At times. Jaree-Ra stopped by the other day. Told me to keep an eye out for him, see if any new people arrived in the city.

Name: Blaise
description: Blaise is a Breton child who works and lives at Katla's Farm near Solitude. He sleeps outside on a hay pile in the stables.
canonical_lines: You probably want someone else. I'm just the stable boy. | I take care of the horses. And the goats. And the chickens. And the garden. | Talk to Geimund if you want a horse, or Katla if you're looking for work. I can't help you. | I hate it. Everyone feels sorry for me, so they don't treat me too bad, but...

Name: Katla
description: Katla is a Nord farmer who runs Katla's Farm southwest of Solitude. Although she is married to Snilling, he doesn't help her out with the farm as he would "only hurt himself out here".
canonical_lines: It's hard work keeping Solitude fed, but I wouldn't trust any other family to do it. | It's too cold for my bones, but that never seems to stop you, dear. | I'm gonna go play soldier with a tree! | I'll see if I can't get some work done inside, where it's warm. | My husband handles the indoor work. Just as well - he'd only hurt himself out here. | Another gorgeous gray day, right dear?

Name: Knud
description: Knud is the Nord child of Katla and Snilling. He lives with his parents at their farm near Solitude.
canonical_lines: Mama said I could help with the goats! | It's too cold for my bones, but that never seems to stop you, dear. | I'm gonna go play soldier with a tree! | I'll see if I can't get some work done inside, where it's warm. | You're a guest. And father says we have to be polite to guests. | Another gorgeous gray day, right dear?

Name: Snilling
description: Snilling is a Nord farmer who lives in Katla's Farm near Solitude. He is the husband of Katla and father of Knud.
canonical_lines: I was a much younger man when I married Katla. Now I can hardly keep up with her. | My brothers would have been appalled to see a man outdone by his wife, but not me. Without Katla, this farm would fall apart. | It's too cold for my bones, but that never seems to stop you, dear. | I'm gonna go play soldier with a tree! | I'll see if I can't get some work done inside, where it's warm. | He'll only hurt himself out there.

Name: Deeja
description: Deeja is an Argonian assassin. Prior to the related quest, she can be found at the docks of Solitude.
canonical_lines: I ain't begging. I just need a job. Anything. | Shoo! Go away! You get nothing from me. | You bother me again, maybe you will not walk away. Understand? | I know of a ship coming in from Hammerfell. Some cargo might get "lost"... | My brother told me you were coming. I'm supposed to give you what you've earned. | Ha! I'm afraid the loot's already been moved. You've been useful to the Blackbloods, though. You've earned a quick death.

Name: Dervenin
description: Dervenin is a half-mad Bosmer beggar wandering the streets of Solitude mumbling to himself. He is distraught over being abandoned by his master and wants you to get him to take him back.
canonical_lines: Oh, you just don't understand. Without him, I am not free! Without him, I am doomed! All of his empire shall fall into chaos... | Who among you can help me? | Wait, hear my plea! My master, he is lost between worlds and I cannot bring him back! | Why does everyone ignore me? Why do you turn your heads? Why will no one help me? | The flame of my master burns low. Without him, we are all lost and forever gray... Please, help us! | You! You'll help me! You help people, right? That's what you do?

Name: Jaree-Ra
description: Jaree-Ra is an Argonian rogue residing in Angeline's Aromatics in Solitude. He and his sister Deeja work for Vittoria Vici at the East Empire Company, while his brother Gulum-Ei is a Thieves Guild fence.
canonical_lines: But I'd fancy a chance at that Jaree-Ra's scrawny neck. Filthy Argonian never would have set foot in this city, if High King Torygg was still alive. I can tell you that. | I'm not interested, Jaree. | I'm not interested, Jaree. It doesn't matter how many times you ask. | I've got a little work if you're interested, traveler. | You should see me when you get bored, stranger. | You look new. I'm new, too. I think we could be friends.

Name: Jolf
description: Jolf is a Nord warrior and the ferryman for Solitude. He wears a set of farm clothes, a pair of boots, and carries an iron war axe.
canonical_lines: I can take you to any port on the coast. | May not look like much, but my boat can get you there faster than walking. | I'll take you as far as I can, but I'm not hanging around and it's gonna cost you extra. | You just enjoy the fresh air and leave the rowing to me. | You think I do this for my health? Come back when you've got the coin. | Looking to hire a boat?

Name: Noster Eagle-Eye
description: Noster Eagle-Eye is an Imperial beggar and war veteran who fought in the Great War but now lives in the streets of Solitude. He was on the battlefield when the Aldmeri Dominion attacked Anvil.
