canonical_lines: I hid here in Solitude for a long time but war, war follows a man until it's done with him. | Can you spare a septim? Lost an eye during the Great War, or I'd earn it myself... | Look at it. That's real Nord craftsmanship, that is! Let me show you an old trick I learned as a scout. Makes it harder for the enemy to notice you. | A man opens a gate and they cut his head off? Doesn't seem right to me. | Them Imperials left me to die in Anvil, and I had to fight my way back home. Then they followed me back here! | It's best you leave me be.

Name: Roggvir
description: Roggvir, a Nord warrior, is a citizen of Solitude who worked as a city guard. According to his sister Greta, he opened the city gates to allow Ulfric Stormcloak into the city when he first came with the hidden intention of challenging the then-High King Torygg.
canonical_lines: You should tell her that her uncle is scum that betrayed his High King. Best she know now, Addvar. | Roggvir. You helped Ulfric Stormcloak escape this city after he murdered High King Torygg. By opening that gate for Ulfric you betrayed the people of Solitude. | There was no murder! Ulfric challenged Torygg. He beat the High King in fair combat. | Such as our way! Such as the ancient custom of Skyrim, and all Nords! | I don't need your help. | On this day... I go to Sovngarde.

Name: Jordis the Sword-Maiden
description: Jordis the Sword-Maiden, a Nord warrior, is a housecarl assigned to you upon becoming a thane of Solitude. Jordis is eligible to become a member of the Blades, and is also a potential candidate for marriage.

Name: Endarie
description: Endarie is a High Elf tailor living in Solitude. She runs Radiant Raiment along with her twin sister Taarie, and she can be found behind the counter in the store while Radiant Raiment is open.
canonical_lines: Let me just drop everything to help you. I don't mind. Really. | I can help with purchases, but if you came to talk, see my sister. She's the one who gets on with people. It's her one virtue, really. | So good to see you again. How can I be of service? | Radiant Raiment has the finest attire in all of Skyrim. That is how we get business. | I suppose I would, but you're the only one who gets that greeting. | Oh, we do all right. Rumor is, you had a hand in the Blackblood Marauders' demise... I'm impressed.

Name: Taarie
description: Taarie is a High Elf tailor living in Solitude. She runs a tailor shop called Radiant Raiment with her twin sister Endarie and can be found there during the day.
canonical_lines: Excuse me, I'm trying to watch a traitor to the Empire lose his head. | I do hope you'll remain in Solitude. This city could do with some new blood. | Good. Here's the outfit. Try to get Elisif's opinion on it and if she likes it mention it's from Radiant Raiment. | If you're heading to the Blue Palace, you might want to rethink that outfit. | If you ever want to replace those old rags, stop by Radiant Raiment. | Nothing at all. It's very... nice. If you want something with a little more style, however,

Name: Dorian
description: Dorian is a Breton thief and a pirate aboard the Red Wave, under the command of Safia.
canonical_lines: Forget wine, women and wealth - give me a calm sea and a good book, and I'm happy. | I hear Adonato Leotelli's staying up in Windhelm. I've read a few of his works. Be good to meet him someday. | Don't misunderstand me, friend. I like my quiet, and I like to read. But you trifle with my ship, my crew, or my captain? I will kill you. | I've only been a part of the Red Wave's crew for a few weeks, but I've already learned to stay out of Captain Safia's way. | You've not seen the night sky until you've seen it on the open sea.

Name: Eris
description: Eris is a Nord witchblade and a pirate aboard the Red Wave, under the command of Safia.
canonical_lines: My father was all the family I ever had. I felt so lost when he died. But the crew of the Red Wave's my family now. | I've seen some truly wondrous sights since I took to the seas. I had no idea a man could die in so many ways... | Just yesterday, I saw a musician in the street playing a flute. It was... beautiful. I want that flute. | Dorian is helping me perfect my fencing, but I don't think I'll ever be as good as he is.

Name: Sabine Nytte
description: Sabine Nytte, a Breton warrior, is first mate aboard the Red Wave in Solitude harbor. She claims to be the only source for the illicit drug Balmora Blue in the whole of Tamriel.
canonical_lines: Whatever you're about to say, don't bother. I don't want to hear it. | Out of my way, or I'll cut you. Deep. | I'm afraid not. How else can a poor, overworked sailor like myself expect to earn a living? | Well then, you're talking to the right person. I'm the only one left in Tamriel that can get my hands on it. It's damn near impossible to find anymore. You want to buy it off of me? | I do. Still want to buy it? | You reek. A little friendly advice - take a bath and get some new clothes.

Name: Safia
description: Safia, an Imperial nightblade, is a pirate captain that commands the vessel Red Wave. She is the assassination target in the quest Kill Safia.
canonical_lines: It takes a brave soul to wander onto my deck. It'll take more than that to leave in one piece. | I'd say you're either brave or stupid, but I can already tell which one it is. | Let me guess - you have some 'business' to discuss... | The Dark Brotherhood? Yeah, yeah, yeah. You think this is the first contract I've had on my life? I'm a pirate, sweetie. Assassination attempts are an occupational hazard. Now, shall we? | I've only been a part of the Red Wave's crew for a few weeks, but I've already learned to stay out of Captain Safia's way. | You haven't faced that pirate captain yet, have you? Safia? I only ask because you're still alive...

Name: Xander
description: Xander is an Imperial nightblade who lives aboard the ship, the Red Wave. He used to be the captain of a ship known as the Argent Raptor, but now he is just a lowly crewman for Safia.
canonical_lines: I once captained a ship called the Argent Raptor. She struck an iceberg and went down, just north of Windhelm. | My shipmates scoff at my love of finery, but I know envy when I hear it. | I hail from Cyrodiil, but after a... misunderstanding with the law, I needed a swift change of scenery. | Can you make some new poultices for my...you know? | I need some things for the ship. | If you give me a list, I'll have them delivered to the dock by tomorrow morning.

Name: Beirand
description: Beirand is a Nord blacksmith who runs the smithy in Solitude with the help of his assistant, Heimvar. He will give you Imperial armor after joining the legion.
canonical_lines: These days? Aye. Most of what I make is for folk like you. There's still Imperial camps to be cleaned up, as you know. And that takes arms and armor. | I've been waiting for Roggvir's execution. Talk to me later. | A traitor right up to the end. | Yep. I have just one question for you. Light, medium, or heavy? | I got some fine armor, if you're lookin'. | Need a weapon? Hmmm... By the looks of ya, yeah... Think I've got somethin' you can use.

Name: Heimvar
description: Heimvar is a Nord blacksmith who lives and works at Solitude Blacksmith in Solitude. He is Beirand's apprentice and can most often be found using the grindstone inside the shop.
canonical_lines: Food, experience and a roof over my head in the finest city in Skyrim. If money's all you hold dear, then end up like Jaree-Ra. A plague on the city. | Ha! I like you. No man is a match for a dragon. Buying one of our blades wouldn't hurt your chances, though. | Really? I knew he was mixed up in something dangerous, but I hadn't heard he was dead. | Good hunting. Go kill some bandits for me. | May sword feast on Stormcloak flesh. | You're making progress, Heimvar. But my boy could still work the bellows better than you do!

Name: Hjorunn
description: Hjorunn is a Nord lumberjack and operator of the Solitude Sawmill. He lives in the neighboring house and watches over his small potato farm and the mill with his employee, Kharag gro-Shurkul.
canonical_lines: Come in, come in! I was just about to take a break. | We had some trouble, while back. Stormcloak tried to set the mill on fire. But we sorted her out. | Kharag, take a break and drink with me! Those trees aren't going anywhere! | Why so glum, my ugly friend? Did we run out of mead? | I have to work twice as hard because you drink up all our profits. | What did you say? I ought to break off one of those tusks and use it as my drinking horn!

Name: Kharag gro-Shurkul
description: Kharag gro-Shurkul is an Orc lumberjack who works for Hjorunn at the Solitude Sawmill. He will sell lumber to you if Hearthfire is installed.
canonical_lines: Out here at work, Hjorunn treats me like an equal. Better than most in town. | Any day is a good one, long as I don't have to go into town. But some days, Hjorunn's too drunk... | Any day is a good one, long as I don't have to go into town. But with Hjorunn gone... | Kharag, take a break and drink with me! Those trees aren't going anywhere! | Why so glum, my ugly friend? Did we run out of mead? | I have to work twice as hard because you drink up all our profits.

Name: Fridrika
description: Fridrika is a Nord citizen who lives at Solitude Stables with her husband Geimund and son Horm. Geimund owns the Solitude Stables and is a retired mercenary.
canonical_lines: Is this about the stables? With my husband gone, my son Horm handles the business. | A man like you should own a horse. Go speak with my husband. | A woman like you should own a horse. Go speak with my husband. | I really don't mean to be rude, but I don't think there's much I can help you with. | Is this about the stables? My husband, Geimund, handles the business. | My family's weathered its share of problems, but we don't dwell on the past.

Name: Geimund
description: Geimund, a Nord citizen, is the proprietor of Solitude Stables, where he lives with his wife Fridrika and son Horm, and sells horses. He used to be a mercenary, but retired after a nasty encounter with hagravens at Ravenscar Hollow.
canonical_lines: With a wife like Fridrika and a son like Horm, I'm the luckiest man in Skyrim. | You wouldn't know it, but before my family got killed? I was the luckiest man in Skyrim. | Work hard for the future and leave the past be. That's my motto. | Carriages to all the cities in Skyrim. Don't forget us. | You got business at the stables? | If you're heading up to Solitude you should stop to check out the view on your way. It's a good one.

Name: Horm
description: Horm is the adult Nord son of Geimund and Fridrika, and works with his father in the Solitude Stables at Katla's Farm outside Solitude. He will sell the stables' horses if his father dies.
canonical_lines: With a wife like Fridrika and a son like Horm, I'm the luckiest man in Skyrim. | Looking for a horse? You'll want to talk to my father, Geimund. | Need a horse? I guess I'm the one to talk to, since my da passed... | I grew up tending some of these horses. They're almost family. | When I was a boy, there was a year we couldn't afford enough feed. So I snuck my dinner out to the stables, and gave it to the horse. | I want to check on Grani, one of our horses. Goodbye, stranger.

Name: Thaer
description: Thaer is a Nord warrior, and the Solitude carriage driver. He wears a set of farm clothes and a pair of boots.

Name: Freir
description: Freir is a Nord priestess at the Temple of the Divines in Solitude. Her husband is Rorlund, the high priest of the Eight Divines.
canonical_lines: I fear attendance is down. There are so few worshippers coming to see us. Could it be that the war has weakened their faith? | Blessings of the Eight Divines upon you. How may I help? | Blessings of the Nine Divines upon you. How may I help? | I know you and father Rorlund have many duties to attend to in the temple, but sometimes I feel that there are more important matters to attend to. | I know, and I appreciate it dearly. But I can't help but feel we could be doing more... | Kynareth has blessed you with tremendous compassion, Silana. You will find a way, I'm sure of it.

Name: Rorlund
description: Rorlund is a Nord high priest of the Eight Divines in Solitude. He lives at the Temple of the Divines with his wife Freir, and Silana Petreia, a girl whom they both saved from a life on the streets.
canonical_lines: As high priest of the Eight/Nine Divines, I bid you welcome. | I'm afraid I'm running errands at the moment. But please, visit me later at the Temple of the Divines. | Well, now. I'm afraid you've caught me a bit out of my element. Most of my time is spent in the Temple of the Divines, you see. | I believe Sanguine would be pleased with this festival. | Oh, yes sir. I feel many of Solitude's families are having trouble getting by, and the wealthy do nothing to help them! | I respectfully disagree, sir. Stendarr and Zenithar may demand strength from them, but Mara and Dibella demand compassion from us.

Name: Silana Petreia
description: Silana Petreia is an Imperial priestess who lives and works in Solitude at the Temple of the Divines. She can be found wandering around Solitude or in the Temple.
canonical_lines: The Divines saved me from the streets, and they can save you from your trials, as well. | May you be saved by the Divines, as I once was. | Talos protect you. | I know you and father Rorlund have many duties to attend to in the temple, but sometimes I feel that there are more important matters to attend to. | I know, and I appreciate it dearly. But I can't help but feel we could be doing more... | Kynareth has blessed you with tremendous compassion, Silana. You will find a way, I'm sure of it.

Name: Belrand
description: Belrand is a Nord spellsword and blade-for-hire who can be found in the Winking Skeever in Solitude. He charges a fee of 500 gold.
canonical_lines: I have to admit, I admire your courage. Most folk in Skyrim wouldn't go near a place like this. | I wonder why the Dwemer came to Skyrim. Why did they build this place? I suppose we'll never know. | Just look at this place. I have seen better craftsmanship from Nord children only ten winters old. | Versatility is the key to survival. Hire me, and you'll be well prepared to face threats both magical and mundane. | I fight with both blade and spell. You might say I'm equipped to handle nearly any threat. So, shall we talk price? | By blade or by spell, if you want it dead, I'll make it dead. All you need to do is pay my fee.

Name: Corpulus Vinius
description: Corpulus Vinius is an Imperial food vendor and the owner of The Winking Skeever in Solitude. He will rent out a room to you for 10 gold a night and offers merchant services.
canonical_lines: Magic? You're on the wrong side of Skyrim. You want Winterhold... that College there. They teach it. Otherwise you can try the Jarl's wizard. I wouldn't, though. | If you need a good rumor or two, I think I can oblige... | Traitors, civil wars, dragons. Bah. Those troubles don't cross my doorway. So sit, relax. | Well, as it turns out, I had a pet skeever when I was a boy and he used to wink. | I thought so. | I've heard Jaree-Ra is hanging around the Wells District, looking for new blood to hire.

Name: Gulum-Ei
description: Gulum-Ei (also known by the alias Gajul-Lei) is an Argonian dock-worker living in Solitude and employed by the East Empire Company to maintain their warehouse. He also made significant money on the side by co-operating with the Thieves Guild, who would pay him handsomely for select items lifted from the trading company's inventory.
canonical_lines: Hasn't Mercer Frey taught you people anything? You kill me and your only contact with the East Empire Company vanishes. That's not smart for business. | Ah, you're just in time. I got in a shipment of fine wares courtesy of the East Empire Company. | I have no business with you. Leave me. | Go away. I have more important things on my mind right now. | I don't like strangers... | Ah, you're back. I'm hearing good things about the Guild through the grapevine.

Name: Lisette
description: Lisette is a Breton bard who lives and performs at the Winking Skeever in Solitude, spending most of her day there.
canonical_lines: It's too bad the Burning of King Olaf was called off this year. I think some of the younger bards would really have gotten a kick out of it. | I think our flutist here has gotten better since the Burning of King Olaf. | I'm so glad we got to have it. I thought for sure Elisif would never consent. | That's definitely going in my part of the Edda. | Sorry, I need to pay attention so I can remember this. | Uh oh. A fellow bard. I best not miss my notes or forget any lyrics.

Name: Minette Vinius
description: Minette Vinius is a Nord child who lives in Solitude in The Winking Skeever. She is the daughter of the inn's owner, Corpulus Vinius, and the sister of Sorex Vinius.
canonical_lines: Papa says I won't see Roggvir any more, but he wouldn't tell me why not. | My papa says I'm greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him! He says it means "smart," but my brother says he's wrong. | Papa used to say I was greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him. I miss him... | It's been a good week, my boy! Loud customers mean good cheer, and good cheer means a full till! You know, I ought to retire and leave you to run the Skeever, son! | I don't know, father... There's a whole world out there I want to see. | You'd just spend your time here, bossing me around and yelling for more drinks. You're practically retired already...

Name: Sorex Vinius
description: Sorex Vinius is an Imperial who works at the Winking Skeever in Solitude, and is the son of the owner Corpulus Vinius. He expects to take over the inn, should anything happen to his father (see bugs).
canonical_lines: Save me a lot of trouble. Here. Take it to the Blue Palace. | Stormcloak lover. | I think I expected more. | I guess it's all over. I expected... I don't know what I expected. | Don't mind me. I'm just running errands for the old man. | Roggvir always talked about his honor. Seems to me honorable men don't get executed

Name: Alexia Vici
description: Alexia Vici is an elderly Imperial citizen.
canonical_lines: Oh, I know enough. I know those trouble-making Stormcloaks refuse to submit to Imperial authority. Such seditious behavior. Why it's treason! | The thought of paying a dowry to that, that... Stormcloak turns my stomach. | I most certainly am not. I've just lost my daughter to a Nord beast, and my nephew Titus is too busy playing Emperor to even show up. | Typical Imperial rot. You speak of Skyrim like you know the land, know its people. You're from Cyrodiil! You know nothing about us! Nothing! | What my daughter sees in that... barbarian, I'll never know. | My nephew, the Emperor. Too busy to attend the wedding of his beloved cousin. We won't stand for this slight, I assure you.

Name: Aquillius Aeresius
description: Aquillius Aeresius is an Imperial citizen living in Solitude with Vittoria Vici.
canonical_lines: I don't have time for anyone too lazy or cowardly to make their own way in this world. | Good business for the East Empire Company means all the more gold for the Empire.

Name: Vittoria Vici
description: Vittoria Vici is an Imperial citizen of Solitude and the first cousin to Emperor Titus Mede II. She oversees the East Empire Company's holdings in Solitude and is influential and wealthy enough to be invited to Elenwen's upper-class party at the Thalmor Embassy.
canonical_lines: Now if you'll excuse me. The East Empire Company has several accounts, and it's up to me to balance them. | Oh, I know enough. I know those trouble-making Stormcloaks refuse to submit to Imperial authority. Such seditious behavior. Why it's treason! | The best. Vittoria's a good woman. Any man should be so lucky. I know my father's not happy about me marrying an Imperial, but that's his problem. | I most certainly am not. I've just lost my daughter to a Nord beast, and my nephew Titus is too busy playing Emperor to even show up. | The thought of paying a dowry to that, that... Stormcloak turns my stomach. | Oh. Why... why certainly. I keep telling myself I'm not losing a son, I'm gaining an Imperial. Daughter. An Imperial daughter...

Name: Neloth
description: Neloth is an eccentric Dark Elf wizard-lord and a Master Wizard of House Telvanni. He lives in the main tower of Tel Mithryn with his apprentice, Talvas Fathryon.
canonical_lines: Obviously not, or you would have a cup of tea in your hands. I supposed you want to be asked nicely. Very well. Please, oh hero of Skyrim. Please find Varona. I shall be ever so grateful. | The Forsworn live in the hills on the western edge of Skyrim. From there, you're on your own. I'm given to understand that they are not difficult to find, if you gather my inference. | Maybe not. I hear they've been seen over Skyrim too. Even so, there is more than enough evidence that someone is out to get me. | I don't know how he does it, but he never rests for even a moment. | Shh! Master Neloth is concentrating. At least I think he is. He might be sleeping with his eyes open again. | Don't be ridiculous. Of course I can. The question is, should I? I suppose if it's going to be done right, I'll have to do it.

Name: Talvas Fathryon
description: Talvas Fathryon is a Dark Elf conjurer living in Tel Mithryn. He is currently the apprentice of Master Neloth.
canonical_lines: If you must know, I'm trying to get this Ash Guardian spell to work. Now let me concentrate. Besides, shouldn't you be worrying about the damage to the tower? | The, uh... the lab is too small. Also, I need the ashy soil. Master Neloth knows what I'm doing. No need to bother him about it though. He's quite busy at the moment. | The, uh... the lab is too small. Also, I need the ashy soil. Master Neloth told me to practice this spell while he's away. No need to tell him about it when he gets back. He knows all about this. | It's on your head if something goes wrong. Just let me get back inside first. | I don't think you belong here. Does Master Neloth know about you? | Yes. I'm trying to summon an Ash Guardian. I'm just having trouble deciphering Master Neloth's handwriting. I don't mean to be rude, but I need to concentrate.

Name: Elynea Mothren
description: Elynea Mothren is a Dark Elf apothecary and mycologist living in Tel Mithryn. She needs your assistance in repairing the Tel Mithryn tower and in acquiring certain ingredients for her.
canonical_lines: That fetcher told me to fix his house. If I don't, he'll find a new mycologist who will. Decades of loyal service, and he'd just throw me out like yesterday's breakfast! | Hmmph. Another wanderer. I suppose you'll be wanting potions, just like the others. | I like it here at Tel Mithryn. Hardly anyone bothers me. People can be so irritating. | I've got the best selection of mushroom and fungi. | I probably have some potions around here that might interest you. | Every type of fungus in Solstheim or Skyrim. Oh, and a few other ingredients and potions.

Name: Ulves Romoran
description: Ulves Romoran is the Dunmer cook of Tel Mithryn, working for Neloth. He can be found in the Tel Mithryn Kitchen.
canonical_lines: Oh dear. Why am I out here? I need to get back and make Master Neloth's tea. | Damnable ash! No matter how much I wash the food, it still gets in it. | I hope you're not expecting a meal. I've barely got enough for them that live here. | Still can't figure out why I was working on that damned pillar. | I'd better tell Varona to bring apples and cabbage from Raven Rock. | I can't believe Varona is dead.

Name: Varona Nelas
description: Varona Nelas is a Dark Elf farmer living in the small settlement of Tel Mithryn who has the questionable honor of being personal steward to town founder Neloth. Like the other residents, she is overworked and estranged by her employer's eccentric personality and wishes herself far away.
canonical_lines: If you must know, I'm trying to get this Ash Guardian spell to work. Now let me concentrate. Besides, shouldn't you be worrying about the damage to the tower? | The, uh... the lab is too small. Also, I need the ashy soil. Master Neloth knows what I'm doing. No need to bother him about it though. He's quite busy at the moment. | The, uh... the lab is too small. Also, I need the ashy soil. Master Neloth told me to practice this spell while he's away. No need to tell him about it when he gets back. He knows all about this. | It's on your head if something goes wrong. Just let me get back inside first. | I need to tell Master Neloth about that Ash Guardian, but I don't dare leave my home. | There are rumors of dragons on the mainland. I hope they don't show up here.

Name: Amren
description: Amren is a Redguard warrior and the common trainer for One-handed. He and his wife, Saffir, have a daughter named Braith.
canonical_lines: No, I'm a Redguard. My family hails from the Imperial Province. Ah, but I left home when I was just a lad. Been wandering ever since. | When I go away, I go to war. That's no place for a little girl. | I just need to hire one, maybe two good men. | If you find that sword, please bring it to me. | Sometimes I miss the soldier's life, but when I hold my daughter in my arms, I know I made the right choice. | My wife's a good woman. She can be a little hard-headed sometimes, but I love her just the same.

Name: Braith
description: Braith is a Redguard child, and the daughter of Amren and Saffir living in Whiterun. The family is clearly dysfunctional, with Saffir buried in books all day long and Amren worrying about nothing but his lost family sword.
canonical_lines: When I go away, I go to war. That's no place for a little girl. | Boys, girls, dogs, elders - there's nobody I won't fight! | What're you lookin' at? I'm not afraid of you, ya know. Even if you are my elder. | I don't fight Lars no more, if that's what you're worried about. | Battle-Born! Give me your money! | I... I don't have any money.

Name: Saffir
description: Saffir is a Redguard who lives in Whiterun with her husband Amren and their daughter Braith. Lately she has been having arguments with her husband over his continued hunt for his father's sword which has cost the family a significant amount of money.
canonical_lines: I import books whenever I can. Most Nord works are too bawdy and violent for my tastes. | My daughter Braith says I read too much. 'Play with me, mother!' Girls need to learn how to take care of themselves. | Really, I wish my girl would read more. It would certainly keep her out of all those fights. | Excuse me, but I'm trying to read. | Damn it, I lost my page... | Back to my book...

Name: Arcadia
description: Arcadia is an Imperial apothecary and the proprietor of Arcadia's Cauldron, an alchemy shop in Whiterun. She sells potions and poisons and allows you to use her alchemy lab.
canonical_lines: Why, because I'm a proud citizen of the Empire? Because the Stormcloaks only want Nords in Skyrim? The Stormcloaks can rot in Oblivion for all I care. I've lived in this city for twenty years. Twenty! | You brewed a truly vile poison there. I pity the beast that tastes that. | You'll find tonics, salves, poultices and potions on my shelves. Browse to your heart's content. | If there's anything I can help you with, you have but to ask. | I offer remedies for ailments both common and rare. Do let me know if I can be of service. | Maybe a hardy Nord like you doesn't need a cure-all. Hmm? What about an invisibility potion?

Name: Hulda
description: Hulda is a Nord innkeeper who owns the Bannered Mare in Whiterun. She plans on retiring soon and selling the inn to Ysolda, who is trying to save up the money to buy the inn, but this never actually happens.
canonical_lines: Magic is for the weak. Elves, I mean. They keep to that College in Winterhold. They know their place. Of course there is Farengar. He might be the Jarl's wizard, but at least he's a Nord. | Pfft, I should've guessed as much. Your kind sticks to that College in Winterhold, as well they should. You'd never think to learn from a Nord like the Jarl's wizard. | I enjoy this work well enough, but I'm ready to retire. I've been thinking of selling the inn to Ysolda. Anyway, what do you need? | What can I get you? | Here for work? Get an axe and bring me all the wood you can chop. | I'm just out and about, love. The Bannered Mare can do without me for a bit, I suspect.

Name: Mikael
description: Mikael is a Nord bard performing at the Bannered Mare, and the author of A Gentleman's Guide to Whiterun. He believes himself to be quite enthralling to women and is currently attempting to conquer Carlotta Valentia romantically, though she has no interest in him.
canonical_lines: I don't have much of a reputation in Skyrim yet, but I'm working on that, one sweet lady at a time. Haha! | Maybe you're right. I guess I just didn't want anyone to think I couldn't handle one Nord lass. On my honor, I won't bother Carlotta ever again. | I'm a bard by trade. Perhaps you've heard of me? | No doubt they still tell tales of Mikael and his sweet songs across Cyrodiil, where last I traveled. | I enjoy steady patronage in the Bannered Mare. I do hope you'll watch me perform. | There was a great battle here in Whiterun. It was quite thrilling! I must compose a song about it someday.

Name: Saadia
description: Saadia, also known as Iman, is a Redguard living and working in Whiterun's inn, The Bannered Mare. She spends her days helping Hulda around the inn, sweeping the floors, cooking meals and serving food and drink to any customers or residents present.
canonical_lines: Are you sure? Oh no! They've found me? I need your help! Please, come with me. I need to speak to you privately. | Quickly, come with me. | So, are you working with them? You think you can take me? You so much as touch me, and you're going to lose fingers. I mean it! I'll... I'll cut you in half! | Then I need your help and there isn't anyone I can trust here in the city. | You can't tell them! Please, I need your help and there isn't anyone I can trust here in the city. | I'm sorry, just... Just don't hurt me. I know you're not one of them, but you just can't help them. You can't let them know I'm here. Please, will you help me? There's no one here I can trust.

Name: Sinmir
description: Sinmir is a Nord and a permanent resident of the Bannered Mare in Whiterun. He quite obviously dislikes the Whiterun and Imperial guard, and if Whiterun is taken for the Stormcloaks, he will be the new commander of the guard.
canonical_lines: Commander walks around Dragonsreach like a preening rooster. I could do his job blindfolded.... | I'm commander of the guard, now. | The Stormcloaks can keep this city safe better than those Legion lapdogs. | Well, Sabjorn. Now that you've taken care of your little pest problem, how about I get a taste of some of your mead? | Help yourself, milord. It's my finest brew yet... I call it Honningbrew Reserve. I think you'll find it quite pleasing to your palate. | I... I don't know. What's wrong?

Name: Gwendolyn
description: Gwendolyn is a Nord farmer living at Battle-Born Farm outside Whiterun.

Name: Brenuin
description: Brenuin is a Redguard beggar and drunkard living in the city of Whiterun. He wants someone to steal a rare Argonian ale from The Bannered Mare, the local inn.
canonical_lines: I ain't done nothin'. | What? Why you lookin' at me? | That Heimskr's crazy. Him and his talk o' Talos. Wish he'd just shut up. | Only if I can help it. But damn if I ain't almost sober. Hey. In the Bannered Mare, there's this bottle of Argonian Ale. The good stuff. Get it for me. They won't even know it's gone. Come on... | Argonian Ale! I can almost taste it! | Well, would you look at that! Argonian Ale! My thanks, friend. Here, take this here healing potion. I, er, "found" it in the temple.

Name: Lucia
description: Lucia is a homeless Imperial child beggar who wanders around the streets of the Wind District in Whiterun. She can usually be found outside the Temple of Kynareth, sitting on the benches around the Gildergreen tree.
canonical_lines: I played with Lucia in the park today. I feel sorry for her. | Mister/Lady, could you spare a coin? | Just one gold? Please?

Name: Belethor
description: Belethor is a Breton pawnbroker and also the owner of Belethor's General Goods in Whiterun. As a general merchant, he buys and sells almost everything.
canonical_lines: You just let me know what you need. I am at your beck and call. | Imperials, Stormcloaks... I don't care who's in charge around here. I just want to make money! | I know what you're thinking: "This Belethor, how could he part with such magnificent treasures?" | I can tell you're a discriminating customer. Perhaps you're a wealthy one, too, hm? | Everything's for sale, my friend! You need visit no other shops today! | I assure you, I'm prepared to make any deal.

Name: Sigurd
description: Sigurd is a Nord living in Whiterun. He works for Belethor at Belethor's General Goods, which incidentally is the only information you will get out of him.
canonical_lines: Don't mean to be rude, but I'm too busy to talk right now. | I work for Belethor, at the general goods store. | Come by Belethor's shop, at the market. I'm sure we've got something you need. | Shop at Belethor's for the best prices and selection. He pays me to say that.

Name: Lydia
description: Lydia is a Nord warrior living in Whiterun. She is likely to be your first housecarl, which she becomes after you are titled a thane of Whiterun upon completion of the Dragon Rising quest.
canonical_lines: The Jarl has appointed me to be your housecarl. It's an honor to serve you. | As my Thane, I'm sworn to your service. I'll guard you, and all you own, with my life. | As you wish, my Thane. | As you will, my Thane, I will protect you with my life. | I am at your command. | I am sworn to carry your burdens.

Name: Carlotta Valentia
description: Carlotta Valentia is an Imperial food vendor who runs a stall selling produce at the Whiterun market. According to Severio Pelagia, most of her wares come from his farm.
canonical_lines: That bard Mikael is begging for a dagger up against his throat, the way he goes on about me. | Life's hard enough with all these men propositioning me. But that bard is the worst. | I can take care of myself. A Whiterun woman learns how to handle a few idiot men early in life. | If you want to try, go right ahead. I don't think anything will get through that thick skull of his, though. | I've got a hungry daughter to feed, so selling my produce is really the only thing I care about. | I've really got to be going.

Name: Mila Valentia
description: Mila Valentia is an Imperial child from Whiterun. She is the daughter of Carlotta Valentia and helps her mother selling fruits and vegetables at her stall in the plains district.
canonical_lines: Tsk. And to think, I was going to feed you cabbages and apples again. Yes, little fairy, we can go to the tavern for supper. | Could I keep him? Please? | I work with my mother, to sell fruits and vegetables. It's fun most days, but hard work. | I'm afraid not, little fairy. The fruit has about another day left before it spoils. We've got to sell as much as we can, as soon as we can. | Did you know there are some fruits that actually grow better in the cold weather? | You'd be surprised how much work it is selling things at a stall. It's not all standing around and chatting.

Name: Wilmuth
description: Wilmuth is a Nord running Chillfurrow Farm east of Whiterun.

Name: Balgruuf the Greater
description: Balgruuf the Greater, a Nord warrior, is the Jarl of Whiterun, and a descendant of High King Olaf One-Eye. An admirer of the Greybeards, Balgruuf made the pilgrimage to High Hrothgar in his youth.
canonical_lines: I'm the Jarl, not a barkeep. Speak your business. | I should have guessed Ulfric would be mixed up in this. | Yes, my Jarl. | Of course, Jarl Balgruuf. You seem to have found me an able assistant. I'm sure he/she will prove most useful. | Hrongar, calm yourself. What does any of this Nord nonsense have to do with our friend here? Capable as he/she may be, I don't see any signs of him/her being this, what, 'Dragonborn.' | I'd rather be back at Whiterun, but while I'm here I might as well get some real news from the other parts of Skyrim.

Name: Dagny
description: Dagny is a Nord child who lives in Dragonsreach. She is the daughter of Jarl Balgruuf the Greater, sister of Frothar and Nelkir, and niece to Hrongar.
canonical_lines: Blasted war. I was due a new dress from Solitude, but it will never get here. | One of the new servants? Remember that I like my meat rare. | I'm so angry, I can hardly stand it! Father promised me a new dress days ago, now where is it? | Father won't let me go see the dragon. | But I don't want to be patient! I want my dress right now! Father promised! | I'm going to tell father you said that. He'll tan your hide for sure!

Name: Farengar Secret-Fire
description: Farengar Secret-Fire, a Nord elemental mage, is the court wizard of Jarl Balgruuf the Greater. He advises the Jarl when Whiterun is facing a magic-based threat.
canonical_lines: Ah! The Dragonstone of Bleak Falls Barrow! You already found it! You are cut from a different cloth than the usual brutes the Jarl foists on me. | Of course, Jarl Balgruuf. You seem to have found me an able assistant. I'm sure he/she will prove most useful. | Off to Bleak Falls Barrow with you. The Jarl is not a patient man. Neither am I, come to think of it. | Oh, have no fear. The Jarl himself has finally taken an interest, so I'm now able to devote most of my time to this research. | It seems this damnable conflict has claimed everyone's passions of late. Me, I prefer my books, and my spells. | A prospective student, are you? Well, I'm afraid I'm not much good at teaching. You should try your luck at the college of Winterhold.

Name: Fianna
description: Fianna is a Breton who works as a maid in Dragonsreach.
canonical_lines: Ain't nobody here high and mighty exceptin' the Jarl and don't you forget it! | Don't you go trackin' your dirty boots on my clean floor! | Soldiers! They're everywhere these days. And not a one of them knows how to pick up after themselves.

Name: Frothar
description: Frothar is a Nord child who lives in Dragonsreach. He is the eldest son of Jarl Balgruuf the Greater, and brother to Dagny and Nelkir, and nephew of Hrongar.
canonical_lines: Hope this war lasts long enough that I get to fight in it. Battle! Glory! | You're not from around here. I'll bet you're here to help with the war! | Father says I'm too young to train with a sword, so I practice with my fists. | I'm so angry, I can hardly stand it! Father promised me a new dress days ago, now where is it? | But I don't want to be patient! I want my dress right now! Father promised! | I'm going to tell father you said that. He'll tan your hide for sure!

Name: Gerda
description: Gerda is a Nord who works as a maid in Dragonsreach.
canonical_lines: I might just be a servant among lords, but don't think for a moment I don't take my duties as seriously as the jarl himself takes his! | This here is Dragonsreach, an' it's my task to see it looks fit for a jarl, night an' day. | If my cleanin' gets in your way, just say the word an' I'll be off.

Name: Hjornskar Head-Smasher
description: Hjornskar Head-Smasher is a Nord soldier and Stormcloak commander who is initially located at the Whiterun Stormcloak Camp.

Name: Hrongar
description: Hrongar is a Nord warrior and a descendant of High King Olaf One-Eye, who serves as an advisor and courtier for his brother Balgruuf the Greater, the Jarl of Whiterun. He resides at Dragonsreach initially, and so long as Balgruuf remains jarl.
canonical_lines: Hrongar, calm yourself. What does any of this Nord nonsense have to do with our friend here? Capable as he/she may be, I don't see any signs of him/her being this, what, 'Dragonborn.' | Proventus, you must help me. We need to convince my brother to do more for the war effort. | What would you have me do, Hrongar? Your brother is the Jarl. You know I can't question his judgement. | I'm not asking you to defy the Jarl, just to open his eyes. Use subtlety and suggestion to turn his thoughts to the war. You're his steward, he'll listen to you. | Leave me out of your intrigues, Hrongar. If you have something to say to the Jarl, say it with your own tongue. | I'm not a man, I'm a weapon in human form. Just unsheathe me, and point me at the enemy.

Name: Irileth
description: Irileth is a Dark Elf nightblade who serves as housecarl to Jarl Balgruuf the Greater of Whiterun, and remains by his side at all times within Dragonsreach. Although it is highly uncommon for a Dunmer to serve as housecarl in Skyrim, Irileth and Balgruuf fought in many battles together when they were younger.
canonical_lines: As housecarl, my job is to deal with all dangers that threaten the Jarl or his people. So you have my attention. Now, explain yourself. | Whatever you have to say to the Jarl, you can say to me. I'm starting to think... | Yes, my Jarl. | I'm taking command here for the moment. You head back to Whiterun and let the Jarl know what happened. | I don't know how you talked the Jarl into this plan of yours. | I am concerned about the Jarl's safety. Too many of our guards have been redeployed to fight the Stormcloaks.

Name: Legate Quentin Cipius
description: Legate Quentin Cipius, an Imperial soldier, is an Officer in the Imperial Legion. There are two ways of encountering Quentin Cipius, depending on whether you joined forces with the Legion or the Stormcloaks.
canonical_lines: My men are fearless. It's the Imperial milk drinkers I'm worried about. | Sir! I... I have... | My scouts tell me they're loading them with fire. | So, he wants to take my city, walls intact. | If you prefer I took my men and left... | No. Of course not. Just - don't let me down Cipius. We'll need to set up water brigades to combat the flames.

Name: Nelkir
description: Nelkir is a Nord child and the youngest son of the Jarl of Whiterun, Balgruuf the Greater. He can be found in Dragonsreach, where he lives with the family along with his brother Frothar, sister Dagny, and uncle Hrongar.
canonical_lines: Another wanderer, here to lick my father's boots. Good job. | The only grownups who talk to me are the kind I don't want talking to me. | A dragon in Dragonsreach. That's something I never saw coming. | Have I done something to make you bother me? | In the basement. Trust me, you'll see it. I bet she'll talk to you, too. | You're going to end up helping me. I know it.

Name: Proventus Avenicci
description: Proventus Avenicci is the Imperial steward to Jarl Balgruuf of Whiterun. He is also the father of Adrianne Avenicci, from whom he allegedly takes advice.
canonical_lines: I serve Jarl as steward. | The Jarl is, as you can imagine, very busy. Perhaps I can assist you? | The task of Steward is more important than you realize. The Jarl often asks for my invaluable council. | Yes, my Jarl. | Hrongar, calm yourself. What does any of this Nord nonsense have to do with our friend here? Capable as he/she may be, I don't see any signs of him/her being this, what, 'Dragonborn.' | I'm not a fool, Proventus. I mean it's time to challenge Ulfric to face me as a man, or march his Stormcloaks up to the gates.

Name: Ahlam
description: Ahlam, a Redguard priestess, is a healer at the Temple of Kynareth in Whiterun. She is the wife of Nazeem, who owns Chillfurrow Farm.
canonical_lines: Looking for my husband Nazeem? Check the Jarl's backside. That's usually where he stuffs himself these days. | Oh, it's Nazeem. He's been spending more and more time fawning over the Jarl. I swear, we're hardly ever home at the same time anymore. | But it's more than that. Nazeem is obsessed with this civil war, as is all of Skyrim. I swear, it will consume us all. | Maybe Nazeem's obsession has merit. This civil war seems so big, so unceasing... I'm afraid, Danica. Afraid for us all. | Looking for my husband? Have you checked the jarl's backside? That's usually where he stuffs himself these days. | There are those, like my husband, who feel the war is a noble endeavor. Everything is noble from the safety of your own home.

Name: Anoriath
description: Anoriath, a Wood Elf scout and hunter, owns a vendor stall in the Whiterun market, and is co-owner of the Drunken Huntsman with his brother Elrindir. He played a part in the amusing tale of how the combination of shop and pub got its name, although only his brother will tell it.
canonical_lines: Chops and steaks fit for a jarl! Come and see my selection of finest-quality meats! | You're making me... nervous. Is there something you need? | Are you... threatening me? Walk away fool. It's not worth it. | Well now, you are a strange one. You remind me a bit of myself, when I'm stalking a deer. I'm not quite sure how I feel about that... | I find your presence... unnerving. Please depart. | Remember, I've got the freshest meat in all of Whiterun.

Name: Elrindir
description: Elrindir is a Wood Elf fletcher who owns The Drunken Huntsman with his brother, Anoriath. He can be found behind the counter at The Drunken Huntsman at all times.
canonical_lines: Ah, hello my Nord/Argonian/Khajiit friend. In the market for some hunting supplies? | Ah, kinsman. How my heart soars to look upon a brother Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs? | Ah... How my heart soars to look upon a sister Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs? | So what can I get you? | I'll be happy to assist you if you'd like to purchase something. | I can tell you're no stranger to the wilds. I'm sure we can meet your hunting needs.

Name: Jenassa
description: Jenassa is a Dark Elf ranger who can be found at a table at the Drunken Huntsman shop in Whiterun. Should you choose to pay her fee, Jenassa is available as a follower for 500 gold.
canonical_lines: It is a lonely thing, facing all the dangers of Skyrim by yourself. Come and find me if you decide that you miss my companionship. | I thought about becoming a bandit. I decided I preferred clean clothes and fresh mead. | I am the shadow at your back. | Blade and shadow, silence and death - these are my arts. For a modest fee, I'll make great art for you. | I know nothing of fear and nothing of remorse. Pay my fee, and together we will vanquish any foe. | I am a lethal instrument, yours to command for a modest sum. I suggest you hire me before one of your enemies does.

Name: Nazeem
description: Nazeem is a Redguard citizen of Whiterun and the owner of Chillfurrow Farm, south of the city. He is arrogant and patronizing, much to the chagrin of his wife Ahlam and the citizens of Whiterun.
canonical_lines: I actually advise the Jarl on political matters. My input is invaluable, of course. But this is all probably a bit over your head. | Do you get to the Cloud District very often? Oh, what am I saying - of course you don't. | Oh, it took years, but I earned my way to the top. I own Chillfurrow Farm, you see. Very successful business. Obviously. | Right, I really am busy. So... | If there's nothing else, I really do have important matters tend to. | I get them fresh from the farms daily, Nazeem.

Name: Commander Caius
description: Commander Caius is an Imperial soldier stationed in Whiterun. He is in charge of the guards patrolling the hold under Jarl Balgruuf the Greater.
canonical_lines: I'll always be a commander, even if those Stormcloak dogs have stripped away my power. | The Legion will regain control of this city. Mark my words. | I'm the commander of the guard here in Whiterun. | You're not thinking of causing trouble here in this beautiful city, now are you? 'Cause I'd strongly advise against such foolishness. | I said move! | Well, Sabjorn. Now that you've taken care of your little pest problem, how about I get a taste of some of your mead?

Name: Whiterun Guard
description: Whiterun Guards are Nord guards who act as the military and police force of Whiterun Hold.
canonical_lines: Riverwood's in danger, too? You'd better go on in. You'll find the Jarl in Dragonsreach, at the top of the hill. | I can't believe it! You're... Dragonborn... | In the very oldest tales, back when there still were dragons in Skyrim, the Dragonborn would slay dragons and steal their power. That's what you did, isn't it? Absorbed that dragon's power? | Hey, when you make up your mind, just let me know. I'll be here. | Halt! City's closed with the dragons about. Official business only. | Fine, but we'll be keeping an eye on you.

Name: Andurs
description: Andurs is a Nord priest of Arkay living in the Hall of the Dead located in Whiterun. He tasks you to find his Amulet of Arkay which helps him perform the death rituals.
canonical_lines: With this terrible war, I'm a bit overwhelmed. As are the catacombs. | I spend so much time among the dead, I sometimes forget how much I miss the company of the living. | As Whiterun's priest of Arkay, I spread his word whenever I'm able. In the meantime, most of my hours are spent with the city's dearly departed. | She finally decided to return it to her father. Bless her heart. I will make sure this finds its way to his tomb as soon as possible. Thank you for bringing it, my child. | Tell me, do you believe in mighty Arkay, god of life and death? | I'm glad to know that, because I need your help. You see, I've lost something precious.

Name: Heimskr
description: Heimskr is a Nord priest of Talos in Whiterun. He can usually be found in the Wind District, in front of the shrine of Talos.
canonical_lines: Let them come! I have no fear, for Talos is my ally and I am his prophet. His word is upon my lips, his voice in my throat. | With open arms, with cheers and song, with joy in my heart and tears in my eyes! Praise be to Talos, this is a glorious day for Whiterun and for all Skyrim! Our liberators have come at last! | Trust in me, Whiterun! Trust in Heimskr! For I am the chosen of Talos! I alone have been anointed by the Ninth to spread his holy word! | You have come! You have come to hear the word of Talos! | The truth, child of Talos, is that the Dragon's children have come! To purge the world in fire and righteousness! | There are those who would silence the Dragon's truth! But not I! Not Heimskr! His word will be known!

Name: Eimar
description: Eimar is a Nord warrior who works at the Honningbrew Meadery.
canonical_lines: If you need to sample some of the finest mead in Skyrim, you're in the right place. | Mallus is teaching me everything he knows about brewing. Hopefully, I can take over his position some day. | It's a shame the Black-Briar Meadery took this place over. I never liked it as much as our Honningbrew. | If you're looking to purchase any mead, speak to the owner, Sabjorn. | We're a small meadery, but growing quickly. Sabjorn's only been running this place for a short time now and look at it. | If you're looking to purchase any mead, speak to the brewmaster, Mallus.

Name: Mallus Maccius
description: Mallus Maccius is an Imperial citizen who works at the Honningbrew Meadery in Whiterun. Once you complete Dampened Spirits, he will become the Thieves Guild fence for Whiterun, though he is actually located at the meadery rather than in the city itself.
canonical_lines: I've never met someone like him that revels so much in other's misery. | Now that this is a Black-Briar Meadery, you can be sure that we'll be selling only the best mead in Skyrim. | Maven Black-Briar always remembers her friends. That's why I'm in charge of the place. | Been a while since I've seen you at the meadery. Bring me anything interesting? | What have you got for me today? Nothing illegal I hope! | I've got plenty of good merchandise for you. Take a look.

Name: Sabjorn
description: Sabjorn is a Nord citizen who lives in and owns Honningbrew Meadery, a newly established competitor to the Black-Briar Meadery in Riften. Sabjorn had the courage to turn down an offer from Maven Black-Briar, and as a result, you will have to cause Sabjorn's downfall during the Thieves Guild quest Dampened Spirits.
canonical_lines: Welcome. I'm the owner and proprietor of Honningbrew Meadery. | What are you gawking at? Can't you see I have problems here? | Are you kidding me? Look at this place. I'm supposed to be holding a tasting of the new Honningbrew Reserve for the Captain of the Guard. If he sees the meadery in this state, I'll be ruined. | Oh really? And I don't suppose you'd just do it out of the kindness of your heart, would you? I hope you're not expecting to get paid until the job's done. | Oh, very well. Here's half. You get the rest when the job's done. My only demand is that these vermin are permanently eliminated before my reputation is completely destroyed. | Well that's not how I operate, so forget it.

Name: Avulstein Gray-Mane
description: Avulstein Gray-Mane is a Nord warrior hiding out in House Gray-Mane in Whiterun. While his brother was reported to have fallen in combat, his family believes Thorald to have been captured by the Imperial Legion instead, and that the Battle-Borns are aware of this.
canonical_lines: This was such a glorious city. But then everything had to change. The Empire came... and my brother was lost... | Avulstein Gray-Mane and I were best friends as children. If I see that traitor again, I'll throw him in jail myself! | I knew it! Let me see, please! The Thalmor? By the Nine, it's worse than I thought. So... Northwatch Keep. Then we know where to hit them. | What? You, alone, reasoning with the Thalmor? That's madness, friend. You're serious, aren't you? Very well. I'll give you a chance. But if you can't do it, I will. | Are you sure? I doubt you stand a chance against the Thalmor. I can have a group of men ready at a moment's notice. Many would be willing to help rescue my brother. | Forget Solitude or Windhelm. Whiterun was the crown jewel of Skyrim before... Well, before... Never mind.

Name: Eorlund Gray-Mane
description: Eorlund Gray-Mane is a Nord blacksmith in Whiterun in charge of forging weapons and armor for the Companions at the Skyforge, located behind Jorrvaskr. He is married to Fralia Gray-Mane, with whom he has three children: Avulstein, Olfina, and Thorald.
canonical_lines: Ha! I'd sooner bend my knee to Ulfric Stormcloak. Besides, Gray-Mane would never make steel for the Legion. | I'm not sure that it's news, but lately it seems like every blacksmith in Skyrim is out to prove that he's the equal of Eorlund Gray-Mane. | Yes, lad, you've been raised into the Companions, I hear. I can fashion whatever kind of weapon you want. Finest forge in Skyrim, right here before you. | Everyone says Eorlund Gray-Mane is the best smith in Skyrim. I plan to change a few minds about that. | I ain't much for talkin'. | Whiterun belongs to the true sons of Skyrim now, just as it should.

Name: Fralia Gray-Mane
description: Fralia Gray-Mane is an elderly Nord pawnbroker living in Whiterun. She is the wife of the famous blacksmith Eorlund Gray-Mane, and the mother of Avulstein, Thorald, and Olfina.
canonical_lines: Pleased enough, I suppose. At least they appreciate the ways and traditions of the Nord people. Can't say I ever much liked the idea of the Empire telling us what to do an' how to do it. | Oh, I always knew my Thorald was alive. Ain't a man in Skyrim who can keep a Gray-Mane down. | Bits and baubles for sale, all crafted by the best blacksmith in Skyrim. | My Eorlund is the finest blacksmith in Skyrim, and that's not just a wife's boasting. | I can't stand those braggart Battle-Borns. They think they're the gods' gift to Whiterun. | Eorlund and I... we both miss our son terribly. Thorald... he went missing, fighting the Imperials.

Name: Olfina Gray-Mane
description: Olfina Gray-Mane is a Nord food vendor who lives in Whiterun alongside the rest of her family. Despite the general uneasiness between her family and the Battle-Born family, Olfina is in a relationship with Jon Battle-Born, although both try and keep the relationship a secret from their families.
canonical_lines: I miss the days when the Gray-Manes and Battle-Borns got along. Ever since the war started, we've been at each others' throats. | It's not easy being a woman in Skyrim, I know. But stay strong, and men will come to respect you, and maybe even fear you. | I don't know how you talked the Jarl into this plan of yours. | Oh Jon, darling. You know you're the only stone-biting, knuckle-dragging Nord for me. | What's the matter? You can't stand the sight of a strong Nord woman? | The daughters of this land are just as strong and loyal as its men.

Name: Alfhild Battle-Born
description: Alfhild Battle-Born is a Nord farmer and a member of Clan Battle-Born living in Whiterun. Jon Battle-Born is her brother, and her parents are Olfrid and Bergritte.
canonical_lines: This war's as stupid as our feud with Clan Gray-Mane. | Need something? Unlike the rest of my family, I've got work to do. | My brother Jon's got the right of it - join the Bards College and avoid this pointless war. | My brother Jon had the right of it - join the Bards College and avoid this pointless war. Too bad he died before he had the chance. | I'll see you. | So, what book should I read to you tonight?

Name: Bergritte Battle-Born
description: Bergritte Battle-Born is an elderly Nord living in the House of Clan Battle-Born in Whiterun. She is the wife of Olfrid Battle-Born and has two children, Jon and Alfhild.
canonical_lines: There's a rift here in Skyrim, and can't neither magic nor the passin' of time make it right. | Evgir Unslaad has come at last. The 'Season Unending.' War... without end. | Do be careful, child. Skyrim is not as safe as it used to be. | It's a miracle these Stormcloaks haven't robbed us blind yet. I'm sure it's only a matter of time. | Can you believe those pathetic Gray-Manes still show their face in public? | There was love between our families. For generations. Now, there is only enmity.

Name: Idolaf Battle-Born
description: Idolaf Battle-Born is a Nord warrior and a member of clan Battle-Born living in Whiterun. He is fixated on the feud between his family and the Gray-Manes, as evidenced by his insistence that you choose a side as soon as you enter Whiterun.
canonical_lines: Avulstein Gray-Mane and I were best friends as children. If I see that traitor again, I'll throw him in jail myself! | Ha! I'd sooner bend my knee to Ulfric Stormcloak. Besides, Gray-Mane would never make steel for the Legion. | I used some contacts in the Legion, asked some questions. Was told I should mind my own business. | Then I say well met, friend. I could tell you were a sharp one the moment I laid eyes on you. | Then you're either a Stormcloak sympathizer or a fool. Either way, you're no friend of mine. | Got stones in your ears? I asked what side you're on, Gray-Mane or Battle-Born!

Name: Jon Battle-Born
description: Jon Battle-Born is a Nord citizen living in Whiterun. He is a member of Clan Battle-Born, rivals of Clan Gray-Mane.
canonical_lines: Oh Jon, darling. You know you're the only stone-biting, knuckle-dragging Nord for me. | Truly? Ha! By Shor, this city could use more like you. Best head up to Dragonsreach, then, and see the Jarl. He's lord of Whiterun, and can set you on the path. | A pity. And words I hear too often. But there's no shame in it. One of them dragons comes callin' in Whiterun, the place'll go up like tinder. | You know what's wrong with Skyrim these days? Everyone is obsessed with death. | People have forgotten humor, poetry and romance. I love a good sword fight as much as the next man, but there's more to life. | It's my desire to enter the Bards College. My parents are hesitant, but I pray they let me attend.

Name: Lars Battle-Born
description: Lars Battle-Born is a Nord child of Alfhild and Idolaf Battle-Born living in Whiterun. He can be found walking around Whiterun or inside his family's home.
canonical_lines: I can't fight. Not even a little bit. My family thinks I'm a little milk drinker. | My friend Olava? The seer? She says things will get better for me, once I start sticking up for myself. | I don't know what you did, but Braith won't even look at me now! So thanks! | That's all right, grandmother. I like staying home with you. | It's that Braith girl again, isn't it? Has she been bothering you again? I've a mind to speak with her mother. | No! Please, grandmother, don't do that. I'm fine. Really. Can we just... not talk about this anymore?

Name: Olfrid Battle-Born
description: Olfrid Battle-Born is a Nord warrior who is widely known in Whiterun as head of the Battle-Born Clan. He owns Battle-Born Farm outside Whiterun's walls.
canonical_lines: I'm as much a Nord as the next man, but I'm no fool. I don't live in the past, like those Gray-Manes. | My bloodin' days might be over, but my son Idolaf is out there fightin' for Skyrim! Fightin' for the Empire! Ha! | I hope the Empire is sending reinforcements to drive these heathens out of Whiterun. | You won't be laughing when the Thalmor come to root out Stormcloak sympathizers. I've already put in a claim to the land and property you'll forfeit when you're found to be a collaborator. | I have eyes within Dragonsreach. They tell me that all correspondence from other holds are sent to the Jarl's private chambers. | Olfrid, patron of the great Clan Battle-Born, a name I'm sure you know well.

Name: Aela the Huntress
description: Aela the Huntress, a Nord thief, is one of the elite members of the legendary Companions who reside in Jorrvaskr, the overturned ship and mead hall in the Wind District of Whiterun. Aela is one of the highest-ranking members and as such, is a member of the secret group, the Circle, along with Skjor, Farkas, and Vilkas.
canonical_lines: Good. I knew that we could count on you. It's simply a beast, but be cautious. The beasts of Skyrim are made of sterner stuff than most. | Kodlak says not to worry about it. I worry that a bunch of snowberries are out there earning glory while we stay out of it. But I will follow the old man's lead. As ever. | Certainly not. But a true warrior would have relished the opportunity to take on a giant. That's why I'm here with my Shield-Brothers. | Not for me to say. You'll have to talk to Kodlak Whitemane up in Jorrvaskr. The old man's got a good sense for people. He can look in your eyes and tell your worth. If you go to him, good luck. | If you wish to hunt with me, your feet need to be quick, and your eyes quicker. | So the old man thinks you've got some heart, I guess.

Name: Athis
description: Athis, a Dark Elf warrior, is a member of the Companions living in Jorrvaskr within the city of Whiterun. Once the Companions questline is completed, Athis can become a follower, a new member of the Blades, and a candidate for marriage.
canonical_lines: I swear, every wanderer who shows up in Whiterun looks weaker than the last. | I always thought the new Harbinger would be taller, myself. | I never thought they would actually let me join the Companions. But now it looks like they're letting anyone in these days. | To what do I owe the honor? | Look, I've got training to get to. | Are you thinking you're better than me? I crawled west out of the wasteland, so show some respect.

Name: Brill
description: Brill is a Nord citizen of Whiterun, and can be found in either Jorrvaskr or Dragonsreach, depending on the status of the civil war.
canonical_lines: I'd be dead if not for Vignar. He took me in and helped me turn my life around. | I had a bad time a few years back, an' I turned to drink. But Vignar, he wouldn't let me stay weak. | Ah, hello my good friend. Would you do me a kindness? I seem to be down to my last drop of spirits. Let me tell you, sobriety is highly overrated. | Oh, I'm not a Companion, myself. I just help Vignar. He took me in when I was having a tough time, and... well, I owe him. | And we've done as much as we can to prepare for that. But remember, Whiterun is home to the Companions. Any dragon that comes here will get more than he's bargained for, I think. | To hear Vignar tell it, they're a bunch of layabouts and useless whiners who used to know how to fight. But he thinks everything used to be better. I, for one, would trust any Companion with my life.

Name: Farkas
description: Farkas is a Nord and member of the Companions. While, as his twin brother Vilkas puts it, "his brains are not his strong suit", Farkas is respected as a skilled warrior.
canonical_lines: Too confusing for me. Empire, Nords, Talos. Who cares? Just tell me who needs bludgeoning. | I think that maybe Kodlak was right. As a werewolf, I can't be a good Nord. I want to be clean, like he was, and go to Sovngarde when I finally die. | My brother Vilkas is a better talker than me. He should be around someplace. | The Companions are my family. We fight so that other people don't have to. We bring honor and glory to ourselves and each other. | Vilkas and I have been here since we were little whelps. Our father, Jergen, raised us here. Even Vignar couldn't remember Companions younger than us! | I've never been a smart one. But the Companions welcome anyone with the heart of a warrior. When we step into battle, we fight for our own name, and the name of the Companions.

Name: Kodlak Whitemane
description: Kodlak Whitemane is a Nord warrior and a well-respected Harbinger of the Companions. He maintains the Companions' long-standing tradition of abstaining from political conflicts.
canonical_lines: I hear your heart beat like the Harbingers of old. Your glories in Skyrim are seen and honored. | Some nights I dream about the mists of Sovngarde. | When you get to be my age, you'll come to miss the smell of blood. Strange, I know. | Politics are something best avoided. I prefer more personal confrontations, myself. | But I still hear the call of the blood. | You have my brother and I, obviously. But I don't know if the rest will go along quite so easily.

Name: Njada Stonearm
description: Njada Stonearm is a Nord warrior and member of the Companions. She resides in Jorrvaskr within the walls of Whiterun.
canonical_lines: I'm still trying to figure out why Skjor let you in in the first place. | If the Circle vouches for you, I'm sworn to respect their judgment. But that doesn't mean I like it. | What guidance could you offer me? | So I wouldn't have to answer stupid questions from idiots. | Are you looking to join? I wouldn't get your hopes up. The short answer is: we fight. The long answer is... eh, forget it. You're not worth my time. | I can. It might save your life.

Name: Ria
description: Ria, an Imperial warrior, is the newest member of the Companions before you join, and highly determined to fight and die honorably alongside them.
canonical_lines: Here to be a Companion? Well, they just let me in, so there's probably no more room. | I was the newest Companion, until you came along. I guess that's ok. Just means I can show you the ropes! | Oh, hey there. I killed a bear yesterday. Did you kill anything? | Good to have some fresh eyes up there at the top. I follow your lead, Harbinger. | I... I can only imagine Aela's anger. I'll make sure not to cross her for a while. | Why can't I train with Aela?

Name: Skjor
description: Skjor, a Nord warrior, is a prolific member of the Companions, the legendary group of warriors operating out of Whiterun. He is also a member of the Circle, a subgroup of Companions blessed with the gift from Hircine.
canonical_lines: One of the guards from Dragonsreach came to me, worried about a prisoner who's escaped. The Jarl has too much on his mind right now, we won't be troubling him with this. | Keep a sharp eye about you and maybe I'll teach you to fight some day. | Haven't seen your face before. I'm watching you. | I can't keep helping you with everything. | So you think you have what it takes? Huh. Lucky for you, I'm not the one who makes that decision. Talk to Kodlak. Who knows, maybe he's in a generous mood. | I've got a bad grip on my shield, could you take a look at it?

Name: Tilma the Haggard
description: Tilma the Haggard is an elderly citizen and the maid of Jorrvaskr, cleaning up for the Companions.
canonical_lines: I just try to keep out of the way when they start roughhousing. | Oh, I'm just a servant, dear. You want to talk to one of the Companions, I'm sure. | I've been tending to the warriors of Jorrvaskr for as long as I can remember. | I see everything, hear everything and know everything. | Oh, I'm a good keeper of secrets. Have to be, in this hall. | A good bunch of men and women, who make too much a mess, if you ask me.

Name: Torvar
description: Torvar, a Nord warrior, is the drunken misfit of the Companions of Jorrvaskr. Initially of a friendly disposition, his attitude towards you seems to sour as you rise through the ranks, but he still becomes a potential follower after the Companions questline is completed.
canonical_lines: What do you want from me, now? | I haven't seen you before. Have I? Have we met? I'm sorry if I've forgotten. | It means when I have troubles, I always have someone on my side. We have Shield-Siblings to keep our backs as dangerous as our fronts. I think. | We're the best damned fighters in Skyrim. At least, some of us are. | In charge of what? I'm in charge of me, and you're in charge of you. If you're looking to join up, Kodlak's the one to be talking to. | He did? That's... I'm surprised. I wonder who will replace him in the Circle?

Name: Vignar Gray-Mane
description: Vignar Gray-Mane is an aging Nord and former Companion, living in Jorrvaskr in Whiterun. He is the patron of Clan Gray-Mane, one of the two oldest families in Whiterun, the other being Clan Battle-Born.
canonical_lines: For thirty years, I served the Legion as a commander. Fought in the Great War. | The people saw fit to name me Jarl, and I don't intend to let them down. | This isn't over. You hear me you old fool! This isn't over! And you. A Stormcloak? I'd thought better of you. You'll all come to regret this day. | Spoken like a true Nord. I'll stand beside you, Dragonborn. Now, what's this nonsense about trapping a dragon in my palace? | Vignar. We will do whatever I decide is in the best interests of Skyrim. Are we clear? | I admit, I really didn't expect these talks to succeed. Getting the Empire to agree to anything at all is quite an achievement.

Name: Vilkas
description: Vilkas is a Nord warrior and a werewolf, usually found within Jorrvaskr in Whiterun. He is a member of the Circle, a faction within the Companions, alongside his twin brother Farkas.
canonical_lines: I think by now I've killed one of every living thing in Skyrim. May be time for a trip to Morrowind. | There are always good reasons to fight. I just wish this war had them. Who cares who worships what dead god? Give me something to make me draw my sword. | What brings you to me? Find some strange creature in your travels? | I suppose you have another question? | I don't know if you're capable, and I don't have time to find out. If Kodlak will vouch for you, I might have some work you could do. | I don't know what you're talking about. But if I were you, I'd hold my tongue.

Name: Olava the Feeble
description: Olava the Feeble is an elderly citizen and a seer who lives in her house behind Breezehome in Whiterun's Plains District. She has a dark secret and will provide you with a very special reading, pointing you to an assassin of old.
canonical_lines: Maybe some other time I'll reveal your destiny, hmm? Tea leaves, palm reading, crystal scrying... Oooh! Maybe trepanning? Ha ha ha ha. | Come to Olava for a reading, did you? Sorry, pup, but I'm not quite in the mood. | Oh, I tell the future, all right. When I feel up to the task, that is. But I'm afraid my energies haven't quite peaked yet today. | Token, you say? Let me see. Oh, goodness me. You're a friend of Gabriella's, then. Well, I guess we both know why you're here. | No... no, that is all. Now... now please. I find myself very weary all of sudden. | By Sithis, but you are a persistent one. Haven't I told you enough?

Name: Gloth
description: Gloth is a Nord farmer who works for Severio Pelagia at Pelagia Farm outside Whiterun.

Name: Nimriel
description: Nimriel is a Wood Elf farmer who works and lives at Pelagia Farm, which is owned by Severio Pelagia. Her co-worker Gloth also lives there.
canonical_lines: I own the Pelagia Farm, just outside the city walls. Nimriel takes good care of things for me.

Name: Severio Pelagia
description: Severio Pelagia is an Imperial farmer who owns Pelagia Farm just outside Whiterun. He will pay you for any cabbages and potatoes you bring him.
canonical_lines: I had my years of war and fighting. Senseless, all of it. All I want now is a rake and a patch of good soil. | There's a nip in the air. Cold won't be good for my crops. Never is. | I own the Pelagia Farm, just outside the city walls. Nimriel takes good care of things for me. | You should stop by my farm if you get a chance. See what life's really about. | You should buy some produce from Carlotta's stall. Most of it comes from my farm.

Name: Acolyte Jenssen
description: Acolyte Jenssen is a Nord priest at the Temple of Kynareth in Whiterun.
canonical_lines: How are you feeling? | Feeling any better? | How is the pain? Any better? | Has your fever cooled? | Patience. The body needs time to mend. | More rest will do you good.

Name: Danica Pure-Spring
description: Danica Pure-Spring is a Nord priestess of Kynareth living in Whiterun where she tends to the sick and wounded inside the Temple of Kynareth. She is the master trainer in Restoration, but will only offer training after you've completed her quest The Blessings of Nature.
canonical_lines: Lately it seems I am more nurse than priestess, tending to those who have suffered, both in mind and body, from this terrible war. | It was taken by a lightning strike. Wish I had time to deal with it, but it's hard enough with all these wounded from the war. | Oh, it's Nazeem. He's been spending more and more time fawning over the Jarl. I swear, we're hardly ever home at the same time anymore. | But it's more than that. Nazeem is obsessed with this civil war, as is all of Skyrim. I swear, it will consume us all. | Maybe Nazeem's obsession has merit. This civil war seems so big, so unceasing... I'm afraid, Danica. Afraid for us all. | These are trying times for us all, I know. Many come to the Temple of Kynareth to pray, and I assist them as best I'm able.

Name: Maurice Jondrelle
description: Maurice Jondrelle is a Breton monk who shows up in the Temple of Kynareth in Whiterun during the quest The Blessings of Nature. If Maurice overhears you talking with Danica Pure-Spring about going to the Eldergleam, he will ask to accompany you.
canonical_lines: It was taken by a lightning strike. Wish I had time to deal with it, but it's hard enough with all these wounded from the war. | What is it... what has happened to the Gildergreen? I have traveled long here to worship beneath its branches. | I really don't have time to deal with you right now. Please just let me get back to my work. | Was I correct in hearing that you were traveling to the grove of the Eldergleam? | I am a traveler. A pilgrim. I follow the voice of Kynareth wherever it can be heard. I've dreamed of seeing Eldergleam for years. Might I travel alongside you? I promise not to get in the way. | I understand. Some journeys must be taken alone. I'll be here if you change your mind.

Name: Uthgerd the Unbroken
description: Uthgerd the Unbroken is a Nord warrior who lives in Whiterun. Talking to her about her attitude will annoy her and result in an option to challenge her to a brawl for a 100 septim bet.
canonical_lines: Thought did cross my mind, yeah. War brewing, talk of a riot in Helgen, and now jesters? Skyrim's gone mad, girl. You need to be careful out there. | Now that's what I call a punch. You got me. | You're no liar. Best fight I've had in years. There you are. You ever need another blade at your side, just ask. Wouldn't mind seeing how you handle a few trolls. | Want to hear a little Nord wisdom? You don't really know a woman 'til you've had a strong drink and a fistfight with her. | Keep walkin', softgut. I'm more woman than you can handle. | I got no quarrel with you, but I won't turn one down, neither.

Name: Adrianne Avenicci
description: Adrianne Avenicci is an Imperial blacksmith who runs Whiterun's armory Warmaiden's with her husband Ulfberth War-Bear. She is the daughter of Proventus Avenicci, the steward for Jarl Balgruuf the Greater.
canonical_lines: You think all the council my father provides the Jarl is his alone? Hmph. Let's just say I advise the advisor, and leave it at that. | Have you met my father? He was the steward, up at Dragonsreach when Balgruuf was Jarl. | Well, not a great deal, no. But I do less business than I used to. The Stormcloaks don't like buying from anyone that isn't a Nord. If I wasn't married to Ulfberth, I'd be out of business. | Ha! I'd sooner bend my knee to Ulfric Stormcloak. Besides, Gray-Mane would never make steel for the Legion. | Have you met my father? He's the steward, up at Dragonsreach. | I don't claim to be the best blacksmith in Whiterun. Eorlund Gray-Mane's got that honor. Man's steel is legendary. All I ask is a fair chance.

Name: Ulfberth War-Bear
description: Ulfberth War-Bear is a Nord blacksmith living in Whiterun where he runs the armory Warmaiden's with his wife, Adrianne Avenicci.
canonical_lines: Welcome to Warmaiden's. Now you, my girl, have definitely come to the right place. | We'll have to have a pint together sometime, you and me. | Everything you see here was made with skill and care by my wife Adrianne. | Welcome to Warmaiden's. Now don't let the name worry you - we got plenty o' steel for fightin' men. | We've got small weapons as well as big. Maces, daggers, that sort of thing. | You look like maybe you need a new weapon. Something big, perhaps? An axe maybe? Or a greatsword?

Name: Bjorlam
description: Bjorlam is a Nord warrior, and the Whiterun carriage driver. He can be found near the Whiterun Stables, and will take you to any of the hold capitals on his carriage, for a fee.

Name: Jervar
description: Jervar is a Nord warrior who lives at Whiterun Stables. He works for his father, Skulvar, who owns the stables.
canonical_lines: Need a horse? Talk to my father, Skulvar. | Old lady Maiden-Loom owns the stables. But my father and me, we run things. | So I mostly sweep and shovel. You got a problem with that? | Father says I ain't smart enough to be stablemaster. Some day. | My father never thought I was smart enough to be a stablemaster. I just wish he was around to see me now. | Back to work, I guess. There's never a shortage of dung to shovel around here.

Name: Lillith Maiden-Loom
description: Lillith Maiden-Loom is an elderly citizen of Whiterun who owns Whiterun Stables. She is the employer of Skulvar Sable-Hilt and lets him take care of selling the horses, while she merely lives in the stable house.
canonical_lines: My friend Maven and I - have you met Maven, in Riften? - we know our place in this world. Know the place of others, too. | Do you like horses? You should visit the stables. I own them, you see. And our stock is excellent. | Looking for a horse? I own these stables, but Skulvar can help you with a purchase. | Funny you should ask. I'm in the market for a new locket.
