Something in polished silver, maybe. Big enough to hold, say, a folded-up letter? | Perfect, my dear. Absolutely perfect. I'll see you then. | Everyone this city is so on edge. I could use some good news, Skulvar.

Name: Skulvar Sable-Hilt
description: Skulvar Sable-Hilt, a Nord warrior, is the hostler who runs the stables outside the city of Whiterun. He lives there with his son Jervar and will sell you a horse for 1000 gold.
canonical_lines: Thought did cross my mind, yeah. War brewing, talk of a riot in Helgen, and now jesters? Skyrim's gone mad, girl. You need to be careful out there. | Got the fleetest steeds in all o' Skyrim. | Gal/Man like you needs a warhorse, I reckon. | You know I did. | Yes, father, I did. And I watered her, and got fresh feed, and refitted the old saddle, just like you asked. Is there anything else? | I tell you? Saw that wild mare again yesterday, just outside the walls.

Name: Ysolda
description: Ysolda is a Nord citizen who lives in Whiterun. She is one of the few citizens who trusts the Khajiit traders traveling across Skyrim, and her greatest dream is to buy The Bannered Mare from Hulda.
canonical_lines: Matter of fact, I do. They've been fair enough with me, far as I can tell. And a Nord knows a liar when she sees one. | I spend a lot of time at the market stalls so I can learn the merchant's trade. I need more experience if I'm going to run an inn someday. | They say there's nothing a Nord woman can't do if she puts her mind to it. | We Nords are strong, and we're as tough as stone. But I'll bet you didn't know there's some of us with a good head for business, too. | There's some company I certainly prefer over others. Have you met Mikael? The most amazing bard I've ever heard. | Goodbye. And if you happen to see Mikael, tell him I was asking about him...

Name: Aventus Aretino
description: Aventus Aretino is a Nord child located in the Aretino Residence in Windhelm. He escaped from Honorhall Orphanage in Riften, where he ended up after his mother, Naalia Aretino, died from sickness during the winter, and he now seeks revenge on the owner of the orphanage, Grelod the Kind, for mistreatment of orphans.
canonical_lines: Sweet Mother, sweet Mother, send your child unto me, for the sins of the unworthy must be baptized in blood and fear. | Please... How long must I do this? I keep praying, Night Mother. Why won't you answer me? | It worked! I knew you'd come, I just knew it! I did the Black Sacrament, over and over. With the body and the... the things. And then you came! An assassin from the Dark Brotherhood. | Of course you are! I prayed, and you came, and now you'll accept my contract. | It took so long. So very long. But now that you're here, you can accept my contract. | You don't have to say anything. There's no need. You're here, so I know you'll accept my contract.

Name: Neetrenaza
description: Neetrenaza is an Argonian scout and dock worker who lives in the Argonian Assemblage, outside of Windhelm. He maintains a very bitter attitude about the Nords that he works for.
canonical_lines: The Nords don't appreciate us, but so what? I don't appreciate them right back. | The docks can be a dangerous place. Don't expect me to save you if you fall in. | Look, we don't have much love for your kind down here. Probably best if you just left. | Haven't seen you here before, egg brother/sister. Welcome!

Name: Scouts-Many-Marshes
description: Scouts-Many-Marshes is an Argonian employed as a dock worker in Windhelm. He and his clansmen reside within the Argonian Assemblage and can be seen working around the Windhelm docks.
canonical_lines: I came here as a hatchling. The only thing colder than Skyrim is the Nords themselves, but we stick together. | I think that's just their way. The Nords don't like anybody who's not a Nord, but they're not bad people, deep down. | Look around you. Don't you know what their little war is about? They want all non-Nords out of Skyrim. That means you and me. | Shatter-Shield, East Empire Company... we'll work for any ship that pulls in, as long as they can pay. | I wish the Nords, Argonians and dark elves in Windhelm got along better. | I hope we will speak again soon, marsh-friend.

Name: Shahvee
description: Shahvee is an Argonian citizen living in the Argonian Assemblage outside Windhelm. She is the only female Argonian in the game that can be married but only if you bring back her amulet.
canonical_lines: Skyrim isn't very friendly for Argonians, so we mostly just keep out of the way. | I enjoyed our talk. | Windhelm has been hard on my people. But our fortunes will turn, and we have shelter and food. | Bless you, friend. You have renewed my hope. Before I came into Zenithar's calling, I confess I was a thief. But I learned a few things in my time that might help you survive. Let me show you. | I hear the work is hard, down at the docks. | Shahvee knows the dock doesn't look nice, but fresh air is good for the gills.

Name: Stands-In-Shallows
description: Stands-In-Shallows is an Argonian rogue who works on the docks of Windhelm. He lives in the Argonian Assemblage with the rest of the local Argonian population.
canonical_lines: Huh? What's that now? Leave me alone. | I would not complain if you returned with some Skooma. | Trust me, marsh-friend, you do not want to live in this city. | By the Hist, if I don't get something soon, I don't know how I'll get through the week. | Thank you. I'll be waiting for that bottle. | Thank you so much. Here. Let me show you a thing or two about not attracting attention to yourself.

Name: Aval Atheron
description: Aval Atheron is a Dark Elf pawnbroker and street vendor in Windhelm. He is the only one in the Atheron family who owns a business; his brother Faryl works for a local Nord farmer and his sister Suvaris works for Torbjorn Shatter-Shield at the Clan Shatter-Shield Office.
canonical_lines: I'm not looking for special treatment, Aval. All I did was talk to Ulfric. For all I know, he's already forgotten the conversation. I wouldn't get your hopes up. | I'll be sure to stress your urgency. It seems like every farmer in Skyrim is short on supplies these days. | Come and browse my fine selection! Everything's fresh and delicious! | I've a little of everything for sale, come and see for yourself! | Wherever I can find them. If you're the type who worries over where something came from, you may want to move on. | You need anything, come see me. I'm as resourceful as I am discreet.

Name: Faryl Atheron
description: Faryl Atheron is a Dark Elf farmer who lives with his brother Aval and his sister Suvaris at the Atheron Residence in the Gray Quarter of Windhelm. He is employed as a farmhand by Bolfrida Brandy-Mug at her farm just outside the city gates.
canonical_lines: Bolfrida is certainly grating, but she pays well. Thankfully nobody from the city can see me out here. | Farm work is hard work. It's good to relax when I can, but I still have to listen to my harping about "injustices." | Of course working for Bolfrida isn't my first choice. But what else can a Dunmer do out here? | My sister and I both work for the Nords. Our brother is ashamed of us, but at least we can to eat. | I don't think we're going to get those potatoes to come in. | I'll bet this is better than trying to grow something in Morrowind, though, isn't it?

Name: Suvaris Atheron
description: Suvaris Atheron is a Dark Elf citizen who resides in Windhelm, in the segregated Gray Quarter. She has two brothers, Faryl and Aval, with whom she shares the quarters of the eponymous Atheron Residence.
canonical_lines: Looking for shipping? We'll always offer better service than the East Empire Company. | Torbjorn wants the shipping operations to bring in cash, and I make it happen. | Hey, maybe the reason these gray-skins don't help in the war is because they're Imperial spies! | Imperial spies? You can't be serious! | What do you want from me? I work for them ok? We're not friends. They pay me, and I get the job done. That's it. | Don't you ever find it demeaning, working for that Nord family?

Name: Belyn Hlaalu
description: Belyn Hlaalu is a Dark Elf farmer living in his house in Windhelm. He is also the owner of Hlaalu Farm, a small wheat farm just outside the city walls that he runs with his employee, Adisla.
canonical_lines: I'm proud of my farm. It takes no small effort to grow crops in this part of Skyrim. | I was wondering if you had ordered the shipment of that frostwheat, sir. | Yes, but that fool Endario can't get it in. I'll thrash him when I get back to the city, don't you worry. | I wasn't wanting to get anyone in trouble, sir. Just wanting to get the new planting started, is all. | Make sure the back fence is repaired. I swear I saw some skeevers in nibbling the leaves yesterday. | You just need to remember that there's a war on, and firewood isn't cheap these days.

Name: Hermir Strong-Heart
description: Hermir Strong-Heart is a Nord blacksmith who works at the outdoor forge in Windhelm. She works alongside Oengul War-Anvil, who she admires.
canonical_lines: I never expected Ulfric to live up to his legend, but he does. | Working in Windhelm is great. I get to see Ulfric up close. | I just can't believe that Ulfric is gone. Skyrim will never be free now. | Can't believe Ulfric's gone. If not for Hermir, I'd have left the city by now. | Every Nord should admire Ulfric. He fights for all of us. In fact, he's the reason I chose to learn the smithing trade. I want to make weapons and armor for the great Stormcloak army. | I couldn't ask for a better teacher than Oengul.

Name: Oengul War-Anvil
description: Oengul War-Anvil is a Nord blacksmith living in Windhelm. He can be found at the forge outside the Blacksmith Quarters, next to Windhelm's marketplace on the west side of the city.
canonical_lines: With my swords in their hands, Ulfric's men will send those legion dogs running. | I don't see these troubles as business. This is a labor of honor. To bring Ulfric his rightful glory, I would hammer a thousand shields. Shor's bones, I might just have to! | Can't believe Ulfric's gone. If not for Hermir, I'd have left the city by now. | Look at that edge. Still sharp. I'll present this to the Jarl later. As for you, let me show you some smithing techniques. | Everyone says Eorlund Gray-Mane is the best smith in Skyrim. I plan to change a few minds about that. | Every bit as sharp as what you'll find in Whiterun. I respect Eorlund, but he has the good fortune to work the Skyforge. Something about the fires... their steel just holds tighter.

Name: Bolfrida Brandy-Mug
description: Bolfrida Brandy-Mug is a Nord farmer who lives and works at Brandy-Mug Farm, a short distance east of Windhelm Stables. She lives with a goat and two chickens.
canonical_lines: The weather's harsh, but I like to think my sunny personality is what helps the grain grow. | I don't think we're going to get those potatoes to come in. | I'll bet this is better than trying to grow something in Morrowind, though, isn't it? | Firstly, I've never even seen Morrowind. Secondly, in volcanic soil the plants practically grow themselves. | Oh, my, I hadn't thought of that. | Now, I've been reading about the best ways to grow corn in permafrost...

Name: Brunwulf Free-Winter
description: Brunwulf Free-Winter is a Nord warrior who lives on the east side of Windhelm. He is known for standing up for the Dunmer and Argonians in the city and thus openly goes against the opinions of his fellow Nords Rolff Stone-Fist and Angrenor Once-Honored.
canonical_lines: I'll speak to Ulfric soon, but I make no promises that I can change his mind. | They call me a war hero. I'm no hero, I'm just a soldier who didn't want to die. | And you're right, at least as far as I'm concerned. Don't let Ulfric or some of these other short-sighted Nords bother you. Most of us are happy to welcome newcomers. | Being a Jarl isn't something I ever wanted, but if I can help these people, then I will. | The dark elves and Argonians won't soon forget Ulfric's prejudice, but I'm doing what I can to repair that rift. | I'm not looking for special treatment, Aval. All I did was talk to Ulfric. For all I know, he's already forgotten the conversation. I wouldn't get your hopes up.

Name: Calixto Corrium
description: Calixto Corrium is an Imperial rogue living the life of leisure in Windhelm. He used to go adventuring with his sister Lucilla until she passed away.
canonical_lines: I don't care much for the war one way or the other. | If you've got some time, stop by my House of Curiosities. I promise you'll see wonders! | My sister and I once traveled the world to assemble our collection. Such adventures we had! | It bothers me that fewer people come to Windhelm these days. | Welcome to the House of Curiosities! I offer a brief tour for a few coins, or you can simply browse at your leisure. | Indeed I did! For a few septims, I'll tell you about some of the most interesting curiosities in my collection.

Name: Adonato Leotelli
description: Adonato Leotelli is an Imperial bard and author, known for his books Olaf and the Dragon and Ghosts in the Storm. He is staying at Candlehearth Hall in Windhelm.
canonical_lines: I'm a writer by trade. Not that the people of Skyrim do much reading. | You're sure? When you see , ask for a tip. I'm sure he'll be in a good mood after reading this. | I still can't believe Isabella's gone. She was such a helpful young lass. | Know any tales of nobility and courage? I'm keen to record them. | I'll find some way to finish my book. | As you travel Skyrim, make sure you stop and take in the view from time to time!

Name: Captain Lonely-Gale
description: Captain Lonely-Gale is a Nord warrior living in Windhelm. He's a retired sailor, and a pretty well-known face around Windhelm.
canonical_lines: I made my fortune as a sea captain, but now I'm retired. | The townsfolk call me "captain" because I used to be a sailor. | I lost my family years ago. Truth is, I don't really know what keeps me going. | Did Viola send you to bother me? | Captain Lonely-Gale, it's always a pleasure to see you. In fact, I'm glad you're here. There's something I'd like to ask you. | A pleasure to see you as well, Niranye. What can I do for you?

Name: Elda Early-Dawn
description: Elda Early-Dawn is the Nord proprietor of Candlehearth Hall in Windhelm. She will rent out a room to you for 10 gold a night and offers merchant services.
canonical_lines: Don't bother. I don't have much chance of seeing my husband these days. He's always up in the palace with Ulfric. | I know, dear. He's a busy man, though, and loyal to the Stormcloaks. | Tell me about the Great War. I hear you were in the battle of... | Magic? Goodness, not here! Nothing dangerous like that! That's all kept to that College up in Winterhold. Better that than dealing with Ulfric's wizard like some do. | Eh. I try not to trouble myself with stories of the outside world. Now if someone brings me a dragon scale, that's a different story. | Ah, I can't reveal all my secrets to you. But rest assured, I would know.

Name: Luaffyn
description: Luaffyn, a Dark Elf rogue, is a bard who can be found in Candlehearth Hall in Windhelm.
canonical_lines: I'm a bard, trained at the Bards College in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying.

Name: Nils
description: Nils is an elderly citizen working at Candlehearth Hall in Windhelm as a cook and caretaker. He takes over running Candlehearth Hall if anything happens to Elda Early-Dawn.
canonical_lines: When I'm cutting wood, I pretend there's an Imperial soldier's head on the stump. | Name's Nils, I'm the cook up at Candlehearth Hall. | Nils is a hard worker, but I wouldn't trust him to run things. | Can't just throw the big logs on the fire. Got to have the little bits, too.

Name: Rolff Stone-Fist
description: Rolff Stone-Fist is a Nord warrior living in Windhelm who spends much of his day in Candlehearth Hall. He is very hateful towards Dark Elves and Argonians, and he is somewhat convinced that the Dark Elves are Imperial Legion spies or even allied with the Thalmor.
canonical_lines: Hey, maybe the reason these gray-skins don't help in the war is because they're Imperial spies! | Imperial spies? You can't be serious! | Don't like it? Too bad. This is our city. Ours! Don't think I can take you? One hundred septims says I can punch you back where you came from. | Get out of my face. | Every night, I walk around the Gray Quarter and let them gray-skins know what I think of 'em. | Only thing I hate worse than those scale-backs are them filthy gray-skins!

Name: Stenvar
description: Stenvar is a Nord warrior and a mercenary living in Windhelm. He can usually be found on the second floor of Candlehearth Hall.
canonical_lines: Well, look who it is. Been keeping out of trouble much? I hope not! | If you need some skulls crushed, I'm still available for hire. | Patience isn't exactly my strong suit, but alright. | You're letting me go? What's wrong, is my skill with a blade making you look bad? Hah! I'm kidding, friend. Until we meet again! | I'm bored. What do you say we go smash a few heads in? This one's on me. | Good, I can use the exercise. Haha!

Name: Susanna the Wicked
description: Susanna the Wicked is a Nord rogue who is usually found in Candlehearth Hall, in Windhelm. She is the fourth of the Butcher's victims during the quest Blood on the Ice.
canonical_lines: You didn't hear it from me, but if you want a real drink, try the Cornerclub in the Gray Quarter. | See you around, my beauty. | I guess I been complaining a lot about it, cause Susanna was yelling at me. Nils, she says, if your chaurus pie is so good, you should write down the recipe. | Served me a drink just a few nights ago | Did you know that candle on the mantle hasn't gone out in over a hundred years? | How can Elda hate the dark elves when she knows so little about them?

Name: Adelaisa Vendicci
description: Adelaisa Vendicci is an Imperial citizen who works for the East Empire Company in Solitude. She appears during Rise in the East, when she visits Windhelm's East Empire Company office to check on its condition.
canonical_lines: Adelaisa, I'm sorry. | I've got a top man/woman on the job right now. He/She should be returning any moment. | Haldyn? I've heard of the man, but never guess he'd join up with some pirates. This could be even more dangerous than I thought. Head to the ships. We'll plan the assault on the way. | Let me know. We shouldn't stay here all day. My men want their mead. | I appreciate everything you've done for us, but I really am quite busy. | This must be him/her, then.

Name: Orthus Endario
description: Orthus Endario is an Imperial citizen who works for the East Empire Company in Windhelm. He doesn't try to hide the fact that the office looks terrible but blames it on the pirates who have been plundering the Company's ships.
canonical_lines: 'Windhelm's an easy posting!' they said. Guess they didn't count on Ulfric's little uprising. | If you want to help, I suppose you could head to Windhelm and talk to Orthus Endario. He's in charge over there. | I know this place looks horrible -- please don't hold it against the Company. | A very powerful family in Windhelm. They run a private shipping operation, and would love to have the docks to themselves. Wouldn't surprise me if they were in league with the pirates, somehow. | Our own security forces are limited, and all the mercenaries are tied up in this ridiculous war. We don't even know their base of operations, and attacking single ships won't really solve the problem. | If you get me the logbook, I'll have proof of Shatter-Shield's treachery.

Name: Calder
description: Calder is a Nord warrior from Windhelm.

Name: Helgird
description: Helgird is an elderly priestess working in Windhelm's Hall of the Dead. During the quest Blood on the Ice, her extensive knowledge on dead bodies will prove helpful when you investigate a series of killings in town.
canonical_lines: Mighty Arkay, I pray that you will shepherd these souls as they pass into the realm of death. | Lord of Seasons, I pray to you now that you might watch over the dead with mercy and kindness. | Hear my prayer, son of Akatosh, and grant peace to these departed souls. | Well, she's dead. But I guess that's not unusual, at least not for somebody in here. I mean, someone who's not me, that is. Sorry, was only joking with you. | I wouldn't hold out too much hope. | I'm too busy tending to the dead to spend my time making more of them. And I wouldn't very well tell you about the cuts if I had made them, now would I?

Name: Adisla
description: Adisla is an aging Nord farmer working the land for Belyn Hlaalu at his farm east of Windhelm.
canonical_lines: Starting to get too old to work all day, but hopefully Master Hlaalu will let me slow down a bit. | Every now and then someone from the city comes by. Think they aren't used to seeing a Nord working for a Dark Elf. | Better to work in the cold than in the heat, I say. Though some sun would be nice. | I was wondering if you had ordered the shipment of that frostwheat, sir. | Yes, but that fool Endario can't get it in. I'll thrash him when I get back to the city, don't you worry. | I wasn't wanting to get anyone in trouble, sir. Just wanting to get the new planting started, is all.

Name: Tulvur
description: Tulvur is a Nord farmer living at Hollyfrost Farm east of Windhelm. He lives there alone with two dogs, Tiber and Ysgramor.
canonical_lines: Master Cruel-Sea comes and works the fields sometimes. I don't think the Mistress likes it, but she won't come out here to catch him. | I wonder if I could put the dogs to work somehow. But I don't really have the heart to force them. | Once the sun goes down, it's just me and the dogs. They keep me warm, and don't talk too much. | I'm not building a ship, sir. I'm trying to make things grow. | Sir, I'm going to need some better equipment if you want me plowing the frost. | I'll provide you wood and timber to make whatever riggings you'll need.

Name: Tiber
description: Tiber is one of two dogs who live at Hollyfrost Farm, the other being Ysgramor.

Name: Ysgramor
description: Ysgramor, "the harbinger of us all", was an Atmoran who came to Tamriel before recorded history as a refugee fleeing civil war in Atmora. He is generally regarded as the first human ruler, and king, of Skyrim.
canonical_lines: The three await your word to loose their fury upon the perilous foe. | Do not fail Kodlak. He's earned his place here, and does not deserve to fall prey to Alduin's insatiable hunger.

Name: Grimvar Cruel-Sea
description: Grimvar Cruel-Sea is a Nord child living in the House of Clan Cruel-Sea in Windhelm. He is the son of Hillevi and Torsten Cruel-Sea.
canonical_lines: Do you know my father? He's really important. He even talks to the Jarl sometimes. | Did you know my father? He was really important. He even talked to the Jarl sometimes. | Fine. Then I'll invite him out to play. He lives right there. I'm going to knock on his door... | Ha! Then I'm right. I knew it. He's trying to have somebody killed! | All right. I won't deny it, child. What you heard is true. But Aventus Aretino walks a dark path. His actions can lead only to ruin. | Now. Enough. We will speak no more of this. I am the only friend you need.

Name: Hillevi Cruel-Sea
description: Hillevi Cruel-Sea is a Nord farmer who sells food from her stall in Windhelm's marketplace. If you ask her whether she grows her own crops, she mentions that she also grows herbs, reminding her that she has some Nightshade she wants delivered to Wuunferth the Unliving.
canonical_lines: What is it Ulfric's always saying... "On my honor as a Nord, I swear it. (Laughter)." | You know, I'm not sure. My husband's been tending to the vines, so I'll have to ask him. | Just promise me when you get some more in, I'll have the first crack at them. | I wanted to thank you again for the mince pie you sent over. It was delicious. | It was my pleasure, Torbjorn. I enjoy cooking, and after what you and Tova have been through, it was the least I could do. | I... I'm so sorry, Torbjorn. You must be suffering terribly. I wish there was some way I could help.

Name: Idesa Sadri
description: Idesa Sadri is a Dark Elf citizen living in the House of Clan Cruel-Sea in Windhelm and is the sister of Revyn Sadri. She assists in the upbringing of Hillevi and Torsten Cruel-Sea's son, Grimvar Cruel-Sea.
canonical_lines: Fine. Then I'll invite him out to play. He lives right there. I'm going to knock on his door... | Ha! Then I'm right. I knew it. He's trying to have somebody killed! | I try to keep Grimvar safe, but these are dangerous times. | When Grimvar asks me why women are being killed around the city. I don't even know what to say to him. | I like to bring Grimvar down here sometimes. It reminds him how lucky he is. | Then it's true, what everyone is saying? That Aventus Aretino is doing the Black Sacrament? Trying to summon the Dark Brotherhood?

Name: Torsten Cruel-Sea
description: Torsten Cruel-Sea is a Nord farmer working and living in Windhelm. He is the patriarch of important clan Cruel-Sea, he also owns the large farm east of the city, Hollyfrost Farm, where Tulvur works alongside his two dogs, Tiber and Ysgramor.
canonical_lines: I'll be sure to stress your urgency. It seems like every farmer in Skyrim is short on supplies these days. | A navy, my lord. We could stop the influx of Imperial troops to Solitude, and choke their northern lines. | What is it Ulfric's always saying... "On my honor as a Nord, I swear it. (Laughter)." | If I make enough money from the farm, I'll buy myself a longship to sail the rivers of Skyrim. | I own Hollyfrost Farm outside the city. It's honest work, if a bit dull. | The Cruel-Seas have been sailors for generations. In truth, I regret not following in the footsteps of my ancestors.

Name: Nilsine Shatter-Shield
description: Nilsine Shatter-Shield is a Nord citizen living in Windhelm. She lives with her family, her mother Tova Shatter-Shield, and her father Torbjorn Shatter-Shield, at the House of Clan Shatter-Shield.
canonical_lines: What is it Ulfric's always saying... "On my honor as a Nord, I swear it." (Laughter). | I lost my twin sister a while back. Have you ever lost anyone close? | Muiri? I can't believe my family trusted that backstabbing little strumpet. You tell her she's dead to us. You hear me? Dead! | What kind of cruel, horrible person are you? My sister was murdered. Do you have any idea what that's like? What I'm going through? | You just going to stand there, gawking? Go away. You're starting to... frighten me. | You know, I'm not sure. My husband's been tending to the vines, so I'll have to ask him.

Name: Torbjorn Shatter-Shield
description: Torbjorn Shatter-Shield is a Nord warrior living in Windhelm with his family, the Shatter-Shield clan. He is the owner of the Clan Shatter-Shield Office, which is involved in local trade affairs.
canonical_lines: Those boots aren't worth the septims I do pay them. I'm not giving them coin I could give to good, Nord workers. | Seeing a good tavern brawl might be just the thing to lift my spirits. | I'd appreciate if you don't bother my wife, Tova. She's still in mourning. | Both my daughters. Now my wife? How much is one man supposed to take? | Brond's one of the most savage fighters I've ever seen... | Please forgive my mood. I'm still coming to terms with my daughter's death.

Name: Tova Shatter-Shield
description: Tova Shatter-Shield is a Nord citizen living in Windhelm. She lives at the House of Clan Shatter-Shield, with the rest of her family.
canonical_lines: I've been a bit out of sorts since... since our daughter was taken from us. | Not really sure what to do with myself anymore. I just miss her so much. | I try not to think of her, but sometimes the simplest thing will remind me. | Friga Shatter-Shield's old place? It's been abandoned ever since she was killed. I think her mother, Tova, has the key. | Her mother would have had the key, but she... that family is cursed, I tell you. | Hjerim? Well, I'm not sure what you think you'll find there, but you're welcome to have a look.

Name: Ambarys Rendar
description: Ambarys Rendar is the Dark Elf innkeeper at the New Gnisis Cornerclub in Windhelm. A true supporter of the Empire, Ambarys flies several Imperial banners on the second floor of the Cornerclub, and keeps a set of Imperial armor.
canonical_lines: It's not the cold of Skyrim that gets to me. It's the stink from the people. | Apparently some Nord women were murdered. Not sure why I should care. | I think that's just their way. The Nords don't like anybody who's not a Nord, but they're not bad people, deep down. | Look around you. Don't you know what their little war is about? They want all non-Nords out of Skyrim. That means you and me. | Oh splendid, another Nord. | When I've made enough money, I'm going to return to Morrowind in high style.

Name: Brelas
description: Brelas is a Wood Elf pawnbroker met during Elenwen's party at the Thalmor Embassy during Diplomatic Immunity. If you use Erikur as a distraction option, he will attempt to seduce Brelas.
canonical_lines: Um, thank you. Sir. Was there anything else I can do for you? | I hope you're enjoying yourself, sir./ma'am | I'm sorry, but I think you've have enough for the moment. | Hello, my dear. I got your message. Where shall we go? | What? I'm sorry, sir, but you must have misunderstood. I'm not permitted to leave the party. | Oh, I don't think so. I think I understand perfectly. Don't worry, my dear, I will make any necessary excuses to your employer.

Name: Malborn
description: Malborn is a Wood Elf servant who becomes a crucial informant for the Blades. He is initially found in Solitude during the Diplomatic Immunity quest, offering to smuggle your equipment into the Thalmor Embassy, where he will return for your subsequent infiltration.
canonical_lines: Now the Thalmor will be hunting me for the rest of my life. I hope it was worth it. | Trying to stay ahead of the Thalmor assassins. I'm not a hero like you. I don't stand a chance. | Trying to stay alive. I think time's running out though. I'm pretty sure the Thalmor know where I am. Maybe you can help me. You know, by way of making amends for getting me into this mess. | That's... great news. I'd... I'd better go. Right now. Before they find me again. This is my chance. Thank you! Here... I stole a lot from the Thalmor over the years. You may as well have some of it. | You're asking me? She promised that she was sending someone who knew what they were doing... If you actually want to get out alive, I'd bring whatever you need to move quietly, and kill quickly. | I'll give you a few more minutes. I can't wait here all day. It's my neck on the line, remember.

Name: Malthyr Elenil
description: Malthyr Elenil is a Dark Elf pawnbroker who lives in the Gray Quarter of Windhelm. He can usually be found in the New Gnisis Cornerclub, where he works for Ambarys Rendar.
canonical_lines: When Ulfric was killed, justice was done. That might sound harsh, but it's how I feel. | I'll speak to Ulfric soon, but I make no promises that I can change his mind. | You're a war hero, Brunwulf. Ulfric will listen to you. | I thought Gray Quarter would be a haven for my kind. I was wrong. | The cornerclub's been filling up at night lately. I'll bet Elda up at Candlehearth is jealous. | Not a day passes that I don't wonder if life would be better in Morrowind.

Name: Niranye
description: Niranye is a High Elf thief living in Windhelm. She has a stall in the market where she buys and sells goods of all kinds.
canonical_lines: I used to fence for the Thieves Guild in Skyrim a long time ago. When Linwe moved into the area, he contacted me and said if I didn't fence for them, he'd kill me. | If I tell you, you need to promise not to kill me. I'd prove to be quite an asset to the Thieves Guild... I'm one of the best fences in Skyrim. | I doubt Ulfric would let Thalmor into Windhelm without a fight. But please, do continue. How would you help us? | I'd travel more, but who wants to deal with all the soldiers these days? | Come see me in the marketplace tomorrow. You won't find better prices in all of Eastmarch. | I don't haggle. If you want a lower price, look elsewhere.

Name: Dalan Merchad
description: Dalan Merchad is a Breton warrior who works at the Windhelm docks. He serves as a sailor aboard the ship The North Wind, under the ship's captain, Kjar.
canonical_lines: I'm a lot happier out here on the deck than I am wandering around in that ruin of a city. | I heard those Forsworn out west live in caves. Can't be any worse than trying to live in Windhelm. | Can't wait until we raise anchor. Anytime the boat's not moving, I start to get impatient. | Never much cared for Windhelm. The place is depressing.

Name: Kjar
description: Kjar, a Nord ranger, is a boat captain commonly found on his ship, The North Wind, docked at the Windhelm docks.
canonical_lines: Sorry, but I'm not looking to hire any more crew right now. | You don't know what freedom really means until you've lived on the river. | Been on the river since I could walk. Wouldn't trade it for the world. | While you're on my boat, you follow my rules. First rule is, don't touch anything that doesn't belong to you. | Right. See you around. | Aye, 'til next time.

Name: Voldsea Giryon
description: Voldsea Giryon is a Dark Elf rogue and a sailor of The North Wind, serving under captain Kjar. Since the ship is currently laying over in Windhelm, she takes the opportunity to walk around the city as much as she can.
canonical_lines: I'm a sailor on the . We're laying over in Windhelm for awhile. | A life on the river makes for weak legs, so I do a lot of walking when we dock. | It's good to spend some time on solid land. | Captain Kjar expects the best, but he's a fair man. He never fails to reward hard work and loyalty.

Name: Gjalund Salt-Sage (Skyrim)
description: Gjalund Salt-Sage is the Nord captain of the Northern Maiden, and offers passage to Solstheim. Sogrlaf and Lygrleid are his crewmen.
canonical_lines: Look, the East Empire Company didn't give me a choice. They've raised their prices again, and there's nothing I can do about it. | I'll give you a fair price. Not many people headed over there these days. If you ask me, I can't blame them. Things aren't what they used to be out there. | Suit yourself. I'll be here if you change your mind. | We sail as soon as I'm paid. | Well... I guess nothing bad happened the last time. Fine, we'll sail as soon as I get paid. | I've been sailing the Northern Maiden across the Sea of Ghosts for nearly twenty years now. Might be time to find a new home port, though, the way things are going around here.

Name: Lygrleid (Skyrim)
description: Lygrleid is a Nord crewman aboard the Northern Maiden. He wears farm clothes with a pair of boots.
canonical_lines: Captain Gjalund does the business around here... I'm just a ship's mate. | The Northern Maiden is ship-shape and ready to go. | Talk to Captain Gjalund if you're looking to book passage. | If you're looking for passage, talk to Captain Gjalund.

Name: Sogrlaf (Skyrim)
description: Sogrlaf is a Nord crewman aboard the Northern Maiden. He can be found wandering at the ship all day.
canonical_lines: Captain Gjalund does the business around here... I'm just a ship's mate. | The Northern Maiden is ship-shape and ready to go. | Talk to Captain Gjalund if you're looking to book passage. | If you're looking for passage, talk to Captain Gjalund.

Name: Angrenor Once-Honored
description: Angrenor Once-Honored is a Nord beggar who was a former member of the Stormcloaks. Upon raising his disposition by giving him a coin, he will become a potential candidate for marriage.
canonical_lines: I was one of the best soldiers in the Stormcloak army, until I took a sword through the chest. | I once killed six men, single-handed, while rescuing my brothers-in-arms from ambush by an Imperial patrol. | I'd be out there fighting the Imperials right now if it wasn't for my war-wound. | Ever since that Imperial craven ran me through from behind, I've had trouble breathing. Don't have any trouble drinking though. | I'm not too proud to admit that I need a bit of help from time to time. | Hey, maybe the reason these gray-skins don't help in the war is because they're Imperial spies!

Name: Gort
description: Gort is a Nord warrior who works as a ferryman in Windhelm. He wears a set of farm clothes with a pair of boots and defends himself with an iron war axe.
canonical_lines: I can take you to any port on the coast. | May not look like much, but my boat can get you there faster than walking. | You just enjoy the fresh air and leave the rowing to me. | Ah, Solitude. Best port in Skyrim. You should try the Spiced Mead while you're there. | I'll take you as far as I can, but I'm not hanging around and it's gonna cost you extra. | You think I do this for my health? Come back when you've got the coin.

Name: J'datharr
description: J'datharr is a Khajiit assassin working for the Thalmor who can be found just outside of Windhelm at the Khajiit caravan campsite during the quest Find the Thalmor Assassin.
canonical_lines: Why struggle? The Thalmor will only send more like me. | Malborn? Ah... very good. I think we understand each other. | The Thalmor never forget. | You'll have a traitor's death. | Move on, friend. I have nothing to sell today. | Indeed. That would be best.

Name: Silda the Unseen
description: Silda the Unseen is a Nord thief living as a beggar in Windhelm. She is an expert trainer for the Pickpocket skill.
canonical_lines: A few septims for my supper is all I ask. | I ask only for a little charity. | I ain't askin' for much, just a few septims. | I hope the wind dies down a bit before tonight. Hard when you've got to sleep outside. | Up to no good, are we? I'll teach you, but it'll cost you. | I heard a scream and came running, but she was already... like this... when I got here.

Name: Sofie
description: Sofie is a Nord child who wanders around the streets of Windhelm selling flowers from her flower basket. Starting her day at 8am, she sells her flowers for nine hours until 5pm.
canonical_lines: Not much. But I hope you like them! | Some pretty flowers. I picked them all by myself! | Oh, I... okay. Sorry. | I'm... I'm so cold... | No one bought any flowers again today. I... I don't know what to do. | Thanks... Thanks for talking to me.

Name: Galmar Stone-Fist
description: Galmar Stone-Fist is a fierce, veteran Nord warrior who is the housecarl of Ulfric Stormcloak and the general of the Stormcloak uprising. In his younger years, Galmar fought for the Imperial Legion at the frontlines of the Great War, along with rebellion leader Ulfric Stormcloak and Rikke, the latter now a Legate in the Legion and the right hand of Galmar and Ulfric's archenemy, General Tullius.
canonical_lines: ...But first, tell me. Why's an Elf want to fight for Skyrim? | ...But first, tell me. Why's a cat want to fight for Skyrim? | ...But first, tell me. Why's a lizard want to fight for Skyrim? | ...But first, tell me. Why's an Orc want to fight for Skyrim? | ...But first, tell me. Why's a Redguard want to fight for Skyrim? | ...But first, tell me. Why's a Breton want to fight for Skyrim?

Name: Jorleif
description: Jorleif is a Nord citizen employed as a steward to Jarl Ulfric Stormcloak of Windhelm. He resides in the Palace of the Kings.
canonical_lines: I'm not much of a strategist, but Lord Ulfric listens to my counsel all the same. | Serving as Steward to Ulfric Stormcloak was a great honor, but I don't miss his temper. | I don't care that we lost, I'm just glad the war is over. | To be sure, my lord, I am not skilled in the arts of war or military tactics. | Blasted dark elves. I don't suppose you could tell them that I presently have larger concerns? Such as all of Skyrim? | If you have any news of the western holds, take it to Ulfric immediately.

Name: Sifnar Ironkettle
description: Sifnar Ironkettle is an elderly servant found in Windhelm in the Palace of the Kings.
canonical_lines: I do the cookin' and cleanin' round the palace. | Don't need to pay me no mind. I'm the least important person in here. | Keepin' the palace clean an' the lords fed ain't easy work, but it's honest work.

Name: Ulfric Stormcloak
description: Ulfric Stormcloak of the Stormcloak Clan is the rebellious Jarl of Windhelm who started the uprising against Imperial rule known as the Stormcloak Rebellion. An incredibly divisive and controversial figure, Ulfric leads the fight against the Empire in a bid for free Talos worship and political independence in Skyrim.
canonical_lines: I killed Torygg to prove our wretched condition. How is the High King supposed to be the defender of Skyrim, if he can't even defend himself? | Only the foolish or the courageous approach a Jarl without summons... do I know you? | Fine. I'll send the Unblooded here with you. Fancy a crawl through a moldering dungeon to see if you can't stir up Galmar's Jagged Crown? | The blood of our fallen heroes will make Skyrim free, never doubt it. Also, never doubt me or Galmar. Now then... | Damn it. What were they doing there? Imperial spies are everywhere. Never forget that... I trust you gave them a thrashing? | I'm glad you're here. I have a message I need delivered to the Jarl of Whiterun. Deliver this axe to Balgruuf the Greater.

Name: Windhelm Guard
description: Windhelm Guards are guards who act as the military and police force of Windhelm. These guards serve under Jarl Ulfric Stormcloak.
canonical_lines: Look, there's a war going on, if you hadn't heard. We barely have enough soldiers to walk the streets, much less patrol every corner. | We're stretched thin as it is with the war. Nobody has the time to spend on this. Not pleasant, but it's the truth. | If you want to help, ask some of those gawkers if they saw anything useful. I'm going to examine the body before the rats can get to it. | Friga Shatter-Shield's old place? It's been abandoned ever since she was killed. I think her mother, Tova, has the key. | Indeed. I... I thought you captured the Butcher. You said the city was safe, now. How could this have happened? | I've never seen anything like it. I would take this to Calixto at the House of Curiosities. He has a good eye for strange trinkets. Might even give you a bit of gold for it.

Name: Wuunferth the Unliving
description: Wuunferth the Unliving is an elderly sorcerer from Windhelm, in the service of Ulfric Stormcloak. He is a common trainer in Destruction and, like all court wizards, sells magic items.
canonical_lines: No, but then I don't have much need for Ulfric. We both leave well enough alone. | Whatever you've heard I can do is probably true. | If Ulfric needs a favor, he has it. Anyone else better pay well for the service. | I'd explain my research, but you'd just be confused. | Yes, I'm a powerful wizard. No, I won't put on a magic show for you. | Ah yes, the extract, good. Poisonous, of course, but it has its uses. I suppose you want something for your trouble? Here.

Name: Yrsarald Thrice-Pierced
description: Yrsarald Thrice-Pierced is a Nord Stormcloak commander found at the Palace of the Kings in Windhelm. He is removed from the game if the Empire seizes Windhelm, with Legate Hrollod taking his place in the palace.
canonical_lines: Good. You've got a real fire in you. I like that... Talos guide you. | I see. Well, make it quick, or do you have better things to do than fight for the people of Skyrim? | This won't be easy. But that's why I'm sending you. You got the grit to get this done? | That's what I wanted to hear. Good luck out there. | You've done well. | Excellent. Put the fear of the Nine into those motherless dogs.

Name: Revyn Sadri
description: Revyn Sadri is a Dark Elf warrior living in the city of Windhelm, in the Gray Quarter. He owns and runs a small pawn shop, Sadri's Used Wares, which also serves as his home.
canonical_lines: Can't say I mind the sight of all the Imperial guards. It's a change for the better. | All my goods are legitimate, which is more than I can say for some. | I like to share my good fortune with those who deserve it. | Make yourself at home, please. Let me know if I can be of any assistance. | I'm always happy to see a customer, but I won't deal in stolen goods. That's trouble I don't need. | You're telling me.

Name: Jora
description: Jora, a Nord, is a priestess of Talos at the Temple of Talos in Windhelm. She runs the temple with her husband Lortheim.
canonical_lines: I can't believe the Empire would turn its back on Talos. | The Stormcloaks are gone, but Talos will never be forgotten. I don't care what the Thalmor say! | I doubt Ulfric would let Thalmor into Windhelm without a fight. But please, do continue. How would you help us? | Don't bother. I don't have much chance of seeing my husband these days. He's always up in the palace with Ulfric. | I know, dear. He's a busy man, though, and loyal to the Stormcloaks. | To receive the blessing of Talos, you have only to pray at the altar.

Name: Lortheim
description: Lortheim is a Nord priest who lives and works in Windhelm at the Temple of Talos with his wife, Jora.
canonical_lines: I've spent my life serving Talos. I don't plan to stop now, no matter what the Empire says. | The way I see it, Ulfric's fighting a holy war. | Talos created the Empire. You'd think they'd show him more respect. | Someday all of Skyrim will worship Talos openly, as we do here. | May Talos shield you from all harm. | May Talos bless your days.

Name: Viola Giordano
description: Viola Giordano is an Imperial citizen who has a house in Windhelm. She pines after Captain Lonely-Gale, but her feelings towards him are unrequited, as he spurns her advances every time she asks.
canonical_lines: They say they're too busy with the war -- I say what good is winning a war if we're still terrorized by one of our own? | After what happened to those other women, I'm worried about my own safety. | Women murdered time and again, and all the guards care about is the war. | I... well. Perhaps. But still! It's curious. | Captain Lonely-Gale! I've been looking for you everywhere! | And now you've found me. Can I help you?

Name: Nurelion
description: Nurelion is an aging High Elf mage who runs The White Phial in Windhelm, an alchemy shop named after a legendary artifact he's spent most of his life trying to find. Now that he's finally learned of its location, a recent illness is preventing him from getting it, granting you the opportunity to retrieve the White Phial for him.
canonical_lines: Alchemy is simple. Unless, of course, you are simple. Then I can't help you. | Must you bother me now? I've almost figured out where it is. | I don't appreciate loiterers, if that's what you're here to do. | I hope to see you again soon. | A wise choice, my friend. After all, what would a common adventurer know about the complexities of finance? | If you change your mind, you know where to find me.

Name: Quintus Navale
description: Quintus Navale is an Imperial sorcerer and the assistant of the alchemist Nurelion. He lives in The White Phial store.
canonical_lines: My master can be a bit short-tempered, but I've learned so much from him. | I'll never have Master Nurelion's skill with potions. | I have a decent mind for potions, but I've also learned how to craft magical items. | I understand what potions do, but I don't always understand how they do it. | Are you kidding? I came all the way from Cyrodiil just to work with him. He's... he's not the best teacher. But just watching him, I've learned so much. | It doesn't matter what I believe. Nurelion does, and it might be the only thing keeping him alive right now.

Name: Alfarinn
description: Alfarinn is a Nord warrior, and the Windhelm carriage driver.

Name: Arivanya
description: Arivanya is a High Elf bard who is married to Ulundil and lives with him at the Windhelm Stables. She takes over selling the stables' horses if anything happens to her husband, even though she constantly tells everyone how much she dislikes horses.
canonical_lines: No! Someone help me! Please! | Ugh. I'll need to bathe thoroughly to get this stench off of me. | I love my husband, but he can be a bit... dull at times. | Horses. All day long. Do you have any idea how dull horses are? | If the guards would listen to Viola, they'd have caught the Butcher by now.

Name: Ulundil
description: Ulundil is an exceptionally cheerful Altmer ranger who runs the Windhelm Stables outside of Windhelm. He will sell you a bay horse with white markings for 1000 gold.
canonical_lines: I see why the Nords like Skyrim. This crisp air is wonderful for the lungs! | Oh, pardon me. Was I in your way? | I hope someday we'll get to know each other better. | You seem like such a nice person. I'd love to hear more about you. | My pa used to say there ain't no use worryin' about the things you can't change. If it's my fate to be ended by a dragon, so be it. Meantime, I'll live my life the way I want to. | That's your best offer? I suppose so. She's the one with the saddle.

Name: Birna
description: Birna is a Nord pawnbroker who runs Birna's Oddments in Winterhold and feels depressed about how little custom the shop has; her only regular customer is the housecarl, Thaena, who isn't the most friendly person. Her brother, Ranmir, has a tendency to drink away their earnings.
canonical_lines: Yeah, it was stupid of me. I shouldn't have believed the story and just refused the trade. But I didn't, and now I'm stuck with this worthless junk. | Fine, here you go. You get killed on account of this thing, it's not my fault. Got it? But, I suppose, if it does actually turn out to be worth something, let me know, will you? | I do what's necessary so we can get by. Have to, since I can't count on my brother for anything. | Ranmir has no problem drinking himself under the table, so I have to be the one to make ends meet. | Things are easier now that I don't have to worry about Ranmir anymore. | I still can't believe you were able to help Ranmir pull himself together.

Name: Ranmir
description: Ranmir is a Nord citizen of Winterhold. He lives with his sister Birna in Birna's Oddments.
canonical_lines: Unless you're bringin' me another round, you can just keep walking. | I'm just trying to have a quiet drink here, all right? | You ask me, not enough of Winterhold fell off into the sea. | Empire's in charge now, huh? Only a matter of time before we start getting bothered by them Thalmoricars... err, Justimors... Y'know, them elves. | Who are you to say what I should do? I'll pay her back when I'm ready. | And I told you I'd pay it when I'm good and ready.

Name: Ancano
description: Ancano is a High Elf elemental mage and an agent of the Thalmor. He resides at the College of Winterhold, where he serves as an "advisor" to the Arch-Mage.
canonical_lines: I am an advisor to the Arch-Mage. The Thalmor wish to promote relations with your College. I am at the Arch-Mage's disposal if he requires advice. | I know he's from the Thalmor, and that he claims to be here simply as an advisor. I also know that no one really believes that. I've been trying to avoid him, honestly. | I have many important things on my mind. Your concerns are not among them. | I'm disappointed to say I've learned little during my time here. | I had hoped your scholars would be on a level comparable with my own colleagues. They are not. | Now that stability has been established between the Dominion and the Empire, we seek only to help Skyrim. To guide its people through this time of transition, to help lead them to a better future.

Name: Gelebros
description: Gelebros, a Wood Elf sorcerer, is a member of the Psijic Order who arrives at the College of Winterhold along with Quaranir and Tandil to secure The Eye of Magnus.
canonical_lines: Our order will not forget your deeds.

Name: Quaranir
description: Quaranir, a High Elf sorcerer, is a member of the Psijic Order. Quaranir is encountered a few times in the College of Winterhold questline.
canonical_lines: Please do not be alarmed. I mean you no harm. | I'd simply like to talk to you. | I merely wish to talk to you. | I'm afraid it's not that simple. | I'm sorry, I'm afraid I don't understand. | There's been a misunderstanding. Clearly I should not be here. I shall simply take my leave.

Name: Savos Aren
description: Savos Aren is a Dark Elf conjurer and the current Arch-Mage of the College of Winterhold. Always buried in work and his own thoughts, Savos has entrusted Master Wizard Mirabelle Ervine with most of his daily duties, a decision that has left a lot of the lesser-ranked College members quite puzzled and frustrated.
canonical_lines: Not often, no. Some risks must be taken, to be sure. I am simply trying to avoid untimely deaths. We also must make an effort to avoid worsening what Skyrim thinks of us. | I am an advisor to the Arch-Mage. The Thalmor wish to promote relations with your College. I am at the Arch-Mage's disposal if he requires advice. | It's no secret that the College's reputation in Skyrim is... tainted. | Someday the College may be fully accepted by the Nords. I hope that my work as Arch-Mage may help. | I've seen documents dating back to the late Second Era. Beyond that, it's hard to say. Events in the last several hundred years resulted in a significant loss of our history, I'm afraid. | Please don't tell me that another one of the apprentices has been incinerated. I have enough to deal with right now.

Name: Tandil
description: Tandil, a High Elf sorcerer, is a member of the Psijic Order who arrives at the College of Winterhold along with Quaranir and Gelebros to secure The Eye of Magnus.
canonical_lines: It is time for us to go.

Name: Urag gro-Shub
description: Urag gro-Shub is an Orc mage and the librarian of The Arcanaeum at the College of Winterhold. He is a Wizard of the College of Winterhold as well.
canonical_lines: I don't care if you wrote it yourself -- if you want a book from here, you go through me. | The Arcanaeum is always accepting new volumes. I'll take what I can get. | I don't want to see you treating any of these books poorly. Are we clear? | You need a book, you talk to me. Otherwise you're going to find yourself in a lot of pain. | May I help you, Arch-Mage? | I'm not one for commendations, but I might've lost everything that matters. So... thanks.

Name: Arniel Gane
description: Arniel Gane, a Breton conjurer, is one of the scholars at the College of Winterhold. He specializes in the art of Alteration although most of his time is spent on research entirely unrelated to the arcane arts, something he keeps a secret from everyone until you have completed Hitting the Books.
canonical_lines: I'm working on some very important research, I assure you. | The exact nature of my research is a secret, I'm afraid, but it really is quite valuable. | I don't think anyone here truly understands how useful my work will be. | When I've finished what I'm working on, everyone will understand its significance. | What? The Augur? Oh no, I'm quite sure that's... He's nothing I'm involved in. No, no. Mirabelle Ervine... Or Tolfdir. They're qualified to discuss him. Not me, definitely not. | I can't lose all my research. There has to be a way to save the College.

Name: Brelyna Maryon
description: Brelyna Maryon is a Dark Elf mystic and a student at the College of Winterhold. She is most likely to be met during Tolfdir's lecture.
canonical_lines: Really? That must be so nice. I came all the way to Skyrim just to get away from my family's expectations. | I'm trying to pay attention. | No, I suppose we haven't. Brelyna Maryon, of House Telvanni. First of my family to leave Morrowind in a long time. Now I'm here to study Conjuration. That's what's important. | I've just been asked enough questions. I'm here to learn, not to explain myself over and over. | Forgive me, I didn't mean to assume. | I think my parents reserved a spot for me here at the College on the day I was born.

Name: Enthir
description: Enthir is a Wood Elf sorcerer living on the second floor of the Hall of Attainment, in the College of Winterhold. When meeting him for the first time, he claims to be able to obtain "hard to find" items, and instructs you to meet him in his quarters to conduct business discreetly.
canonical_lines: If you ever need something, especially something hard to come by, let me know. | I've spent a long time building up a list of contacts for goods from all over Tamriel. | You assured me that you could find it, however. | Perhaps, perhaps. I'm not quite sure what I need. | Well, you know the drill. You don't see it here, it's gonna cost you extra for me to get it. | I'm fully aware of the terms of our... arrangement.

Name: J'zargo
description: J'zargo is a Khajiit sorcerer and an apprentice at the College of Winterhold. He is one of several apprentices encountered soon after joining the College and plays a minor role in some quests in the main College questline.
canonical_lines: I think, given the state of Skyrim, some might disagree. | Skyrim couldn't be more different from Elsweyr. But magic... Magic's the same no matter where you go. | Leave me alone. | So, as I was saying, the first thing to understand is that magic is, by its very nature, volatile and dangerous. Unless you can control it, it can and will destroy you. | Sir, I think we all understand that fairly well. We wouldn't be here if we couldn't control magic! | Please, please! This is exactly what I'm talking about. Eagerness must be tempered with caution, or else disaster is inevitable.

Name: Mirabelle Ervine
description: Mirabelle Ervine is a Breton elemental mage and serves as Master Wizard of the College of Winterhold, reporting directly to Arch-Mage Savos Aren. After first gaining entrance into the college through Faralda, Mirabelle will welcome you to the premises, provide you with your robes, and give you a tour.
canonical_lines: Wonderful. Please follow me, and don't wander off. The College of Winterhold has been a fixture in Skyrim for thousands of years. | I continue to hear things about dragon sightings in Skyrim. | Sir, the Imperial Legion has again requested assistance. What shall I tell them? | If there are any problems, let me know. It's my job to keep things running smoothly. | Day-to-day operations are my responsibility, which means if you become a problem, you get sent to me. | I understand you're the one that found this... Orb in Saarthal.

Name: Nirya
description: Nirya is a High Elf sorceress and a scholar at the College of Winterhold. She does not offer any training to you nor does she offer any kind of merchant services.
canonical_lines: Just like anywhere else, it's all about who you know. Lucky for you, you know me. | I think the College is due for a change in leadership. | May I just say, it's a pleasure to have you leading the College. Really. | I'm sure you've made note of who's been the most helpful during your time here, Arch-Mage. | If there's ever any way I can assist you, please let me know. | It's lies. All of it. I don't care what she told you, it's all lies.

Name: Onmund
description: Onmund is a Nord sorcerer enrolled at the College of Winterhold. He will most likely be encountered for the first time during a lecture given by Tolfdir for the newly enrolled apprentices.
canonical_lines: I know he's from the Thalmor, and that he claims to be here simply as an advisor. I also know that no one really believes that. I've been trying to avoid him, honestly. | It's good to see another Nord. I feared I'd be the only one. Almost doesn't feel like Skyrim, being so far away from the rest of the world here. | I'd hoped I wouldn't be the only Nord, but I should've known better. | I think, given the state of Skyrim, some might disagree. | Really? That must be so nice. I came all the way to Skyrim just to get away from my family's expectations. | The College is a bit of an oddity in Skyrim. Magic isn't something that's looked upon fondly here.

Name: Tolfdir
description: Tolfdir is an elderly mage who lives in the College of Winterhold. He is a professor and serves as the quest giver for the first few quests of that faction.
canonical_lines: Have you, by chance, seen my research notes? I seem to have misplaced them. | I am always available should you need me, Arch-Mage. | So, as I was saying, the first thing to understand is that magic is, by its very nature, volatile and dangerous. Unless you can control it, it can and will destroy you. | Sir, I think we all understand that fairly well. We wouldn't be here if we couldn't control magic! | Please, please! This is exactly what I'm talking about. Eagerness must be tempered with caution, or else disaster is inevitable. | Not going to weigh in either way? I admire your restraint, but there are times when you must make decisions, even without all the facts. Rely on instinct.

Name: Colette Marence
description: Colette Marence is a Breton monk and an instructor at the College of Winterhold. She is the expert trainer in Restoration, able to train you to level 75, and sells several Restoration spells.
canonical_lines: Oh no, I'm not talking about that. I'm not being blamed for saying something out of place. Again. If you want to know, ask Nelacar. He's staying at the Frozen Hearth. Knows the whole sad story. | Oh, goodness no. There are those far more skilled than I. | I... I see. Well, I'm glad we've established this early, rather than me hearing about it years or decades later when the whispers behind my back are loud enough. | Well... No. Not really. Not directly, I mean. But I know things are said behind my back. Conversations cease when I enter rooms. I'm quite aware of what's going on here, and I will not stand for it! | Why are my research materials constantly disappearing? | Once again, I have found very insulting notes left in my personal effects. Simply barbaric.

Name: Drevis Neloren
description: Drevis Neloren is a Dark Elf sorcerer and an instructor at the College of Winterhold. He is the master trainer for Illusion, able to train you to level 90.
canonical_lines: You... You can see me? | But you can hear me, obviously. Something still isn't quite right. At least I'm making progress. | Confound it all. I was quite sure I was invisible, or at least transparent. | If only I could be free of the mundane, day-to-day activities life so often requires. | Hmm? Oh, no. No, not really. I... Actually, I've forgotten what I was looking for. | Ah. Well, then. Let me know if you need anything.

Name: Faralda
description: Faralda is a High Elf sorceress living at the College of Winterhold. She is the guardian who initially stops prospective students and requires them to cast a spell as a demonstration of their abilities.
canonical_lines: No? Well, if you think you're capable of it, then I'd be happy to provide it to you for a mere 30 gold. Or you can try your luck with one of the court wizards around Skyrim. They also sell spells. | Certainly a few of my colleagues aren't quite as talented as they'd like to believe. | I'm sure when the time for promotions comes, they'll go to the most deserving mage. | Don't you think I'd make a wonderful Master Wizard? Maybe even Arch-Mage someday? | If you need any help with Destruction spells, please let me know. | Mirabelle does a fine job as Master Wizard, I suppose. Still, there's always room for improvement.

Name: Phinis Gestor
description: Phinis Gestor is a Breton conjurer at the College of Winterhold, and an expert-level trainer in Conjuration. When your conjuration skill reaches level 90, Phinis offers a quest that, when completed, rewards you with the Flame Thrall spell.
canonical_lines: The people of Skyrim have no appreciation for what we do here. Not that I care. | Should you ever fall victim to an unfortunate accident, I promise your remains will be put to good use... | There are few places where one can pursue my type of work without fear of persecution. | I care little for idle conversation, but perhaps you need help with Conjuration magic? | Nasty business with that Ancano fellow. Pity that I was unable to assist, but I was... otherwise occupied. | I'll teach you some of what I know, but be careful.

Name: Sergius Turrianus
description: Sergius Turrianus is an Imperial instructor at the College of Winterhold and author of the book A Primer on Enchanting. Sergius is the expert trainer in Enchanting, able to train you up to level 75.
canonical_lines: Enchanting services are one of the few things that keep us in touch with the rest of Skyrim. | I think we could blow up half of Eastmarch, and people would still come to us for enchantments. | Savos doesn't care for politics. He likes to believe the College is removed from the rest of Skyrim. | If he could, Savos would float entire College above Skyrim, for all the Nords to see. | I'm sure you can figure it out on your own. If you can't, perhaps Urag has a book you can read. | No. No I can't. I have enough to keep me busy. View it as a chance to hone your craft.

Name: Dagur
description: Dagur is the Nord innkeeper of The Frozen Hearth in Winterhold. Haran is his wife, and together they have a daughter, Eirid.
canonical_lines: Let me know if you need anything. We're here to provide. | If there's anything you need, just let me know. | Turns out who runs Winterhold doesn't change much. Can't say I'm surprised. | I won't complain about Korir being gone, but I don't expect things to really be any different. | Well, not exactly. Listen, we're friends, right? I wondered if I could ask a favor of you. | Well, I'm getting to that. Just let me explain.

Name: Eirid
description: Eirid is a Nord child living in Winterhold. Her parents are Haran and Dagur, and they run The Frozen Hearth inn.
canonical_lines: There's not many people left in Winterhold, so I only have Assur to play with, and sometimes he's mean. | He always wants to play 'Hunt the Elf', and he makes me be the elf. | Assur says his pa says the College made lots of bad things happen, but the mages I've seen look nice. | Assur and I are playing a game. Don't tell him where I am! | Assur had to move away because of the Empire. But we get to stay. | I understand Eirid's been playing 'Hunt the Elf' again.

Name: Haran
description: Haran is a Nord food vendor in Winterhold. She and her husband, Dagur, run the town's inn, The Frozen Hearth.
canonical_lines: Something I can get for you? | You'll wind up getting yourself killed. Dagur shouldn't have said a word about it. I love the man, bless his heart, but he doesn't know the whole story. | Enough to know that it's not worth it. Ranmir's a good boy, and he doesn't deserve what fate pushed his way, but there's only so far I'll go to risk my neck. | No there isn't. And I suggest you leave it at that. I don't want to see anyone get hurt over this. | I never knew the darkness held such colors, or the night air smelled so sweet. You have given me a great gift. | Please, make use of my coffin whenever the need arises.

Name: Nelacar
description: Nelacar is a High Elf sorcerer and former member of the College of Winterhold, currently living in the Frozen Hearth inn in Winterhold. He was asked to leave the college due to some failed experiments.
canonical_lines: My days at the college are long behind me, but I prefer to stay close by. | Dagur and I have an understanding. He gives me my privacy, and I make sure my experiments don't blow up his inn. | I don't deal with any College applicants these days, so don't bother asking. | I don't like doing business with Azura's faithful. Make it quick. | No. Gods no, not for years. I left Winterhold for some time, and returned to stay here at the inn. | I still have research that keeps me busy, and being here in Winterhold ensures I have access to former colleagues.

Name: Assur
description: Assur is a Nord child who lives in the jarl's longhouse in Winterhold with his parents Jarl Korir and Thaena. He is Korir's heir and will become the next jarl of Winterhold after Korir, unless Korir is deposed.
canonical_lines: Someday I'm gonna grow up and be the Jarl. | Did you know that most of Winterhold fell into the sea? I heard it's because of that College. | I heard the College teaches you how to kill people with magic. Is that true? | Pa says the Empire made us leave, but the Empire just does whatever the elves tell them to. | Eirid and I are playing Hunt the Elf. It's my job to find the Elf, and kill it dead. Have you seen it? | Fine, Elf-Lover. I'll find her myself.

Name: Kai Wet-Pommel
description: Kai Wet-Pommel is a ranking Nord soldier for the Stormcloaks. He can be initially found in the war room of the Jarl's Longhouse in Winterhold.

Name: Korir
description: Korir is the Nord Jarl of Winterhold who resides in the Jarl's Longhouse with his wife Thaena and their son Assur. He will generally be found there all day except around 7pm, when he takes his son to The Frozen Hearth for a few hours.
canonical_lines: It wasn't enough that the College still stands, now the damnable Empire has Winterhold. I hope they all freeze to death. | I don't care how many Colleges they build, or how much the sea swallows up. I'll outlast them all. | You know Winterhold used to be the seat of power in Skyrim? Now it's a shell. | The College is the worst thing that's ever happened to Winterhold, maybe to Skyrim. | Driven out of my own home. After all this, I can't believe it. | Then I have no work for you.

Name: Malur Seloth
description: Malur Seloth, a Dark Elf witchblade, is the steward of Winterhold. He can be found in the Jarl's Longhouse or on a bench in front of The Frozen Hearth.
canonical_lines: My job is to serve the Jarl, not waste time talking with travelers. | I don't really know anyone at the College. I guess everyone just assumes I do because I'm a Dunmer. | I'm trying to do as little as possible. You're getting in the way of that. | Thonjolf is convinced I've got some connections at the College, so he goes easy on me. | Malur, I need you to take care of something. | No, I don't think so. Not today.

Name: Thaena
description: Thaena is a Nord citizen of Winterhold. She is Jarl Korir's wife and housecarl, and the mother of Assur.
canonical_lines: I'd rather see the Sea of Ghosts swallow Winterhold entirely than have it run by the Empire. | Korir will be the first to tell you that if it weren't for that College, we'd all be better off. I agree with him. | Don't sass me, little girl. | I certainly hope so, otherwise I'm wasting my time. | Yeah, well I don't see anything. | I'm sure you're right.

Name: Winterhold Guard
description: Winterhold Guards are guards who act as the military and police force of Winterhold. These guards serve under Jarl Korir.

Name: Kraldar
description: Kraldar is a Nord citizen of Winterhold, who will replace Korir as Jarl if the Imperial Legion takes control of the city. Before becoming Jarl, he will simply stay in his house all day with his servant Thonjolf.
canonical_lines: With the Empire's help, Winterhold will be back on its feet in no time. | Winterhold was once the capital of Skyrim, you know. Quite an honor. | I'd like nothing more than to see Winterhold regain its former glory. | It's hard to recover from something like the Great Collapse, but I believe Winterhold can do it. | I have a very close relationship with Savos Aren, the current Arch-Mage. | Goodness, no! They're simply scholars, they mean no one harm. I've had... several conversations with Arch-Mage Aren over the years. He's perfectly polite, if a bit guarded.

Name: Thonjolf
description: Thonjolf is a Nord citizen residing in Winterhold. He lives with Kraldar in the latter's house, where he sleeps from 10pm to 6am, then wanders around the house all day.
canonical_lines: Kraldar's family was once very powerful in Winterhold. It's my honor to assist him even now. | I'm just the Housecarl. If you need something, you should speak to Kraldar. | I'm very busy, you know. I'm the only one Kraldar can rely on to assist him. | I'm not one to complain, but I'm not sure why Kraldar insists on keeping Malur Seloth around. | There is no news, I'm afraid. | I shall inform you the moment something noteworthy happens.

Name: Augur of Dunlain
description: The Augur of Dunlain was formerly a Breton mage who lived and worked in the College of Winterhold. According to Tolfdir and Colette Marence, he was an upstanding mage and advanced student, especially talented in Restoration.
canonical_lines: I am that which you have been seeking. Your efforts are in vain. It has already begun. But those who have sent you have not told you what they seek. What you seek. | Once again you have come seeking something. The circumstances differ. This time I have what you seek. Are you prepared? | There is no solace in knowing what is to come. | There is no help for you here. | Your perseverance will only lead you to disappointment. | Still you persist? Very well, you may enter.

Name: Velehk Sain
description: Velehk Sain is a Dremora pirate that is trapped in the Planes of Oblivion by a binding spell. He will only be encountered when nearing the completion of Forgotten Names, during which you will be able to summon him in The Midden Dark.
canonical_lines: Where am I? You! Out with it, then. | Ah, I was beginning to think the day would never come. So you broke the binding spell, did you? Matters little. Their souls are trapped in Oblivion all the same, and here I stay. | Where I belong is back aboard my ship, on the Abecean. You've no hope of defeating me, pup. Don't bother. Why not broker a bargain, instead? | Those kids are dead anyway. Killing me won't bring them back. I, on the other hand, have done quite a bit with my time on Mundus. Release me and I'll share a bit of my wealth with you. | Names have power, lad/lass. You should know by now. Just say 'Velehk Sain, I release you.', and 'Poof!', I'm on my way. | Have it your own way, meat.

Name: Azzada Lylvieve
description: Azzada Lylvieve is a Redguard farmer living in Dragon Bridge with his wife Michel, son Clinton, and daughter Julienne. He grew up an orphan in Markarth, but was taken in by an old Nord warrior who helped him escape the city.
canonical_lines: A Moth Priest? I'm sorry, but I don't know anything about that. Try asking one of the guards. | Got a loving wife, a healthy son and a patch of land to call my own. What more could a man ask for? | If it weren't for Michel, I'd probably be dead in a Markarth gutter by now. | Seen some mean-spirited folk pass through lately. Thought I was rid of those sorts when I left Markarth. | Oh dear. I wish there was something we could do to help him. | As do I. That man saved my life. I hope that someone helps him, as he once helped me.

Name: Clinton Lylvieve
description: Clinton Lylvieve is a Breton child living in Dragon Bridge. He is the son of Azzada and Michel, and brother of Julienne.
canonical_lines: Pa says I'm too young to fight in the war, but I can still help by patrolling the town. | Are you a soldier? One day, I'm going to be a soldier! | I wish I was a soldier! They get to travel and stay up late and have fun! | Have you met Lucky? He's my pet goat, and he helps me patrol the town. | Father, how come Olda's so mean to everyone? She's only ever happy when she's with Lodvar, and she's not even married to him! | That's no business of yours, son. Mind you keep your nose out of it.

Name: Julienne Lylvieve
description: Julienne Lylvieve is a Breton living in Dragon Bridge, where she works at the Four Shields Inn. She is the daughter of Azzada and Michel, and sister of Clinton.
canonical_lines: I'm hoping to run my own inn someday. | Faida's teaching me the innkeeper's trade. She runs the Four Shields. | Please stop looking at me like that. | First the war and now there's talk of dragons. What's next? | Miss Faida, may I ask you a question? | Since my customers are mostly local, I just knock on their front doors the next day.

Name: Michel Lylvieve
description: Michel Lylvieve is a Breton farmer living in Dragon Bridge with her husband Azzada, son Clinton, and daughter Julienne.
canonical_lines: You hear all this talk about a war? I hope it never comes here. | A Moth Priest? I'm sorry, but I don't know anything about that. Try asking one of the guards. | It's no easy thing to make a living in the wilds of Skyrim. | Oh dear. I wish there was something we could do to help him. | As do I. That man saved my life. I hope that someone helps him, as he once helped me. | If you're looking for a room, you want the Four Shields.

Name: Aldepius
description: Aldepius is an Imperial soldier found at Dragon Bridge. His status as a new recruit was questioned by a guard stationed with Aldepius, who questioned his older age and curiosity relating to who the bandit informant was.

Name: Faida
description: Faida is a Nord food vendor and the innkeeper of the Four Shields Tavern in the small village of Dragon Bridge. She stays behind the inn's counter all day.
canonical_lines: The meals I serve ain't what I'd call a Jarl's feast, but they'll fill your belly. | Well, this is it, then. Look after yourself, Gaius. You're doing your duty, and I'm proud of you. But you'd better come back to me. You hear? | Oh, Faida. I may travel alone, but you know I carry you always in my heart. I'll see you soon. | Faida's the name. I keep the inn. | Something different about you, I can tell. Hope you ain't here to cause trouble. | I think the dragons are here to test us, and make sure we're still true Nords.

Name: Varnius Junius
description: Varnius Junius is an Imperial farmer from Dragon Bridge. He is first found in Solitude's Blue Palace appealing to Jarl Elisif to investigate strange occurences in Wolfskull Cave.
canonical_lines: Then we will immediately send out a legion to scour the cave and secure the town. Haafingar's people will always be safe under my rule. | Th...thank you, my Jarl thank you. | Your eminence, my scrying has suggested nothing in the area. Dragon Bridge is under imperial control. This is likely superstitious nonsense. | I'm trying to speak to the Jarl. | I swear to you, unnatural magics are coming from that cave! There are strange noises and lights! We need someone to investigate! | Oh. Yes, of course you are right. Falk, tell Captain Aldis I said to assign a few extra soldiers to Dragon Bridge.

Name: Horgeir
description: Horgeir is a Nord lumberjack and the owner of the lumber mill in Dragon Bridge. He lives at his house with his wife, Olda, and his brother, Lodvar.
canonical_lines: You're a well-traveled sort, I can tell just looking at you. | You been to Markarth? They've got a tavern called the Silver-Blood Inn. Best ale this side of Skyrim. | Money's been tight since the mill burned down. My wife won't let me forget it. | They say a strong drink, a sharp axe and a good woman are all a Nord needs to be happy. Two out of three's not bad. | Damn right it has. Demand's never been so high, at least not since I built the new mill. | I must have forgotten. I'll take care of it tomorrow.

Name: Lodvar
description: Lodvar is a Nord lumberjack who lives in Dragon Bridge at Horgeir's House. He is the brother of Horgeir, who runs the mill.
canonical_lines: You want anything in this life, you gotta take it. Just not from me. | I'm thinking of going for a walk up the mountain later. Would you like to come? | Oh, that sounds nice.
