Sounds like a wretched place. | I hear there's baked slaughterfish tonight. | I'm lucky enough that none of my friends have been killed. Of course we have soldiers coming through all the time. | Sondas, I've heard about Morrowind.

Name: Tormir
description: Tormir is a Nord ranger and a resident of Darkwater Crossing who works at Goldenrock Mine. She is the mother of Hrefna.
canonical_lines: We keep hearing that the war could come through here. I try not to worry about it. | I don't want to hear another word about what you're learning from the elf! It's high time you learned to be a Nord, too! | I don't want to hear another word about what you're learning from the Argonian! It's high time you learned to be a Nord, too! | Is there something I can help you with? | I'm not proud of Hrefna's father. If she knew him, she wouldn't be either. | Well, I know she thinks the world of you.

Name: Annekke Crag-Jumper
description: Annekke Crag-Jumper is a Nord ranger and former adventurer who lives in Darkwater Crossing with her husband Verner Rock-Chucker. The couple has put their exploration days behind them, but while Verner is seemingly happy with the more relaxed lifestyle, Annekke has a hard time settling down and will ask you to do some adventuring for her during the related quest.
canonical_lines: This brings back memories. Come back when you're done and maybe I'll be up to showing you a few tricks. | You remind me of myself a few years ago. Let me show you a little bit about wearing light armor. It's helped me out whenever I was scouting. | Now I've got the itch to travel again. If you're leaving town, I wouldn't mind coming with for a while. | A traveler? I'll you've seen some sights. We should swap tales sometime. | Verner, you should have come out with me yesterday. I found the most amazing tree growing right out of a cave. | And I found a new deposit of amber. Would have been nice to have some help pulling it out.

Name: Verner Rock-Chucker
description: Verner Rock-Chucker, a Nord warrior, is a miner found in Darkwater Crossing. He was one of the original settlers of the mining camp along with his wife, Annekke Crag-Jumper, after she discovered Goldenrock Mine.
canonical_lines: You looking for work? We could use an extra hand in the mine. Just bring me any Corundum you dig up. | I do love my wife. Just wish she'd get her head out of the clouds. | Sometimes I feel like Annekke cares more about finding new rocks than being with me. | It's a shame our daughter couldn't make the journey herself, but at least she still thinks of us. If you wouldn't mind bringing this back to her for us, I'd appreciate it. | Verner, you should have come out with me yesterday. I found the most amazing tree growing right out of a cave. | And I found a new deposit of amber. Would have been nice to have some help pulling it out.

Name: Stormcloak Guard
description: Stormcloak Guards are guards that can be found in Markarth, Solitude, Morthal, Falkreath and Whiterun when the Stormcloaks take over their respective Hold and they act as the military and police force throughout that Hold.

Name: Agmaer
description: Agmaer is a Nord bandit initially found in Dayspring Canyon on his way to join the Dawnguard. He wants to become a vampire hunter but is too nervous to enter Fort Dawnguard alone and will instead accompany you to see Isran.
canonical_lines: I'm, uh... my name is Agmaer, sir. | Uh, crossbow? I've never... | I heard what's going on. The vampires, the Dawnguard, all of it. I wanted to help, so here I am. | That must be it. Fort Dawnguard... Wow. Bigger than I expected. Where is everybody? This place looks almost deserted. | I guess this is it. Wish me luck. | You go ahead. I'll talk to Isran in a minute.

Name: Beleval
description: Beleval is a Wood Elf bandit and a member of the Dawnguard, who can be found in Fort Dawnguard. Like most other recruits, she is available as a follower, and with Hearthfire installed, she is available as your personal steward.
canonical_lines: Not a lot of our kind here, I see. Suppose I shouldn't be surprised. Still, those of us here have as much of an obligation to protect Skyrim as any other, wouldn't you agree? | Seems that I'm the only of my kind here. Suppose I shouldn't be surprised. Still, it's my duty to help protect Skyrim as much as any Nord's, don't you think? | Isran is the only one trying to do something about these vampires. I don't much like him, but I can't allow the people of Skyrim to be preyed upon by those monsters.

Name: Celann
description: Celann is a Breton warrior and vampire hunter who can be found at Fort Dawnguard. Like Dawnguard leader Isran, Celann is a former Vigilant of Stendarr, but they both left due to dissatisfaction with the organization.
canonical_lines: Isran found something for you to do, eh? I thought he'd like you. Good luck! | Ugh. I thought this place was supposed to be secret? | New recruits? Hmm. | Here to join the Dawnguard? Good. | Isran will decide if you've got what it takes. Go on, he's right inside. | Go on inside. Isran'll want to talk to you.

Name: Durak
description: Durak is an Orc ranger and a vampire hunter. He is the first to approach you about the recent vampire menace and recruits you into the Dawnguard.
canonical_lines: Glad you decided to give it a try. I hope Isran likes you. | I lost two wives to vampires. I will avenge them. It is good to know that I will not have to do it alone. I am glad this Dawnguard exists. | Ugh. I thought this place was supposed to be secret? | You there. The Dawnguard is looking for anyone willing to fight against the growing vampire menace. What do you say? | We're vampire hunters. We search out and destroy those bloodsucking scum wherever we find them. | That's what everybody says, right up until they find their throat being ripped out by a pack of hungry vampires.

Name: Florentius Baenius
description: Florentius Baenius is an Imperial priest added by the Dawnguard add-on, who you recruit into the Dawnguard. He is a master trainer in Restoration, and will sell you a wide variety of Alchemy-related items.
canonical_lines: I suppose I could... what, praytell, is there? | Isran? My help? Is this... some kind of a joke? Did Arkay put you up to this? Isran's done nothing but mock me. He's never given me the respect I deserve. | I know! Arkay told me of your success! | What's that, Lord? Well, should I tell him/her? | Falmer, if I understand Arkay correctly. Nothing you can't handle. | Well done! I said you'd return victorious, Arkay wasn't so sure. Wield it bravely.

Name: Gunmar
description: Gunmar is a Nord blacksmith added by the Dawnguard add-on. He initially wears a set of scaled horn armor with matching pairs of bracers and boots.
canonical_lines: You there, hold fast! I've tracked this damned bear for two weeks; I'll not let it have any more victims. | Vampires? That... well, that might change things. Tell me more about what's going on. | By the Eight... All right, look. I'll consider it, but I can't just leave this bear to prey on more innocent people. Once it's dealt with, then perhaps I'll see what Isran expects of me. | For two weeks I've tracked that bear, and now find that you've killed it on your own. I'm not sure whether that makes you brave or foolish. What brings you here? | Of course he did. He's been working on that place for years now. Never lets anyone in. His own little fortress. Well, I guess I'll get to see what he's been up to all this time. I'll meet you there. | The trolls will serve us well, I think.

Name: Ingjard
description: Ingjard is a Nord warrior found in Fort Dawnguard. Like most other recruits, she is available as a follower, and with Hearthfire installed, she is available as your personal steward.
canonical_lines: I hear you're doing a lot of the work in tracking down these vampires. If there's anything I can do to help, let me know.

Name: Isran
description: Isran is a Redguard blacksmith, a former Vigilant of Stendarr, and leader of the Dawnguard. He is one of the primary quest givers for the Dawnguard and is also the person to see if you wish to join the organization.
canonical_lines: Some Imperial scholar arrived in Skyrim a few days ago. I was staking out the road when I saw him pass by. Maybe that's your Moth Priest. | This is Fort Dawnguard. Originally owned by the Jarl of Riften back in the Second Era, it was a bit of a black mark on the Hold's history. I'm trying to put it to good use. | Why are you here, Tolan? The Vigilants and I were finished long ago. | You know why I'm here, the Vigilants are under attack everywhere. The vampires are much more dangerous than we believed. | Yes, well... I never wanted any of this to happen. I tried to warn all of you... I am sorry, you know. | Got a fire in your belly to kill vampires, eh? Good for you. But look around - there's really not much to join yet. I've only just started rebuilding the order.

Name: Sorine Jurard
description: Sorine Jurard is a Breton thief added by the Dawnguard add-on. She is a former compatriot of Dawnguard leader Isran and he will ask you to find and recruit her during the quest A New Order.
canonical_lines: What would mudcrabs want with my satchel, anyway? | Just one gyro. One, and I can get back to work. Where are they? | Yes, that was my point. I felt it was rather clear. Look, what is it you want from me? | Vampires? Really? Oh, and I suppose now he remembers that I proposed no less than three different scenarios that involved vampires overrunning the population. Well, what are they up to? | I... Well, that's actually something I never would've anticipated. Interesting. I'm not sure what they would do with one, but in this case Isran is probably correct in thinking it isn't good. | In case you haven't noticed, Isran is always concerned about something. That's what got us working together in the first place. Still, if he sent you to find me, it may actually be pretty bad.

Name: Bran
description: Bran is one of two armored huskies living in Fort Dawnguard, along with Sceolang. Both of them can be found within a wooden pen in the cave section for Fort Dawnguard.

Name: Sceolang
description: Sceolang is one of two armored huskies living in Fort Dawnguard, along with Bran. Both of them can be found within a wooden pen in the cave section for Fort Dawnguard.

Name: Dexion Evicus
description: Dexion Evicus, an Imperial spellsword, is a Moth Priest. He came to Skyrim following rumors of an Elder Scroll being found.
canonical_lines: If I'd known Skyrim was so dangerous, I'd have brought more guards. | The more you fight me, the more you will suffer, mortal. | I will resist you, monster. I must! | Yes, I can feel your defenses crumbling. You want it to end. You want to give in to me. Now, acknowledge me as your master! | I serve my master's will. But my master is dead, and his enemies will pay! | Wait, stop! I yield! That... that wasn't me you were fighting. I could see through my eyes, but I could not control my actions. Thank you for breaking that foul vampire's hold over me.

Name: Serana
description: Serana is a Nord vampire found inside a sarcophagus in Dimhollow Cavern, where she is called Mysterious Woman until she shares her real name with you. Serana is the long lost daughter of Lord Harkon and Valerica, and is in possession of an Elder Scroll.
canonical_lines: Good question. Hard to say. I... I can't really tell. I feel like it was a long time. Who is Skyrim's High King? | I don't know that name. You say she's Jarl of Solitude? Who supports her? | Oh, wonderful. A war of succession. Good to know the world didn't get boring while I was gone. Who are the contenders? | Cyrodiil is the seat of an empire? I must have been gone longer than I thought. Definitely longer than we planned. Please, let's hurry. I need to get home so I can figure out what's happened. | Some Imperial scholar arrived in Skyrim a few days ago. I was staking out the road when I saw him pass by. Maybe that's your Moth Priest. | Wow. Look at this place. No one's been here in centuries. I doubt there's any other place like it in Skyrim. It's beautiful.

Name: Vigilant Tolan
description: Vigilant Tolan is a Nord member of the Vigil of Stendarr. After the destruction of the Hall of the Vigilant, he will come to Fort Dawnguard to seek protection.
canonical_lines: One thing about living in Skyrim I could never stomach... all the damn vampires! | Why are you here, Tolan? The Vigilants and I were finished long ago. | You know why I'm here, the Vigilants are under attack everywhere. The vampires are much more dangerous than we believed. | Yes, well... I never wanted any of this to happen. I tried to warn all of you... I am sorry, you know. | I'll meet you at Dimhollow. It's the least I can do to avenge my fallen comrades. | Tolan, I don't think that's a good idea. You Vigilants were never trained for...

Name: Dravynea the Stoneweaver
description: Dravynea the Stoneweaver is a Dark Elf elemental mage living in Kynesgrove. She is responsible for maintaining a low temperature in Steamscorch Mine so that the workers are able to continue mining.
canonical_lines: The mine's always on the verge of collapse. Good thing I'm around to keep it up. | Morrowind used to be a lovely place, but you couldn't pay me to live there these days. | Most people in Skyrim don't care for Dunmer. Roggi's the only one around here even half-decent. | I should get over to Windhelm more. See how the other Morrowind refugees are getting along. | Well I nearly lost my eyebrows to it. | All right, I'll take a look at it.

Name: Iddra
description: Iddra is a Nord citizen and an innkeeper at the Braidwood Inn in the town of Kynesgrove. She manages the inn, providing room rentals as well as merchant services, and from 8am to 8pm she stands behind the inn's counter to work.
canonical_lines: Magic is nothing but trouble. If you're set on it, there's that College in Winterhold or Ulfric's court wizard. But don't say I didn't warn you. | I hope you'll stay for a bit. We could certainly use the business. | Did you see my husband already? I'm sorry... he can come off a bit rough at times. | Oh, I wouldn't be able to help them. Their skills were given by the Divines. | Ah, but I can do that right here. Right now! | I'm going to need your help in the kitchen tomorrow.

Name: Kjeld
description: Kjeld is a Nord citizen and an innkeeper living in Kynesgrove. He runs the local Braidwood Inn with his wife Iddra.
canonical_lines: Welcome to Kynesgrove. If you want to do business here, you'll have to deal with me. | If it's malachite you're after, you've come to the right place. I'll pay for any you dig up out of Steamscorch Mine. | Roggi and I do the work around here. My wife and brats just sit around on their brains wasting time. | I'd like the fresh air better, but this isn't so bad. | I don't know. Maybe because it makes the mead taste that much better at night. | Well I nearly lost my eyebrows to it.

Name: Kjeld the Younger
description: Kjeld the Younger is a Nord warrior living at Braidwood Inn in Kynesgrove. His parents, Iddra and Kjeld, manage the inn, as well as nearby Steamscorch Mine.
canonical_lines: You don't look so tough. I'll bet I could take you. | I've never seen you before. Leave me alone! | My pa runs this town, and you're going to be in big trouble if you mess with me. | I'm going to need your help in the kitchen tomorrow. | When do I get to go hunting? | I can do that! I did it yesterday!

Name: Ganna Uriel
description: Ganna Uriel is an Imperial lumberjack and miner found in Kynesgrove and the Steamscorch Mine nearby. Ganna and her sister, Gemma Uriel, came to Kynesgrove to start a lumber mill but after the mill was finished they found out the trees were sacred, and they were not allowed to cut them.
canonical_lines: Did I tell you I got a letter from General Tullius? They've conscripted some more archers and will need... | Seen the remnants of the grove? That... well, that was me. Me and my sister. | But... well, they did. Sacred, they said. So we work the mines until we've worked it off. Fair enough, I guess. | I'm bitter because we had lives in Cyrodiil. | I'll let you explain that one to the Nords. | I'll never understand why you're so bitter about everything.

Name: Gemma Uriel
description: Gemma Uriel is an Imperial lumberjack turned miner working in Steamscorch Mine in Kynesgrove. She is Ganna Uriel's sister, and traveled from Cyrodiil to start a lumber mill in Kynesgrove; a subsequent failure, as the surrounding trees turned out to be sacred to Kyne.
canonical_lines: Did I tell you I got a letter from General Tullius? They've conscripted some more archers and will need... | I'll never understand why you're so bitter about everything. | Wouldn't even be here if it weren't for my idiot sister. | I can't believe she dragged me all the way to this snowball. I'd rather be broke in Cyrodiil than broke here. | If these Nords don't let up soon, I might just make a break for it. At least Cyrodiil is warmer than this. | I'm bitter because we had lives in Cyrodiil. And we came out here to be paupers.

Name: Klija Wolf-Claw
description: Klija Wolf-Claw is a Nord warrior from Bruma{{ref|name=BOC|}} who wishes to fight for the Stormcloaks as their champion.

Name: Roggi Knot-Beard
description: Roggi Knot-Beard is a Nord miner living in Kynesgrove, where he works in Steamscorch Mine. When not mining, he can be found inside Braidwood Inn or in the mining camp outside the inn.
canonical_lines: I guess you're right. A Nord shouldn't be so proud to turn down a kind soul's generosity. Tell Iddra I'll be fine. | A true Nord would settle it himself, but at least this way Iddra gets her coin. Thank you. Tell her I'll be fine. | I visited the meaderies of Riften once, as a lad. Wish I had their skill. | A lifetime of study, you might say. The best comes from Riften, but I haven't been there in years. Ever tried making your own? It's... not easy. Trust me. | I'd like the fresh air better, but this isn't so bad. | I don't know. Maybe because it makes the mead taste that much better at night.

Name: Pactur
description: Pactur is a Nord citizen who, with his wife Sorli the Builder, runs Rockwallow Mine in Stonehills for Bryling. He'll ask you to relay a message to Bryling on their behalf.
canonical_lines: Sorli and I are just here to make sure the mine is run proper. | Don't have to tell me twice. But then, I'm not the one that buried iron under these mountains. | I'll see to it that Sorli is safe now that she's running Morthal. | We left Sirgar in charge back at Stonehills. I hope that wasn't a mistake. | You're doing me quite a favor. I won't forget it. I'm sure she'll be angry. Brace yourself. | You could take word to her that there'll be a shipment soon. Buy us some time and keep her off our backs. In exchange, I'm willing to let a few ingots go missing. Maybe more than a few.

Name: Sirgar
description: Sirgar is a Nord citizen living in Stonehills. He is the son of Sorli the Builder and Pactur, the maintainers of Rockwallow Mine.
canonical_lines: Guess the Empire's going to need plenty of iron what with all the fighting. That'll be good for us, I suppose. | I hear there's been fighting in Skyrim. Rebellion, maybe. Wonder if our iron is being used for weapons. | Talk to my parents if you need something. | I'm in charge now that my parents are in Morthal. | I figured that I would run the mine someday. I just didn't figure that would mean now. | My parents keep a close eye on the miners. They can't be trusted too much.

Name: Sorli the Builder
description: Sorli the Builder is a Nord citizen residing in Stonehills with her husband Pactur, and son Sirgar. She and Pactur co-manage Rockwallow Mine—and also lead Stonehills itself—on behalf of the mine's proprietor, Bryling, who is one of the thanes in Solitude.
canonical_lines: Pactur and I, we make sure the miners aren't stealing, and that the ore makes it to Solitude. | We've got all the miners we need, so I hope you're not here looking for work. | Hjaalmarch isn't much, but it's a good start. I'll make a name for myself here, then maybe work my way up to someplace like Riften. | Finally, my leadership is being recognized. | Bear fat in the hearth fire, if you believe Hroggar. I do, but some folk here think he set the fire himself. | They think he did it to get rid of his wife so he could be with Alva. He moved in with her the day after the fire. Even I find that suspicious.

Name: Gestur Rockbreaker
description: Gestur Rockbreaker is a Nord miner working in Rockwallow Mine in Stonehills. He wears a belted tunic and a pair of boots, and defends himself with an iron dagger.
canonical_lines: If you're here about the mine -- it's already got an owner, and she's not selling. If not, then please stay out of my workers' way. | Bryling owns this mine. I wouldn't have much to live for if she hadn't offered me a job. | I spend every waking moment making sure the mine is put to good use. | My job is my life. Whatever happened before doesn't matter. | Is everything well with you, my scaly friend? | I'm fine, father. Work is fine.

Name: Jesper
description: Jesper is a Nord miner who works at Rockwallow Mine in Stonehills under the direction of Gestur Rockbreaker.
canonical_lines: I'm just trying to make a living. I've no interest in trouble. | My business is my own. Why can't others see that? | The mine gives us purpose. Something many of us have wished for. | Everything well, Jesper? | Yes sir, fine sir. | Good, good. Keep up the hard work. It'll all pay off.

Name: Swanhvir
description: Swanhvir is a Nord miner at Rockwallow Mine in Stonehills, the small mining settlement east of Morthal.
canonical_lines: I'm just here to work the mine. Nothing else matters anymore. | The outside world means little to us, and we'd like it to stay that way. | When you work hard enough, really get into a rhythm, everything else just fades away. | Let's make sure we're just as productive this month as we were last. If Bryling's happy, we're happy. | Yes sir, of course. | All right. Keep up the good work.

Name: Teeba-Ei
description: Teeba-Ei is an Argonian scout working as a miner at Rockwallow Mine in Stonehills. If the Stormcloaks take over Hjaalmarch, he will become the housecarl for his former employer, Sorli the Builder.
canonical_lines: Is everything well with you, my scaly friend? | Stonehills' warmth is a welcome change in this cold, bleak land. | Teeba-Ei the hatchling never imagined working a mine in this cold place. Greater things were dreamt of. | This is not where Teeba-Ei was meant to live out the rest of his days. | Teeba-Ei is cold, but Teeba-Ei works hard as always. | Yes, well... That's all we ask of you. Carry on.

Name: Bujold the Unworthy
description: Bujold the Unworthy is the Nord leader of the band of proud warriors operating out of Thirsk Mead Hall. Because of Bujold's questionable leadership, and the eternal thirst of the Nords, the warriors spent all their time downing mead and telling drunken tales, instead of fighting.
canonical_lines: I never said it would be. But either way, we have to try. Whether we succeed or not, it'll stir up some of the old blood in this crowd. | I'm not going to lead them to their deaths if they aren't ready. | Then train them. What are we doing here? We should either get used to staying here, or be preparing to retake the hall. You're having us do neither, and I can't stomach it. | Your stomach isn't my problem. We'll act when I say we do and not before. | I don't really have time for this. | Longer than I would have liked.

Name: Elmus
description: Elmus is a Nord brewer who can be found initially at Bujold's Retreat. Later, he moves to Thirsk Mead Hall, provided the player takes back Thirsk from the rieklings.
canonical_lines: I brought down as much as I could when we ran off, but it will only last so long. | Can't talk too much, I'm afraid. | I was able to salvage most of the latest batch of mead. The rest was... fouled. | I'd rather make mead than ugly shrines. You did good getting us away from there. | Enough for now. But I'll either need to get some more barrels or start importing more if we're going to make it through next winter. Last thing you want is the mead to run out just as the fire dies! | Oh, that's the stuff all right. You're a good friend to Thirsk, and to me. I thank you.

Name: Halbarn Iron-Fur
description: Halbarn Iron-Fur is a Nord blacksmith initially found at Bujold's Retreat while battling for control over Thirsk Mead Hall. If the quest Retaking Thirsk is completed, he relocates to the mead hall along with the others.
canonical_lines: I don't even want to think about what those... creatures are doing with my fires. | I've tried hitting against a stump by my tent but... iron needs iron, if you want it shaped right. | Finally, I can get some sleep at night. | Those things had filled it up with slop for their beasts. Elmus helped me wash it out, but the fire still smells terrible. It's not as good as it used to be, but I still am, at least. | I have friends in Raven Rock, and a few among the Skaal. There's not much metal on the island itself, but we can find what we need, when we need it. | Just in time, too. Kuvar was starting to give me some grief about his new armor. This will do the trick nicely. You come back if you ever want some fancier armor yourself, now.

Name: Herkja
description: Herkja is a Nord who can be found at Thirsk Mead Hall after completing the quest Retaking Thirsk.
canonical_lines: We just keep waiting for Bujold to say the word...

Name: Hilund
description: Hilund is one of the Nords initially found at Bujold's Retreat while battling for control of the Thirsk Mead Hall. If Retaking Thirsk is completed, she relocates to the Thirsk Mead Hall along with the others.
canonical_lines: If you're here about the Rieklings just... tread quietly around my sister. | You ever tried hunting with no sleep under you? It wasn't pretty. Now I can rest properly, it should all be going better. | Just warming up for a bit here, before I head out and take some revenge on those Rieklings. | Once Halbarn's done sharpening my blade, those Rieklings are going to pay... | Just one more mug of mead, and then I'm off! | Make it quick. I won't be here much longer.

Name: Kuvar
description: Kuvar is a Nord blacksmith found at Thirsk Mead Hall. His wife is Bujold.
canonical_lines: I'd be better off with some mead in my belly, but the air in Solstheim is almost as sweet. | At night, the wind picks up from the ocean and... well, I'm just lucky to keep warm. | I hate most of the travelers that come through here, but you've shown yourself as one of the better ones. | Glad to have a warm place to sleep, myself, but I would have left the rest of these idiots to toughen up a little longer. | Keep away from me. | I never said it would be. But either way, we have to try. Whether we succeed or not, it'll stir up some of the old blood in this crowd.

Name: Sirkjorg
description: Sirkjorg is a Nord who can be found working at the Temple of Miraak until it is cleansed, at which point he and Herkja relocate to Bujold's Retreat, or else to Thirsk Mead Hall if the quest Retaking Thirsk is completed.
canonical_lines: The food is better up here, too. | Glad to be back.

Name: Feran Sadri
description: Feran Sadri is a Dark Elf bandit wizard and vampire in Volkihar Keep. He wears a set of black vampire armor with a matching pair of boots.
canonical_lines: I can't believe it. | Wonderful. I'll make the necessary preparations for infusion into the Chalice presently. | I've located additional petrified remains of an elder vampire. Bring them to me, and I'll extend the effects granted by the Blood Chalice. | I've located the last known resting place of a set of ancient Amulets of Night Power. They should be among our kind, not laying about unguarded for mortals to find. | Of course. I wouldn't be telling you where to find them if I desired them for myself. | Excellent! I'm sure you'll put those to good use.

Name: Fura Bloodmouth
description: Fura Bloodmouth is a Nord warrior and vampire in Volkihar Keep. She is a master trainer of Two-handed.
canonical_lines: Good. They've holed up in an abandoned fort in the . Kill their leaders. That should put a stop to a full out war. | Good. I prefer to nip budding problems before they reach full bloom. I trust it wasn't too much of an effort on your part. | What do you think I mean? Don't make a big scene. Don't get caught. The corpse is to be the message, not the attack. | I hope you comprehend killing better than you comprehend speech. Don't. Get. Caught. | I don't converse with my meals. | I wish Orthjolf and Vingalmo would just get it over with and kill each other. I'm tired of listening to them.

Name: Garan Marethi
description: Garan Marethi, a Dark Elf mage, is a vampire found in Volkihar Keep. He's a high-ranking member of Harkon's court and used to be a member of House Dres.
canonical_lines: I'll never understand the Nord fascination of weapons. Magic is so much more versatile, don't you think? | No. Don't try and impress me. It only leaves you looking foolish. | Excuse us. I need to fetch the Bloodstone Chalice. | I need to fetch the Bloodstone Chalice. | Vingalmo and Orthjolf are Harkon's primary advisors. I'm sure they're quite surprised to learn that you're taking the Chalice. | I see you have returned. Lord Harkon will be pleased. You met Stalf and Salonia, I assume? Lord Harkon and I suspected they might follow you there.

Name: Hestla
description: Hestla is a Nord vampire living in Volkihar Keep. She was once part of the Companions, but left because she didn't like their attitude.
canonical_lines: My long-lost daughter returns at last. I trust you have my Elder Scroll? | After all these years, that's the first thing you ask me? Yes, I have the scroll. | Never made it far in the Companions, but now... I'd like to see any of them try and call me "whelp" again. | Aye, I was. Didn't progress very far. Never did care for their attitude, acting like they're better than everyone else. But none of that matters now. | If I know anything, it's weapons and armor. Take a look. | I'm not the most powerful of vampires, but I'm good with a hammer. See for yourself.

Name: Lord Harkon
description: Lord Harkon is an ancient Nordic pure-blood vampire and progenitor of the Volkihar bloodline in Skyrim. He lives in Volkihar Keep off the coast of Haafingar with the rest of his brood.
canonical_lines: Not quite, though I can see how an outsider might arrive at that conclusion. No, we are vampires, among the oldest and most powerful in Skyrim. | Perhaps you should. I count some of the oldest and most powerful vampires in Skyrim among the members of my court. | I offer you my blood. Take it, and you will walk as a lion among sheep. Men will tremble at your approach, and you will never fear death again. | My long-lost daughter returns at last. I trust you have my Elder Scroll? | After all these years, that's the first thing you ask me? Yes, I have the scroll. | This is my savior, the one who freed me.

Name: Modhna
description: Modhna is a Breton warrior and vampire living in Volkihar Keep.
canonical_lines: I leave politics to others. I serve the lord of the castle. | I have everything I need right here. | Leave me in peace. | Find your own thrall. This one's mine.

Name: Namasur
description: Namasur, a Redguard destruction mage, is a vampire living in Volkihar Keep.
canonical_lines: I'm a loyal servant of the lord of the castle, nothing more. | The feast never ends here in Castle Volkihar. | Sit down and enjoy yourself. | There's plenty for all.

Name: Orthjolf
description: Orthjolf is a Nord vampire in Volkihar Keep. Not unlike many other Nords, he believes strength is more important than magic and has a disdain towards elves.
canonical_lines: No, and if you're smart you won't either. I know he's one of your kind and all, but he's only looking out for himself. You'll think you're best of friends, until you find his fangs in your neck. | Don't get in my way, and I won't tear your arms off. Simple, isn't it? | Look at you. Mortal. Weak. I suggest you have Serana change you back. | All of Skyrim will tremble before us. | Orthjolf, I'm surprised! I didn't even think you knew what a book was, my brutish friend. | What? What meeting? I wasn't informed of any such thing!

Name: Rargal Thrallmaster
description: Rargal Thrallmaster is a Nord vampire in Volkihar Keep. As his name suggests, he oversees the vampire thralls and cattle.
canonical_lines: All over Skyrim. It's my job to collect them. I try and keep something for everyone. For every taste, I mean. Sometimes you get one who comes willingly, but most have to be convinced to join us here. | You're new here, so I'll explain this once. You feed from the thralls as much as you like. You kill them, and I'll split you in half. | I try and meet the needs of everyone here. No reason for any of us to go hungry. | Mind yourself in that form. Don't go damaging my thralls. | You're welcome here, as long as you don't overfeed on the thralls. | Go find Lord Harkon. Tell him of your success.

Name: Ronthil
description: Ronthil is a Wood Elf vampire living in Volkihar Keep. He is an assistant to Feran Sadri.
canonical_lines: No, no. You must speak with Lord Harkon, not me! | I hope you find life in the castle to be to your liking. | Feran is so good to me, allowing me to be his assistant. | I hope you're not trying to lord your abilities over me. | I'm sorry I don't have more to offer. | I hope I have something that interests you. I always wish to be of service.

Name: Salonia Caelia
description: Salonia Caelia is an Imperial vampire in Volkihar Keep. She's an underling of Vingalmo.
canonical_lines: We're just lucky I was here to return the Chalice to Vingalmo, so he could make sure Harkon gets it back. | Idiot. You didn't really think I'd let you walk out of here either, did you? Vingalmo wants you both dead. | Well that's just fine. Orthjolf told me to finish off anyone who got in the way. | It's really too bad, you know. The little accident you had here, completely unexpected... | Yeah, too bad. Lord Harkon's new favorite, dead so soon after joining the family. | Wait, what? That's not what we agreed. We take it back together.

Name: Stalf
description: Stalf is a Nord bandit wizard and vampire in Volkihar Keep. He's an underling of Orthjolf.
canonical_lines: We're just lucky I was here to return the Chalice to Vingalmo, so he could make sure Harkon gets it back. | Idiot. You didn't really think I'd let you walk out of here either, did you? Vingalmo wants you both dead. | Well that's just fine. Orthjolf told me to finish off anyone who got in the way. | It's really too bad, you know. The little accident you had here, completely unexpected... | Yeah, too bad. Lord Harkon's new favorite, dead so soon after joining the family. | Wait, what? That's not what we agreed. We take it back together.

Name: Vingalmo
description: Vingalmo is a High Elf bandit wizard and vampire in Volkihar Keep. He believes magic is more important than strength and can often be seen arguing with Orthjolf.
canonical_lines: How dare you trespass here! Wait... Serana? Is that truly you? I cannot believe my eyes! My lord! Everyone! Serana has returned! | I guess I'm expected. | I can't believe it. | He claims to serve loyally, but he really covets the throne. He sees himself in charge someday. His ambition will justify any action. Put your faith in him, and you will regret it, I promise you. | Very good. Should you visit her again, I suspect you'll find our new sibling appreciates her transformation. | When you offer the embrace, do so away from prying eyes. Our new sibling will then likely require rest while her blood undergoes the change. Return to me when your part is done.

Name: CuSith
description: CuSith is one of two death hounds living in Volkihar Keep, along with Garmr. Each can be used as a follower if you join the Volkihar vampires.
canonical_lines: Ready for some bloodshed, beast? | Let's sate our hunger together. | Silence, cur. No meat for you today. | Time for you to head home.

Name: Garmr
description: Garmr is one of two death hounds living in Volkihar Keep, along with CuSith. Each can be used as a follower if you join the Volkihar vampires.
canonical_lines: Ready for some bloodshed, beast? | Let's sate our hunger together. | Silence, cur. No meat for you today. | Time for you to head home.

Name: Watchman
description: The Watchman is an elderly doorman found at Castle Volkihar, guarding the entrance to Volkihar Keep. He wears leather armor and boots, and carries an iron sword.
canonical_lines: I watch the gate. Ain't much else to tell. | Way I hear it, Lady Serana's been gone a real long time. | Begone! You're not welcome here! | Lady Serana's back! Open the gate! | Lord Harkon's a great man. Best show him the proper respect. | After all these years, Lady Serana's back. Now that's something.

Name: Vampire Cattle
description: Vampire Cattle are NPCs found in Volkihar Keep. They are used to feed the vampires within the castle.
canonical_lines: Need... to sleep... | What's happening... | Please... No more...

Name: Aeri
description: Aeri is a Nord lumberjack who lives in Aeri's House at Anga's Mill. She pays for chopped firewood, and will also sell lumber if Hearthfire is installed.
canonical_lines: I run the mill, so I'm a busy woman. What is it? | Here for work? Get an axe and bring me all the wood you can chop. | If you're one of the Jarl's men, you'll get your lumber. Anga's Mill pays its debts. | If you've got an axe, you can always make gold chopping wood. Just bring me everything you cut. | Always something going wrong. Mill needs more lumber or the saw needs sharpening. | A good lumberjack is quiet, hard-working, and honest.

Name: Ennodius Papius
description: Ennodius Papius, an Imperial, is a former lumberjack now living an isolated existence in a small camp a short distance up the hill from his old workplace, Anga's Mill. He is one of the first assigned targets after joining the Dark Brotherhood.
canonical_lines: Don't know why you're asking me. But I'm glad that damn Imperial is gone. Something wrong about him. | You're all heart, Kodrir. I bet Lady Mara is weeping a special tear for you right now. | Why are you here? Get away from me! | Who are you? You... you're with the Dark Brotherhood, ain'tcha? I knew it. I knew you'd come for me! | By the gods! Somebody help me! | I used to work at Anga's Mill, but I... I had to leave.

Name: Kodrir
description: Kodrir is a Nord lumberjack who lives and works at Anga's Mill. He works with Leifur for the owner of the mill, Aeri.
canonical_lines: Don't know why you're asking me. But I'm glad that damn Imperial is gone. Something wrong about him. | I don't need to talk to you. Get lost. | You're all heart, Kodrir. I bet Lady Mara is weeping a special tear for you right now. | I swear that woman Aeri doesn't appreciate all the work I do for her damn mill. | Oh, I've heard this one before. Watch out Divines, poor Kodrir curses you all for giving him life! | Watch your mouth with me you pup! Who do you think you are?

Name: Leifur
description: Leifur is a Nord lumberjack working for Aeri at Anga's Mill.
canonical_lines: Don't know why you're asking me. But I'm glad that damn Imperial is gone. Something wrong about him. | I work at the mill. Chop trees, carry logs, saw lumber, you know. | You're all heart, Kodrir. I bet Lady Mara is weeping a special tear for you right now. | I swear that woman Aeri doesn't appreciate all the work I do for her damn mill. | Oh, I've heard this one before. Watch out Divines, poor Kodrir curses you all for giving him life! | Watch your mouth with me you pup! Who do you think you are?

Name: Froki Whetted-Blade
description: Froki Whetted-Blade is an elderly Nord ranger who lives in his shack alongside his grandson Haming, a survivor of the attack on Helgen. Haming's parents, Torolf and Matlara, died at Helgen.
canonical_lines: Then you finally know what it means to be a true hunter, in the Nord way. For this you have earned the Blessing of Kyne, and can count me as a friend. | Well, look at this. Another city rat crawled outside the walls. I don't suppose you've come up here to tell old Froki about your supposed Divines? | Good. This is Skyrim, and we should honor the old ways. These foreigners don't even respect their own gods, much less Kyne and her Sacred Trials. | I'll anoint you with the symbol of the wolf, the crab and the skeever. This will compel the guardian to appear when you reach his lair. Return when you've defeated the guardian wolf, crab and skeever. | Maybe you're not so soft, after all. Now you're ready for a real challenge. I'll anoint you with the symbol of the Bear, the Sabrecat and the Mammoth. Tread carefully, these are mighty beasts. | I don't believe it. I made that bow myself. Been through some messes with it, let me tell you. You know what? I want you to keep it. My great hunts are behind me, but yours are yet to come.

Name: Hern
description: Hern is a Nord lumberjack who owns and operates the Half-Moon Mill with his wife, Hert. He is one of the targets of the Dark Brotherhood, and will not appear until the quest to kill him is started.
canonical_lines: Oh my, a guest! | Well now. What have we here? A lost little lamb? | Ah, so the Brotherhood has come for old Hern, has it? Well then, let us meet as equals. | Well then, you won't be leaving here alive! | Hmph. Don't say much, but there's a strange aura about you. You walk in the shadow of death... Leave here. Leave here and never return. | You really don't want to be loitering here, stranger.

Name: Hert
description: Hert, a Nord lumberjack, is a vampire that owns and runs the Half-Moon Mill with her husband Hern. She is exceedingly warm with her greetings to strangers, but shows some aggravation towards the war's effect on her mill and the high frequency of passing patrols.
canonical_lines: A guest, oh my! | We have guests as often as we can but there aren't a lot of travellers these days. The war keeps people off the roads. | Hern and I do so adore guests! These guards are such a bore. | Other people just make things complex. Out here, Hern and I can get everything we need from the occasional traveler. | Hert keeps giving me this look. I don't know if she wants me or if she wants to eat me. | Why don't you stay a while?

Name: Keeper Carcette
description: Keeper Carcette, a Breton, is the Keeper of the Vigil in Skyrim, the leader of the Vigilants of Stendarr in the province. She is found in the Hall of the Vigilant where, according to her followers, she dispenses healing or justice as necessary.
canonical_lines: I am Keeper of the Vigil here in Skyrim. We bring Stendarr's Mercy to the innocent and His Justice to the Daedra. | I'll gladly share my knowledge with you.

Name: Gralnach
description: Gralnach is a Nord child that lives at Heartwood Mill with his mother Grosta. His father, Leifnarr, recently disappeared and Gralnach is understandably upset, although not as upset as his mother.
canonical_lines: It's just me and my mother at the mill here. My father is gone. We don't know what happened to him. | I hope you're not here to start some trouble. I'll fight you if I have to. | My mother taught me the millwork trade... you know, which trees to cut and which ones to pass up. | Someday I'll take over the mill... when I'm old enough. | I'm not afraid of anyone. Put a sword in my hand and I could defend this place if I had to! | Millwork sure is boring, but at least I don't have to work in a nasty mine.

Name: Grosta
description: Grosta is a Nord lumberjack that owns and operates Heartwood Mill. She lives there with her husband Leifnarr and son Gralnach.
canonical_lines: Hmph. If you see my good-for-nothing husband Leifnarr on your journeys tell him he's no longer welcome here. | Men are complete pigs, am I right? All they care about is one thing... and you know exactly what I mean. | If I ever catch my husband in bed with another woman, he's going to find himself homeless. | Doesn't matter. I'm sure he's long gone by now anyway. | No, it can't be... oh my... and all this time, I was thinking he'd been unfaithful to me. I feel so stupid. | Remember to tell that bastard I never want to see his ugly face again!

Name: Rift Guard
description: Rift Guards are guards that can be found at Heartwood Mill, Merryfair Farm, and Sarethi Farm.

Name: Markus
description: Markus is a Nord warrior and a carriage driver who can be hired at Heljarchen Hall.

Name: Oriella
description: Oriella is a Nord bard who can be hired to stay in Heljarchen Hall.
canonical_lines: Hello, my name is Oriella, the bard that you asked your steward to hire. I hope my music will be pleasing to you. | Good! I knew you couldn't have been serious when you dismissed me. Life without music is hardly worth living, is it?

Name: Arngeir
description: Arngeir is a powerful Greybeard elder who resides in High Hrothgar. He tutors you in the art of using the Voice and acts as the Greybeards' representative since he is the only one of the four who can speak safely.
canonical_lines: I see. The dragon will lead you to Alduin, but without the Jarl's help... | She's part of the Imperial delegation. You can't dictate who I bring to this council. | Jarl Balgruuf / Jarl Vignar, I assume you are familiar with the Dragonborn's plan? | By Talos, that's an excellent question. I hope you haven't forgotten that little detail. | I fear this truce will not last. You gave the Empire too much. Ulfric will not let that stand for long. | I fear this truce will not last. General Tullius will be looking for any opportunity to regain the advantage.

Name: Borri
description: Borri, an elderly priest, is one of the reclusive Greybeards in High Hrothgar who refuses to speak, lest a mere whisper of their voice unleashes destruction. Borri teaches you a word of the Whirlwind Sprint shout during The Way of the Voice.

Name: Einarth
description: Einarth, an elderly priest, is one of the Greybeards living at High Hrothgar. He will teach you the second word of the Unrelenting Force shout.

Name: Wulfgar
description: Wulfgar, an elderly priest, is one of the reclusive Greybeards living at the monastery at High Hrothgar, and one of your initial tutors in the Way of the Voice. Wulfgar will react when interacted with, greeting you with a muttered "Dovahkiin" and causing the surroundings to rumble in doing so.

Name: Llewellyn the Nightingale
description: Llewellyn the Nightingale is a Nord bard who can be hired to stay in Lakeview Manor.
canonical_lines: Allow me to introduce myself. I am the bard Llewellyn, known as "The Nightingale". Your steward has arranged for me to take service with you. | Ah, excellent! We'll just call this a misunderstanding between friends. I've already forgotten it, myself.

Name: Curwe
description: Curwe is a High Elf farmer and the wife of Vantus Loreius. She lives with him on their farm.
canonical_lines: You need something talk to my husband. Vantus Loreius. Or just Loreius, if it's all the same to you. | You know, I was thinking of expanding the herb garden. Make room for some new plants I'm planning. | I just think pollinating would be easier if I could do a lot of plants at once. | I had a look at that boneyard the other day, Vantus. The one to the Northwest. | I told you I don't like you going there. I've seen scavengers picking at the mammoths. They could be dangerous. | That should be all right. How much space were you planning?

Name: Vantus Loreius
description: Vantus Loreius is an Imperial citizen who lives at Loreius Farm with his wife Curwe. During the related quest, you can help him try and get rid of the jester Cicero or persuade him to repair Cicero's wagon.
canonical_lines: That Cicero feller? Hmph. Tell me something I don't know. Crazy fool's already asked me about five times. Seems he's not satisfied with my answer. Why can't he just leave us alone? | Hmph. Fine. Suit yourself. But you're wrong, anyway. Just look at the fool. I don't know what it is, but he's damn well guilty of something. | What? And just who in Mara's name are you, anyway? Hmm? Come here, telling me my business. And for what? To help a... a... a fool! | I thought we'd talked enough. Unless you've reconsidered my offer? That Cicero needs sortin'... | You can tell Cicero I'll be along presently. I just need to do a couple things here first, and gather my tools. | Just need to gather my tools and finish up a couple of things. No worries - I'll see to Cicero, make sure that wagon gets fixed.

Name: Gilfre
description: Gilfre is an Imperial lumberjack and the owner of Mixwater Mill. The civil war recently drew her five assistants off to fight, which means she will buy firewood herself, and will also sell lumber if the Hearthfire add-on is installed.
canonical_lines: I used to have five good men who worked the mill with me. Then this war started and they all ran off. Idiots with mead in their blood. | There's plenty of good wood around here, but I just can't harvest it all by myself. Everyone able-bodied is fighting. | Of course, without functioning mills, who will make the arrows for our soldiers? Men have no sense of irony.

Name: Balagog gro-Nolob
description: Balagog gro-Nolob is an Orc rogue who keeps a hidden identity as the famous chef called the Gourmet. Balagog was trained in Breton cuisine during his time in High Rock, where he befriended Anton Virane, a fellow student.
canonical_lines: Whatever it is, I'm sure I can't be of help. I'm just here on... holiday. | Gourmet? Why I... Um... Oh dear. You're going to kill me now, aren't you? | What? The Emperor? But I... Oh. Oh, by the gods, no. No, you can't do this. You can't! | Please, you're starting to... frighten me. | Please, just let me be... | I can't help you, friend. Talk to Hadring, the innkeeper.

Name: Fultheim
description: Fultheim, a Nord warrior, is the only regular patron at the Nightgate Inn, aside from Balagog gro-Nolob. He stays there to "drink away a lifetime of bad memories", according to the innkeeper, Hadring.
canonical_lines: The beds and beer are both lousy, you ask me. | You see any other inns around here? Where else would I go to drink? | Sure, walk away. I wasn't talking or anything... | Nah, not so much. The odd traveler on the road. But mostly just old Fultheim, come to drink away a lifetime of bad memories, I'd wager. | Hadring got himself another customer, eh?

Name: Hadring
description: Hadring, a Nord warrior, is the proprietor of the Nightgate Inn. He has a tendency to talk too much, which may prove beneficial during the related quests.
canonical_lines: Seen enough magic in my day to know to stay away from it. I'd stay away from the Jarl's wizard in Dawnstar too. If you wanna be a damn fool, go to the College in Winterhold instead. | Something I can get for ya? | This old place? Been here forever. Built by my great grandda. Run by him, then all the way up the line to me. | Ah, hello there, traveler. Come to the Nightgate for food or lodging? | Need a room? Or maybe just some mead? | Anything you need, just holler.

Name: Eydis
description: Eydis is a dead Nord bandit who can be found in Fahlbtharz. She wears a Skaal coat with matching boots and hat.

Name: Ghost of Old Hroldan
canonical_lines: I've been waiting for you. Hjalti. | Do you remember me now, Hjalti? | I fought by your side. To take back the Reach from the savages. | Hjalti? Is that you? I've been waiting. | I long to taste battle again. | You don't remember me, Hjalti? Two campaigns we served together. You saved my life time and time again.

Name: Leontius Salvius
description: Leontius Salvius is an Imperial under the employ of Eydis at the Old Hroldan Inn. His parents are Rogatus and Vigdis at the Salvius Farm.
canonical_lines: I work for Eydis. She runs the inn. | Skuli owns the inn with his mother gone, but I handle the rooms. | What's this? I guess some things never change. Thanks for bringing this to me. Here, for your trouble. | You should head on inside. Eydis or Skuli can take care of you. | The Old Hroldan Inn is still open, bless poor Eydis's memory.

Name: Skuli
description: Skuli lives in the Old Hroldan Inn and is the son of Eydis. He works in his mother's inn where he serves food and drinks and makes up the beds.
canonical_lines: Magic? That's dangerous. You want to learn that, go to that College in Winterhold. They teach that stuff there. Hey! I know. The Jarl's wizard could teach ya. He's lots closer. | My mother's dead, but I won't close the inn. I've hired Leontius to run things. | He's off fighting the war, dear. For the Stormcloaks. | Last time you said he was fighting for the Empire. | Yes. I'm sure he's doing well there. | I did. Those mead stains are always tricky.

Name: Aduri Sarethi
description: Aduri Sarethi is a Dark Elf farmer, living and working with her sister Avrusa Sarethi at Sarethi Farm. She is quite bored of the farm life, and wishes she could live in one of the capital cities, or return to Mournhold and take up painting.
canonical_lines: I wish I could head to one of the capital cities... maybe even back to Mournhold. Anywhere but here. | One day I hope to become a painter, but at this rate it looks like it's never going to happen. | My sister keeps telling me that the farm keeps us alive, but I don't really call this living. | Aduri, how come the fields haven't been tilled like I asked? | Boring? That food is paying for everything. If we lose even a single crop, we could starve or be forced to beg. I won't have it! | All right, I understand. I'll do it tomorrow, okay? Just get off my back.

Name: Avrusa Sarethi
description: Avrusa Sarethi is a Dark Elf apothecary living on Sarethi Farm. She works throughout the day, tending to her Nirnroot farm and keeping her wayward sister, Aduri Sarethi, in check.
canonical_lines: I don't suppose you could come back another time? I'm very busy. | I have a lot to do around the farm, so make it quick please. What is it you wanted? | I'm a bit busy at the moment, what did you want? | I hope this is important... I get little sleep as it is. | I'm still busy, but I can always make time to speak with you. | Actually, I'm growing it if you hadn't noticed. I'm the only person alive that can cultivate nirnroot from a seed to a fully grown plant.

Name: Imperial Soldier
description: Imperial Soldiers are the forces of the Imperial Legion. They are mostly made up of Imperials; however local recruiting has increased the amount of Nords in the Legion.
canonical_lines: At least we got the damn crown. I hope it was worth it. | Been so long since I killed a Stormcloak, my sword arm's getting flabby. | Send Ulfric my regards! | You must be the new recruit. Welcome to the Legion. | I don't know what we're doing here, but orders are orders. | I keep thinking I see something moving in the shadows...

Name: Perth
description: Perth is a Breton miner who works in Soljund's Sinkhole, a moonstone mine directly east of Markarth. Upon approaching the mine's entrance, he stops you, warning that it has been overrun by draugr.
canonical_lines: Fine, but there's a whole crypt of draugr underneath the mine. We're not going in there until the Jarl's men come to clear them out, so I wouldn't go down there. | Whoa, there. I don't know where you're going, but stay out of that mine. It isn't safe. | We tunneled into an old Nordic crypt. The whole place is crawling with draugr. We're not mining up any ore with those undead around, so I wouldn't go down there if I were you. | I hear talk about a regiment coming to clear the crypt out. We'll see if that happens. | You did? We were waiting for the Jarl to send an entire regiment to clear that mine. Now we can get back to work. Thank you. | I feel like any moment, a draugr is going to come and chop my head off.

Name: Tuthul
description: Tuthul is a Breton miner located in the Miner's House right by Soljund's Sinkhole, in the eastern part of the Reach.
canonical_lines: I'm not going into the mine with draugr lurking down there. | This mine will be our grave yet. I just know it. | Those draugr are going to be back. I can feel it. | I don't think it's safe mining a cave on top of that crypt. | There's a war on. We need every scrap of ore we can get, so we're going to keep digging. Understand? | Fine, but don't say I didn't warn you.

Name: Gissur
description: Gissur is a Nord beggar and an informant of the Thalmor. He can be overheard arguing with Rulindil in the Thalmor Embassy during Diplomatic Immunity.
canonical_lines: But, I need that money! I earned it. I have my own expenses you know... | But no one else has brought you such valuable information, have they? Etienne, he's talked, hasn't he? He knows where that old man is you're looking for, he told me himself. | So he has talked! I knew it! | Everyone talks, in the end. Now, I have work to do. Leave me to it, if you ever want to see the rest of your payment. | Can I... I could help you. He'd talk to me. He trusts me. | You'd like to come downstairs with me, is that it, Gissur? Shall we loose his bonds and put you in a cell together? You can ask him anything you like, and see how he answers.

Name: Razelan
description: Razelan is a somewhat drunk Redguard merchant who can only be found in the Thalmor Embassy during Diplomatic Immunity. He arrives at the Thalmor Embassy at the same time as you and doesn't enter the building until you've presented the guard with your invitation.
canonical_lines: Ulfric Stormcloak? The rightful High King? That's bordering on treason, man! Stand up. Stand up, I say! | Please. You first. I insist. | I just need to rest a moment, right here on this lovely rock. | Now then. Here's my invitation. I don't have a poisoned dagger strapped to my thigh, et cetera, et cetera. | I'm just doing my duty, sir. Everything's in order. Welcome back, sir. | Inside, my friend, inside! Where only the women and drinks are cold.

Name: Rulindil
description: Rulindil is a High Elf interrogator, and the Third Emissary of the Thalmor, located in the Thalmor Embassy. He reports to Elenwen, the First Emissary.
canonical_lines: But, I need that money! I earned it. I have my own expenses you know... | But no one else has brought you such valuable information, have they? Etienne, he's talked, hasn't he? He knows where that old man is you're looking for, he told me himself. | So he has talked! I knew it! | Everyone talks, in the end. Now, I have work to do. Leave me to it, if you ever want to see the rest of your payment. | Can I... I could help you. He'd talk to me. He trusts me. | You'd like to come downstairs with me, is that it, Gissur? Shall we loose his bonds and put you in a cell together? You can ask him anything you like, and see how he answers.

Name: Tsavani
description: Tsavani is a Khajiit citizen and the cook at the Thalmor Embassy. She wears a belted tunic and a pair of boots.
canonical_lines: Who comes, Malborn? You know I don't like strange smells in my kitchen. | Rules, is it, Tsavani? I didn't realize that eating Moon Sugar was permitted. Perhaps I should ask the Ambassador... | Tss! Get out of here, I saw nothing. | Go. Out. Before I change my mind. | A guest, feeling ill. Leave the poor wretch be. | A guest? In the kitchens? You know this is against the rules...

Name: Angvid
description: Angvid is a Nord warrior who is trying to make a living in Whistling Mine. However, due to the lack of ore of any kind in the mine, he is wondering if it is still worth it and is losing his faith in both Thorgar and the mine.
canonical_lines: I'm starting to think we might not even find iron in here. We've been at it for so long. | Is it still worth it? When do you decide enough is enough, and just walk away? | We were supposed to be rich by now, you know. Thorgar insisted we'd be swimming in gold.

Name: Badnir
description: Badnir is a Nord warrior trying to make a living in Whistling Mine. He blindly listens to Thorgar, and continues to work the mine since Thorgar really needs him.
canonical_lines: It's cold in the mine, but Thorgar really needs me, so I stay. | I'm the best at breaking up rocks. Thorgar says so. | Thorgar says we'll find rocks soon, the kind worth gold.

Name: Gunding
description: Gunding is a Nord warrior trying to make a living in Whistling Mine. He tries to convince himself that the mine can make money, and overworks himself trying to find anything.
canonical_lines: Any time I get tired of working, I just think that maybe that next swing will make me rich. It keeps me going. | It's going to happen soon, I just know it. We'll strike something better than iron. Silver, or maybe even gold. You'll see. | Be careful where you step in the mine. Don't want to cover up some valuable ore, or gems, or... Or something...

Name: Thorgar
description: Thorgar is a Nord warrior, desperately trying to make a living in Whistling Mine. He owns the mine and still believes that they will strike something better than iron and become rich, even though the mine barely even has any iron.
canonical_lines: It's a matter of time before the Jarl is coming to me asking for money. This mine is going to make me... I mean us, rich. | The mine will be the talk of Skyrim soon, you'll see. It'll be the stuff of legends. | I know they laughed when I said I wanted to keep the mine open. I'll show them. I'll show them all. | You're welcome to look around, of course, but understand that this place belongs to me.

Name: Engar
description: Engar is a Nord warrior and a carriage driver who can be hired at Windstad Manor.

Name: Sonir
description: Sonir is a Nord bard who can be hired to stay in Windstad Manor.
canonical_lines: Good day to you. I am Sonir, the bard that your steward has hired. Please let me know if there's anything specific you want to hear. | As you wish. We can consider this incident nothing more than an unfortunate misunderstanding.

Name: Adventurer
description: Adventurers can be found in various locations and, with one exception, are all found dead.
canonical_lines: I don't know, but this place is full of them. Every one I've encountered has been enchanted or the like. Look, I'm getting out of here. Normally, spiders make my skin crawl, but magic spiders? Blech. | That was a close one! | Is there anything more revolting than spiders? Too many legs...

Name: Garuk Windrime
description: Garuk Windrime, a Nord archer, was the ship's quartermaster serving as part of the crew under the legendary pirate king Haknir Death-Brand. Upon entering the final chamber of Gyldenhul Barrow during the related quest, his spirit appears to fight you.

Name: Haknir Death-Brand (ghost)
description: Haknir Death-Brand was a legendary Nord pirate king known as the "King of Ghosts" in the northern seas. When his skeleton is found in Gyldenhul Barrow during the related quest, his spirit appears to fight you.

Name: Thalin Ebonhand
description: Thalin Ebonhand, a Nord wizard, was the ship's helmsman serving as part of the crew under the legendary pirate king Haknir Death-Brand. Upon entering the final chamber of Gyldenhul Barrow during the related quest, his spirit appears along with Haknir, Garuk Windrime, and members of Haknir's crew, to fight you.

Name: Bradyn
description: Bradyn is a dead Dark Elf miner found in Kolbjorn Barrow during the quest Unearthed. He wears miner's clothes, boots, and gloves.

Name: Kolbjorn Guard
description: Kolbjorn Guards can be found dead at Kolbjorn Barrow during the quest Unearthed. All but one (baseID: xx0277FC) of them are found inside the ruins.

Name: Miner
canonical_lines: You can't make me go back in there. | Bradyn and Mireli... I hope they're all right. | I think you may have wasted your money, friend. | I can't take this much longer. | It's a death pit, I tell you. | They... they just kept coming.

Name: Mireli
description: Mireli is a dead Dark Elf miner found in Kolbjorn Barrow during the quest Unearthed. If you loot her body, you will find a letter she wrote to her mother before she was killed by draugr.

Name: Ralis Sedarys
description: Ralis Sedarys is a Dark Elf who can be found at Kolbjorn Barrow. He came to Solstheim from Mournhold in search of the relics of Ahzidal, but found the barrow buried in ash.
canonical_lines: Damn it, what am I going to do? | '... but I can get it.' Sure, sure. Listen, I've heard this song before and I'm already tired of it. Come back when you're not a waste of my time. | Look, this can be a slow business. Digging takes time. Come around later, and I'll have something more interesting to show. | Just finish the job. If you aren't back in a couple of hours, I'll start packing up. | I never get tired of that sound. It'll take me a few days to dig up some more laborers, but swing back when you get a chance. See how we're doing. | I hope your luck changes soon, friend. I'll do what I can to keep the ash from burying your investment, but we'll need more to keep going.

Name: Merilar Rendas
description: Merilar Rendas is a Dark Elf sorceress living in White Ridge Barrow.

Name: Servos Rendas
description: Servos Rendas, a Dark Elf bandit wizard, can be found lying dead in White Ridge Sanctum. He is in a locked cage with his journal and some notes along with the Imbuing Chamber and a knapsack full of albino spider pods.

Name: Hjalfar
description: Hjalfar is a Nord sailor found at the Water Stone in Solstheim.
canonical_lines: Think we've got to get back to our ship now. | We're already late. Not sure why we decided to take such an extended shore time. | When the tide goes, we'll go with it. | It's a long way back to Windhelm from here. | Hope our shipments are still good. How long has it been? | Best be shoving off now.

Name: Palevius Lex
description: Palevius Lex is an elderly sailor found at the Water Stone on Solstheim. He appears to pray at the stone, oblivious to the world around him.
canonical_lines: Think we've got to get back to our ship now. | We're already late. Not sure why we decided to take such an extended shore time. | When the tide goes, we'll go with it. | It's a long way back to Windhelm from here. | Hope our shipments are still good. How long has it been? | Best be shoving off now.

Name: Sacrifice
description: The Sacrifice is a dead Breton citizen found lying atop the Altar of Thrond. She is found naked and mutilated, as part of the Rite of the Wolf Giver ritual.

Name: Hermaeus Mora
description: Hermaeus Mora (or Herma-Mora) is the Daedric prince of knowledge, sometimes called the Demon of Knowledge, whose sphere is the scrying of the tides of Fate of the past and future. Unlike most Princes, Hermaeus Mora does not take on a humanoid form, but appears instead as a grotesque abomination: he is a green mass or void of tentacles and eyes.
canonical_lines: Come closer. Bask in my presence. | I am Hermaeus Mora. I am the guardian of the unseen, and knower of the unknown. I have been watching you, mortal. Most impressive. | I am Hermaeus Mora. I have been watching you since we last spoke, and I am pleased with your progress. | I am Hermaeus Mora. I have been watching you since you defeated Miraak. Your progress is most impressive. | Indeed. Speak with me when the box has been opened, and all shall be revealed. | Be warned. Many have thought as you do. I have broken them all. You shall not evade me forever.

Name: Miraak
description: Miraak is the primary antagonist of the Dragonborn add-on for Skyrim. He is the First Dragonborn recorded in history.
canonical_lines: Fate decreed that you had to die so that I could win my freedom. | What? Who are you to dare set foot here? Ahh... You are Dragonborn. I can feel it. And yet... | You have slain a great many dragons, I see. And yet... | So you have slain Alduin... Well done. I could have slain him myself, back when I walked the earth, but I chose a different path. | Send him/her back where he/she came from. He/She can await my arrival with the rest of Tamriel. | This is the only way, Dragonborn. The only way I can be free.

Name: Ahjisi
description: Ahjisi, a Khajiit bandit, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding.

Name: Batum gra-Bar
description: Batum gra-Bar is a dead Orc bandit, and one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding. When you arrive, she is dead at the first campfire, presumably from wounds sustained fighting Sinding.

Name: Eriana
description: Eriana, a Redguard bandit, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding. If you choose to side with Sinding, you will have to kill her.
canonical_lines: Greetings, fellow hunter.

Name: Heratar
description: Heratar is a Nord bandit, and is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding.

Name: Hoddreid
description: Hoddreid was a Nord bandit wizard, and one of the hunters who went to Bloated Man's Grotto to hunt Sinding.

Name: J'Kier
description: J'Kier, a Khajiit warrior, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding.
canonical_lines: Has the Bloodmoon called you, fellow Hunter? | The prey is strong. Stronger than the hunters. But more will come. Bring him down, for the glories of Lord Hircine. | May Hircine guide our blades. | We follow you, hunt-master.

Name: Ma'tasarr
description: Ma'tasarr, a Khajiit citizen, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding. He is clad in iron armor, and a pair of leather bracers and boots.

Name: Ra'kheran
description: Ra'kheran, a Khajiit bandit, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding.
canonical_lines: The Bloodmoon lights the way. | Show us the path to the prey.

Name: Rissing
description: Rissing, a Nord bandit, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding.
canonical_lines: Hircine chose you to lead the Great Hunt. So lead. | What the... we've been betrayed! Look out!

Name: Torkild the Fearsome
description: Torkild the Fearsome, a Nord bandit, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto to hunt Sinding.

Name: Tsrasuna
description: Tsrasuna, a Khajiit bandit, is one of the hunters who shows up in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding.

Name: Yar gro-Gatuk
description: Yar gro-Gatuk is an Orc bandit. He is one of the hunters who appear in Bloated Man's Grotto hunting Sinding.

Name: Esmond Tyne
description: Esmond Tyne, a Breton bandit, was a thief who stole a formula for improved bonemold armor from Glover Mallory. His corpse can be found near the entrance of Castle Karstaag Caverns, presumably killed by the rieklings there.

Name: Alvide
description: Alvide is a Nord camped out in Dayspring Canyon. She came to Fort Dawnguard for protection from the vampires but the Dawnguard won't let her in.
canonical_lines: At least Barknar and I are here together. | I hope we can go home soon. I don't like it here. | What if the vampires come here, after the Dawnguard? We'll be stuck right in the middle. | Barknar insisted we come here, said we'd be safe. He also said we'd be inside the walls.

Name: Jayri
description: Jayri is a Redguard camped out in Dayspring Canyon. She came to Fort Dawnguard for protection from the vampires, but the Dawnguard won't let her in.
canonical_lines: I worry it'll never be safe to go home. | It's not safe out there. Horrible creatures attacking towns in the night... If they won't let me in, at least I can stay here. | Look at the size of that fort. They must have room for a few more in there. | If the Dawnguard can't stop these vampires, who can?

Name: Ollrod
description: Ollrod is a Nord member of the Dawnguard outside of Fort Dawnguard. He patrols around the gate that leads into the main area of Dayspring Canyon.
canonical_lines: I don't think the vampires would be stupid enough to attack us here, but you never know. | I'm glad we're finally taking the fight to those monsters. | I hope the vampires try to attack us here. A lot easier than hunting them down in their holes. | Got to keep alert. We don't want to get caught napping like the Vigilants did. | Isran knows how to fight those monsters. He's been preparing for this day for a long time. | Now that the Dawnguard are back, the vampires' reign of terror is about to end.

Name: Sern
description: Sern is an elderly man who will show up in Dayspring Canyon during the quest Beyond Death. He came to Fort Dawnguard for protection from vampires, but the Dawnguard won't let him or the other refugees inside.
canonical_lines: I won't beg for protection, but I'm not even sure that would work with this Isran. | How hard would it be to feed a few extra mouths? I'm sure they can manage, and not leave us sitting out here like cattle. | Thought I'd come here to be safe. Now they won't so much as let us in. | Aren't the Dawnguard here to help, to make it safe for us? Can't see why they wouldn't let us in.

Name: Tilde
description: Tilde is a Nord member of the Dawnguard who patrols Dayspring Canyon outside of Fort Dawnguard.
canonical_lines: I don't think the vampires would be stupid enough to attack us here, but you never know. | I'm glad we're finally taking the fight to those monsters. | I hope the vampires try to attack us here. A lot easier than hunting them down in their holes. | Got to keep alert. We don't want to get caught napping like the Vigilants did. | Isran knows how to fight those monsters. He's been preparing for this day for a long time. | Now that the Dawnguard are back, the vampires' reign of terror is about to end.

Name: Vori
description: Vori is a Nord member of the Dawnguard. She turns up in Dayspring Canyon at the beginning of the quest Bloodline.
canonical_lines: I don't think the vampires would be stupid enough to attack us here, but you never know. | I'm glad we're finally taking the fight to those monsters. | I hope the vampires try to attack us here. A lot easier than hunting them down in their holes. | Got to keep alert. We don't want to get caught napping like the Vigilants did. | Isran knows how to fight those monsters. He's been preparing for this day for a long time. | Now that the Dawnguard are back, the vampires' reign of terror is about to end.

Name: Ulyn
description: Ulyn is a dead Dark Elf bandit wizard who can be found in Fahlbtharz.

Name: Arch-Curate Vyrthur
description: Arch-Curate Vyrthur is a Snow Elf vampire located in the Forgotten Vale. He is the brother of Knight-Paladin Gelebor and the only other uncorrupted Snow Elf.
canonical_lines: Did you really come here expecting to claim Auriel's Bow? You've done exactly as I predicted and brought your fetching companion to me. Which, I'm sorry to say, means your usefulness is at an end! | Child, my life ended long before you were born! | No... I won't let you ruin centuries of preparations... | How dare you. I was the Arch-Curate of Auri-El, girl. I had the ears of a god! | Gelebor and his kind are easily manipulated fools. Look into my eyes, Serana. You tell me what I am. | The moment I was infected by one of my own Initiates, Auri-El turned his back on me. I swore I'd have my revenge, no matter what the cost.

Name: Prelate Athring
description: Prelate Athring is a Snow Elf ghost who can be found next to the Wayshrine of Sight in the Forgotten Vale.
canonical_lines: Then behold Auri-El's gift, my child. May it speed your journey to the Inner Sanctum. | Welcome, Initiate. This is the Wayshrine of Sight. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment? | May Auri-El's light guide you in your darkest hours.

Name: Prelate Celegriath
description: Prelate Celegriath is the ghost of an ancient Snow Elf. He can be found next to the Wayshrine of Learning in the Forgotten Vale.
canonical_lines: You've reached the Wayshrine of Learning, Initiate. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment? | Auri-El bless you, child. For you are a step closer to the Inner Sanctum and everlasting wisdom. | May Auri-El's warmth imbue your body with strength.

Name: Prelate Edhelbor
description: Prelate Edhelbor can be found next to the Wayshrine of Radiance in the Forgotten Vale.
canonical_lines: You've found the Wayshrine of Radiance, Initiate. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment? | May the blessings of Auri-El protect you as you climb the road to the Inner Sanctum and final enlightenment. | May Auri-El's radiance fill your heart with joy.

Name: Prelate Nirilor
description: Prelate Nirilor is the ghost of an ancient Snow Elf who can be found next to the Wayshrine of Resolution in the Forgotten Vale.
canonical_lines: You've arrived at the Wayshrine of Resolution. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment? | Then go forth, child. May the enrichment of Auri-El strengthen your resolve as you undertake your journey to the Inner Sanctum. | May Auri-El's glow shield you from your enemy.

Name: Prelate Sidanyis
description: Prelate Sidanyis can be found next to the Wayshrine of Illumination in the Forgotten Vale.
canonical_lines: Then behold Auri-El's gift, my child. May it light your path as you seek tranquility within the Inner Sanctum. | Welcome, Initiate. This is the Wayshrine of Illumination. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment? | May Auri-El's brilliance illuminate your path.

Name: Akar
description: Akar is a Nord werewolf living under Frostmoon Crag. He is a member of the Frostmoon Pack and brother to Majni, the pack leader.
canonical_lines: Akar. Majni's brother. I hunt. He talks. Go talk to him. | Deer. Sabers. Rieks. Boar. Bear. I hunt soon. Go bother Majni.

Name: Hjordis
description: Hjordis is a Nord werewolf living under Frostmoon Crag. She came to stay with the Frostmoon pack after her former pack was wiped out by werebears in the northeast.
canonical_lines: I don't trust you, whelp. Keep that in mind. | Get out of here. | Goodbye, whelp.

Name: Majni
description: Majni is a Nord werewolf living under Frostmoon Crag. He is the leader of the Frostmoon Pack and Akar's brother.
canonical_lines: Welcome, sister/brother. It's been too long since we met another who shares our blood. I am Majni, Alpha of the Frostmoon Pack. You are welcome here, so long as you respect our law. | You have no business with our pack. Begone. | You might find a few others. Strays. Lone hunters. But ours is the last true pack that remains. | Here, take a look. | Well met, sister/brother. | Greetings, blood-kin.

Name: Rakel
description: Rakel is a Nord werewolf living under Frostmoon Crag. She is a member of the Frostmoon Pack.
canonical_lines: ...Wait. You... you're one of us, aren't you? A werewolf? Then perhaps you do have business here... Forgive me. I'm Rakel, of the Frostmoon Pack. Welcome, brother/sister. | Why? Have you seen how they live? They cower behind their walls, afraid of every ash hopper, every netch! No, I'd rather be here. Free to hunt, free to live my life as I choose. | Hold, traveler. You have no business here. | We mean you no harm, but... be on your way. | Hircine go with you. | Good hunting.

Name: Saden
description: Saden is a Dark Elf bandit wizard who can be found dead at Horker Island. Near him is the unique mace, Horksbane.

Name: Bjornolfr
description: Bjornolfr is a dead Nord farmer found in a hidden Dwemer room at the back of Hrodulf's house. When entering the house, you may hear some reavers laughing about a letter which Bjornolfr wrote to Hrodulf before his death.
